blocks.html_en:
I want to continue to use the enterprise products paid for with billions of dollars of investment, because they still have tremendous value.

Similarly, I want to continue to use the platforms that have all the users, content, and network effects.

BUT - I'd like my own personal agent to sit between everything and strip out the nonsense before it hits my eyeballs.

Ad block used to be good enough, but we can clearly take this much further.

Ads are not the only toxic thing. The algorithm is. Attention seeking from any entity is. The whole internet is adversarial, not just one screen of one product.

An agent could sit between us and the web (or us and our apps) and strip out all the ads, strip out all the clickbait, filter out all the negativity, algo-spam, and low-value content. Calls to upgrade, cookie banners, MrBeast, whatever.

I want an agent that knows me and acts on my behalf as I browse the internet. It should see everything in my browser (or even everything on my screen) and whittles down the bullshit before it ever reaches me.

This kind of tool could focus the whole of the internet into perfect crystal clarity - into an internet whose only objective is to serve me. Certainly not an internet that can make me mad or that tries to convince me to buy things.

Something flexible like this goes beyond just search. It fixes everything on every corner of the internet.

If we treat this as a technology/browser/OS problem, then it can fix everything wholesale. I can continue to use the social media platforms and technology everyone else is using, but I won't be subjected to the bullshit. I don't have to switch to Kagi or BlueSky or whatever small alternative tries to go up against titans, loses the network effects, and winds up as smaller, less-functional offering.

A butler-like agent is a new pane of glass. An internet condom. A judge, jury, and executioner whose only goal is to make sure one stays focused and happy.

This is a product I would pay a thousand dollars a year for.

sentences.en: I want to continue to use the enterprise products paid for with billions of dollars of investment, because they still have tremendous value.Similarly, I want to continue to use the platforms that have all the users, content, and network effects.BUT - I'd like my own personal agent to sit between everything and strip out the nonsense before it hits my eyeballs.Ad block used to be good enough, but we can clearly take this much further.Ads are not the only toxic thing.
sentences.zh: 我想继续使用花费数十亿美元投资购买的企业产品，因为它们仍然具有巨大的价值。同样，我想继续使用拥有所有用户、内容和网络效应的平台。但是 - 我希望我自己的个人代理坐在一切之间，在它击中我的眼球之前剔除废话。广告拦截曾经足够好，但我们显然可以更进一步。广告并不是唯一有毒的东西。

sentences.en: The algorithm is.
sentences.zh: 算法是。

sentences.en: Attention seeking from any entity is.
sentences.zh: 寻求任何实体的关注都是如此。

sentences.en: The whole internet is adversarial, not just one screen of one product.An agent could sit between us and the web (or us and our apps) and strip out all the ads, strip out all the clickbait, filter out all the negativity, algo-spam, and low-value content.
sentences.zh: 整个互联网都是对抗性的，而不仅仅是一个产品的一个屏幕。代理可以坐在我们和网络（或我们和我们的应用程序）之间，删除所有广告，删除所有点击诱饵，过滤掉所有负面内容、算法垃圾邮件和低价值内容。

sentences.en: Calls to upgrade, cookie banners, MrBeast, whatever.I want an agent that knows me and acts on my behalf as I browse the internet.
sentences.zh: 升级电话、cookie 横幅、MrBeast 等等。我想要一个了解我并在我浏览互联网时代表我行事的代理。

sentences.en: It should see everything in my browser (or even everything on my screen) and whittles down the bullshit before it ever reaches me.This kind of tool could focus the whole of the internet into perfect crystal clarity - into an internet whose only objective is to serve me.
sentences.zh: 它应该能看到我浏览器中的所有内容（甚至屏幕上的所有内容），并在这些废话到达我之前将其消除。这种工具可以将整个互联网集中到完美的水晶般清晰的状态 - 成为一个唯一目标是为我服务的互联网。

sentences.en: Certainly not an internet that can make me mad or that tries to convince me to buy things.Something flexible like this goes beyond just search.
sentences.zh: 当然不是一个能让我生气或试图说服我买东西的互联网。像这样灵活的东西不仅仅是搜索。

sentences.en: It fixes everything on every corner of the internet.If we treat this as a technology/browser/OS problem, then it can fix everything wholesale.
sentences.zh: 它可以修复互联网每个角落的所有问题。如果我们将其视为技术/浏览器/操作系统问题，那么它可以批量修复所有问题。

sentences.en: I can continue to use the social media platforms and technology everyone else is using, but I won't be subjected to the bullshit.
sentences.zh: 我可以继续使用其他人正在使用的社交媒体平台和技术，但我不会受到这些废话的影响。

sentences.en: I don't have to switch to Kagi or BlueSky or whatever small alternative tries to go up against titans, loses the network effects, and winds up as smaller, less-functional offering.A butler-like agent is a new pane of glass.
sentences.zh: 我不必改用 Kagi 或 BlueSky 或任何试图对抗巨头的小型替代品，失去网络效应，最终成为更小、功能更少的产品。管家般的代理是一块新的玻璃。

sentences.en: An internet condom.
sentences.zh: 互联网安全套。

sentences.en: A judge, jury, and executioner whose only goal is to make sure one stays focused and happy.This is a product I would pay a thousand dollars a year for.
sentences.zh: 法官、陪审团和刽子手，他们唯一的目标就是确保一个人保持专注和快乐。这是一个我每年愿意花一千美元购买的产品。

blocks.html_en:
There are so many things I can write about this, but I’ll keep it short:

Technological solutions to societal problems is one of the shortest paths to hell.

So, no, thanks.

blocks.html_en:
> Technological solutions to societal problems is one of the shortest paths to hell.

This is an unjustified blanket opinion. Sometimes, societal problems can be solved by technological progress. I think you simply lack imagination if you cannot think of any societal problem that would be solved by better technology.

sentences.en: > Technological solutions to societal problems is one of the shortest paths to hell.This is an unjustified blanket opinion.
sentences.zh: > 解决社会问题的技术解决方案是通往地狱的最短路径之一。这是一种不合理的笼统观点。

sentences.en: Sometimes, societal problems can be solved by technological progress.
sentences.zh: 有时，社会问题可以通过技术进步来解决。

sentences.en: I think you simply lack imagination if you cannot think of any societal problem that would be solved by better technology.
sentences.zh: 我认为如果你想不出任何可以通过更好的技术解决的社会问题，你就是缺乏想象力。

blocks.html_en: For example: the current societal problem of not doing enough against climate change would disappear if we developed cheap fusion power and/or very cheap grid-scale batteries that would remove the intermittency issue from renewables. Such power sources would quickly replace fossil fuels simply due to better economics, no need to change society to care about climate change anymore.

sentences.en: For example: the current societal problem of not doing enough against climate change would disappear if we developed cheap fusion power and/or very cheap grid-scale batteries that would remove the intermittency issue from renewables.
sentences.zh: 例如：如果我们开发出廉价的聚变能源和/或非常便宜的电网规模电池来消除可再生能源的间歇性问题，那么当前在应对气候变化方面做得不够的社会问题就会消失。

sentences.en: Such power sources would quickly replace fossil fuels simply due to better economics, no need to change society to care about climate change anymore.
sentences.zh: 由于经济性更好，这种能源将很快取代化石燃料，不再需要改变社会来关心气候变化。

blocks.html_en: Sure, so then what does society do with all of the cheap energy? Human nature is still there. Among other things, leads directly to discarding even more plastic junk wherever it’s convenient and no one complains too much.

blocks.html_en:
Recently Animats replied to one of my comments and brought "Megawatt Racks" to my attention.

I work at a datacenter. We provide compute resources to researchers, and while our datacenter is not small or low energy, one of our systems have Green500 certification and we try to do things as efficient as possible.

When I look at our racks, some of them are DLC BTW, and imagine each of them can pack a MW system now, I shudder. Not because of us, but what AI datacenters gonna do with that energy density and how much heat will these systems put out.

sentences.en: Recently Animats replied to one of my comments and brought "Megawatt Racks" to my attention.I work at a datacenter.
sentences.zh: 最近 Animats 回复了我的一条评论，并让我注意到“兆瓦机架”。我在数据中心工作。

sentences.en: We provide compute resources to researchers, and while our datacenter is not small or low energy, one of our systems have Green500 certification and we try to do things as efficient as possible.When I look at our racks, some of them are DLC BTW, and imagine each of them can pack a MW system now, I shudder.
sentences.zh: 我们为研究人员提供计算资源，虽然我们的数据中心规模不小，能耗也不低，但我们的一个系统获得了 Green500 认证，我们尝试尽可能高效地开展工作。当我看到我们的机架时，其中一些是 DLC 顺便说一句，想象一下它们现在每个都可以容纳一个 MW 系统，我不寒而栗。

sentences.en: Not because of us, but what AI datacenters gonna do with that energy density and how much heat will these systems put out.
sentences.zh: 不是因为我们，而是人工智能数据中心将如何利用这种能量密度以及这些系统将释放多少热量。

blocks.html_en:
"We need most of our population working full time to keep ourselves fed because running a farm is really hard" is a technical problem, not a societal one. You can solve it with fertilizer and the cotton gin or whatever.

"There's a bunch of us now and barter isn't cutting it anymore" is a technical problem, not a societal one. You can solve it with coinage and banks or whatever.

"The internet used to be fun and useful but now it sucks" is a societal problem, not a technical one. You can't solve it with a federated twitter clone or yet another secure messaging protocol or whatever.

sentences.en: "We need most of our population working full time to keep ourselves fed because running a farm is really hard" is a technical problem, not a societal one.
sentences.zh: “我们需要大多数人全职工作来养活自己，因为经营农场真的很困难”是一个技术问题，而不是一个社会问题。

sentences.en: You can solve it with fertilizer and the cotton gin or whatever."There's a bunch of us now and barter isn't cutting it anymore" is a technical problem, not a societal one.
sentences.zh: 你可以用化肥和轧棉机或其他什么方法来解决这个问题。“现在我们有一群人，易货贸易不再能解决这个问题”是一个技术问题，而不是一个社会问题。

sentences.en: You can solve it with coinage and banks or whatever."The internet used to be fun and useful but now it sucks" is a societal problem, not a technical one.
sentences.zh: 你可以用铸币和银行之类的东西来解决它。“互联网曾经很有趣也很有用，但现在很糟糕”是一个社会问题，而不是一个技术问题。

sentences.en: You can't solve it with a federated twitter clone or yet another secure messaging protocol or whatever.
sentences.zh: 您无法通过联合 Twitter 克隆或其他安全消息传递协议或其他协议来解决此问题。

blocks.html_en:
Generally, if you're targeting behavior of the people or the masses, you're targeting a societal problem.

People bullying other people is not a technical problem. People driving dangerously is not a technical problem. People shoplifting is not a technical problem. All of them have different reasons deep down, and preventing the symptom doesn't solve the problem.

When you effectively prevent bullying, the bully might damage something else or may do self-harm to soothe their demons. Same for people driving dangerously or have to shoplift to survive.

When you cure the root problem, which is generally harder BTW, the problem you're technically trying to prevent disappears, too.

Hope this helps.

sentences.en: Generally, if you're targeting behavior of the people or the masses, you're targeting a societal problem.People bullying other people is not a technical problem.
sentences.zh: 一般来说，如果你针对的是人民或群众的行为，那么你针对的是一个社会问题。人们欺负别人不是一个技术问题。

sentences.en: People driving dangerously is not a technical problem.
sentences.zh: 人们危险驾驶不是技术问题。

sentences.en: People shoplifting is not a technical problem.
sentences.zh: 人们入店行窃不是技术问题。

sentences.en: All of them have different reasons deep down, and preventing the symptom doesn't solve the problem.When you effectively prevent bullying, the bully might damage something else or may do self-harm to soothe their demons.
sentences.zh: 他们的内心深处都有不同的原因，预防症状并不能解决问题。当你有效地防止欺凌行为时，欺凌者可能会损坏其他东西，或者可能会通过自残来安抚他们的恶魔。

sentences.en: Same for people driving dangerously or have to shoplift to survive.When you cure the root problem, which is generally harder BTW, the problem you're technically trying to prevent disappears, too.Hope this helps.
sentences.zh: 对于危险驾驶或不得不入店行窃才能生存的人来说也是如此。顺便说一句，当你解决根本问题时（这通常更难），你在技术上试图防止的问题也会消失。希望这会有所帮助。

blocks.html_en:
> People driving dangerously is not a technical problem.

Complete bullshit. This problem can be completely solved by a technical, not societal solution: actually working self-driving cars. Once they are available, all your imperfect societal solutions to the problem are not needed anymore.

sentences.en: > People driving dangerously is not a technical problem.Complete bullshit.
sentences.zh: > 人们危险驾驶不是技术问题。完全是胡说八道。

sentences.en: This problem can be completely solved by a technical, not societal solution: actually working self-driving cars.
sentences.zh: 这个问题可以通过技术而非社会解决方案来彻底解决：让自动驾驶汽车真正发挥作用。

sentences.en: Once they are available, all your imperfect societal solutions to the problem are not needed anymore.
sentences.zh: 一旦它们可用，所有不完美的社会问题解决方案就不再需要了。

blocks.html_en: That future is not here yet, but my point still stands: once they are sufficiently developed (it's only a matter of time, perhaps 5 years, perhaps 20 years), your societal problem suddenly has a technological solution. QED.

blocks.html_en:
"Sufficiently developed" seems like a cheap proof. There are still many non-technological problems above and below it.

For example, how do you ensure everyone on the road has a self-driving car? We've sufficiently developed electric cars, but they're not nearly the majority.

sentences.en: "Sufficiently developed" seems like a cheap proof.
sentences.zh: “充分发展”似乎是一个廉价的证据。

sentences.en: We've sufficiently developed electric cars, but they're not nearly the majority.
sentences.zh: 我们已经充分开发了电动汽车，但它们还不是大多数。

blocks.html_en:
Didn't I? I have given a direction as a reply. Telling that the road you're planning to go down is not the right one. I have offered you a pruning pattern for your decision tree in your mind, urging you to think on what you have proposed and look at it with a critical eye.

I have offered you to look for other examples, or find a way which has been traveled less or at least ponder on "why?" of this comment. Somebody else did down below, asked for clarification. Somebody beat me to it with a much better example then I can think at that very moment, adding more context and information into my comment.

I don't know you, your circumstances or preferences, hence dictating the way is neither the wise thing to do, nor it's my responsibility to begin with, since you didn't ask me about my opinions about the right way in the first place.

There's help everywhere who dare to look, or explore the the thing they have found and ponder on it. You have a mighty thing in your skull. A brain, a mind. Use it and explore its potential. It might not be easy in the beginning, but it'll be rewarding at the end.

At least, I can promise that to you.

sentences.en: Didn't I?
sentences.zh: 我没有吗？

sentences.en: I have given a direction as a reply.
sentences.zh: 我已经给出了一个方向作为答复。

sentences.en: Telling that the road you're planning to go down is not the right one.
sentences.zh: 告诉你你计划走的路不正确。

sentences.en: I have offered you a pruning pattern for your decision tree in your mind, urging you to think on what you have proposed and look at it with a critical eye.I have offered you to look for other examples, or find a way which has been traveled less or at least ponder on "why?"
sentences.zh: 我为你的头脑中的决策树提供了一种修剪模式，敦促你思考你所提出的建议，并以批判的眼光看待它。我建议你寻找其他例子，或者找到一种较少被采用的方法，或者至少思考“为什么”？

sentences.en: of this comment.
sentences.zh: 的这个评论。

sentences.en: Somebody else did down below, asked for clarification.
sentences.zh: 下面有人做了，要求澄清。

sentences.en: Somebody beat me to it with a much better example then I can think at that very moment, adding more context and information into my comment.I don't know you, your circumstances or preferences, hence dictating the way is neither the wise thing to do, nor it's my responsibility to begin with, since you didn't ask me about my opinions about the right way in the first place.There's help everywhere who dare to look, or explore the the thing they have found and ponder on it.
sentences.zh: 有人用一个更好的例子打败了我，然后我就可以在那一刻思考，在我的评论中添加更多的背景和信息。我不了解你，你的情况或偏好，因此指定方式既不是明智的事情，也不是我的责任，因为你一开始就没有询问我对正确方式的看法。到处都有帮助，谁敢于寻找，或探索他们发现并思考的东西。

sentences.en: Use it and explore its potential.
sentences.zh: 使用它并探索它的潜力。

sentences.en: It might not be easy in the beginning, but it'll be rewarding at the end.At least, I can promise that to you.
sentences.zh: 一开始可能并不容易，但最终会有所收获。至少，我可以向你保证这一点。

blocks.html_en:
People have been trying to use technology to solve the problems technology since this began.

Your solution is probably the worse pipe dream I've ever seen articulated.

You state that current alternatives are too rubbish because you want to use the services you seem to despise. And your willing to pin your hopes on yet another technology to do it because this one is definitely trustworthy and has your best interests at heart... Naivety doesn't quite cover this.

sentences.en: People have been trying to use technology to solve the problems technology since this began.Your solution is probably the worse pipe dream I've ever seen articulated.You state that current alternatives are too rubbish because you want to use the services you seem to despise.
sentences.zh: 自从技术出现以来，人们一直在尝试使用技术来解决技术问题。您的解决方案可能是我见过的最糟糕的白日梦。您说当前的替代方案太垃圾了，因为您想使用您似乎鄙视的服务。

sentences.en: And your willing to pin your hopes on yet another technology to do it because this one is definitely trustworthy and has your best interests at heart...
sentences.zh: 并且您愿意将希望寄托在另一种技术上来做到这一点，因为该技术绝对值得信赖并且将您的最大利益放在心上......

sentences.en: Naivety doesn't quite cover this.
sentences.zh: 天真并不能完全掩盖这一点。

blocks.html_en:
Why is everyone here so myopic?

Why pay Kagi or whatever at all? How can you trust them or anyone, if this is your belief?

Why even pay your ISP? Just go back to candlelight and corn flakes and stay away from radio waves.

You'd better not own a smart phone if this is truly how you feel about the world, because it clashes with what you just said.

Come on.

sentences.en: Why is everyone here so myopic?Why pay Kagi or whatever at all?
sentences.zh: 为什么这里的每个人都如此短视？为什么要付钱给 Kagi 或其他什么？

sentences.en: How can you trust them or anyone, if this is your belief?Why even pay your ISP?
sentences.zh: 如果这是您的信念，您怎么能信任他们或任何人？为什么还要向您的 ISP 付费呢？

sentences.en: Just go back to candlelight and corn flakes and stay away from radio waves.You'd better not own a smart phone if this is truly how you feel about the world, because it clashes with what you just said.Come on.
sentences.zh: 回到烛光和玉米片，远离无线电波。如果这真的是你对世界的感受，你最好不要拥有智能手机，因为它与你刚才所说的相冲突。来吧。

blocks.html_en:
So people that don't agree with you are myopic?

You solution is to hope that a better technology will be invented that won't be subject to what every technology before it has been subject to. You rally against "the algorithm" and beg for another algorithm to save you. You want to live in a filter bubble and not be challenged on you believes. I can't think of anything worse, but I wish you the best.

Also I don't know what comment you read but it's clearly not mine...

sentences.zh: 那么不同意你观点的人是短视的吗？你的解决方案是希望能够发明一种更好的技术，而不会受到之前每种技术的影响。

sentences.zh: 你希望生活在过滤泡沫中，而不希望自己的信念受到挑战。

sentences.en: I can't think of anything worse, but I wish you the best.Also I don't know what comment you read but it's clearly not mine...
sentences.zh: 我想不出更糟糕的事情了，但我祝你一切顺利。而且我不知道你读了什么评论，但这显然不是我的......

blocks.html_en:
I only have so much energy to reply to all of this negativity. Half of it is being cut off at the legs by HN filters from the constant downvotes and flagging. Downvotes and flagging for attempting to be positive and excited about tech progress.

You're not going to do anything about big tech by sitting on the sidelines. I don't see anyone doing anything about it. Nobody's built websites that show people how to contact their legislators. Nobody's building alternative tech.

We have a chance to use this tech to cut off the big tech overlords. I don't see how you're all missing this. We can literally build new browsers now. It reasons that we can place filters at every level of the stack to catch the stuff we don't like and prevent it from reaching us.

For people who supposedly care so much about tech and privacy and digital freedoms, I just see complaints.

I propose a solution to neuter big tech and you label it as a filter bubble instead of a victory.

But you're probably right - I'd like to filter out every grumpy HN person with a negative outlook sentiment score and move on with my day. It'd save me time. I shouldn't be wasting any energy responding to this den of depression.

sentences.en: I only have so much energy to reply to all of this negativity.
sentences.zh: 我只有这么多的精力来回应所有这些消极情绪。

sentences.en: Half of it is being cut off at the legs by HN filters from the constant downvotes and flagging.
sentences.zh: 其中一半被 HN 过滤器从持续的否决和标记中切断。

sentences.en: Downvotes and flagging for attempting to be positive and excited about tech progress.You're not going to do anything about big tech by sitting on the sidelines.
sentences.zh: 对试图对科技进步持积极和兴奋态度的人投反对票和举报。你不会通过袖手旁观来对大型科技做任何事情。

sentences.en: I don't see anyone doing anything about it.
sentences.zh: 我没有看到有人对此采取任何行动。

sentences.en: Nobody's built websites that show people how to contact their legislators.
sentences.zh: 没有人建立网站来告诉人们如何联系立法者。

sentences.en: Nobody's building alternative tech.We have a chance to use this tech to cut off the big tech overlords.
sentences.zh: 没有人在开发替代技术。我们有机会利用这项技术来切断大型科技霸主的地位。

sentences.en: I don't see how you're all missing this.
sentences.zh: 我不明白你们怎么都错过了这个。

sentences.en: We can literally build new browsers now.
sentences.zh: 我们现在可以真正构建新的浏览器。

sentences.en: It reasons that we can place filters at every level of the stack to catch the stuff we don't like and prevent it from reaching us.For people who supposedly care so much about tech and privacy and digital freedoms, I just see complaints.I propose a solution to neuter big tech and you label it as a filter bubble instead of a victory.But you're probably right - I'd like to filter out every grumpy HN person with a negative outlook sentiment score and move on with my day.
sentences.zh: 这意味着我们可以在堆栈的每个级别放置过滤器，以捕获我们不喜欢的东西并阻止它们到达我们。对于那些据称非常关心技术、隐私和数字自由的人，我只是看到抱怨。我提出了一个中和大型科技的解决方案，你将其标记为过滤泡沫而不是胜利。但你可能是对的 - 我想过滤掉每一个脾气暴躁、前景情绪评分为负面的 HN 人，然后继续我的一天。

sentences.en: It'd save me time.
sentences.zh: 这会节省我的时间。

sentences.en: I shouldn't be wasting any energy responding to this den of depression.
sentences.zh: 我不应该浪费任何精力来应对这个抑郁症的巢穴。

sentences.en: We can literally build new browsers now.
sentences.zh: 我们现在可以真正构建新的浏览器。

sentences.en: It reasons that we can place filters at every level of the stack to catch the stuff we don't like and prevent it from reaching us.
sentences.zh: 这说明我们可以在堆栈的每一层放置过滤器来捕获我们不喜欢的东西并阻止它到达我们手中。

blocks.html_en: For people who supposedly care so much about tech and privacy and digital freedoms, I just see complaints.

sentences.en: For people who supposedly care so much about tech and privacy and digital freedoms, I just see complaints.
sentences.zh: 对于那些据称非常关心技术、隐私和数字自由的人来说，我只看到抱怨。

blocks.html_en: I propose a solution to neuter big tech and you label it as a filter bubble instead of a victory.

sentences.en: I propose a solution to neuter big tech and you label it as a filter bubble instead of a victory.
sentences.zh: 我提出了一个中立大型科技的解决方案，你将其标记为过滤泡沫而不是胜利。

blocks.html_en: But you're probably right - I'd like to filter out every grumpy HN person with a negative outlook sentiment score and move on with my day. It'd save me time. I shouldn't be wasting any energy responding to this den of depression.

sentences.en: But you're probably right - I'd like to filter out every grumpy HN person with a negative outlook sentiment score and move on with my day.
sentences.zh: 但你可能是对的——我想过滤掉每一个脾气暴躁、前景情绪评分为负面的 HN 人，然后继续我的一天。

sentences.en: It'd save me time.
sentences.zh: 这会节省我的时间。

sentences.en: I shouldn't be wasting any energy responding to this den of depression.
sentences.zh: 我不应该浪费任何精力来应对这个抑郁症的巢穴。

blocks.html_en:
Negativity does not equals disagreement. I disagree with you I'm not being negative. I can't speak to downvotes and flagging.

Also I find it ironic that you would state no one is building alternatives when you previously lambasted the alternatives (blue sky, kagi, etc). What are you doing? Apart from engaging in fantasy.

Your solution is to have big tech filter out the stuff you don't like. These agents are a product of big tech. They need massive resources in terms of money, hardware, and resources to run. This isn't tech for the poor and downtrodden, it's tech for the rich and privileged. By the time the poor can afford it (if it doesn't destroy the environment first) it'll already be infected by what you despise in current big tech; if it isn't already.

sentences.en: What are you doing?
sentences.zh: 你在干什么？

sentences.zh: 除了幻想之外。你的解决方案是让大型科技过滤掉你不喜欢的东西。

blocks.html_en:
Kimi is open weights and you can rent cloud compute cheaply. It's only going to get more affordable.

Once a proof of concept exists you can distill it down to something that might execute on CPU alone.

With open weights models you can build systems that rend big tech apart.

Last-gen tech has been tried for twenty years. It's not working and it has never gotten distribution.

You know what does work? Adblock. It reasons that we could build a new form of it.

I would pay for someone to build this because I'm busy building other things. I would literally commit money to anyone who suggests they would build this.

sentences.en: Kimi is open weights and you can rent cloud compute cheaply.
sentences.zh: Kimi 是开放权重，您可以便宜地租用云计算。

sentences.en: It's only going to get more affordable.Once a proof of concept exists you can distill it down to something that might execute on CPU alone.With open weights models you can build systems that rend big tech apart.Last-gen tech has been tried for twenty years.
sentences.zh: 它只会变得更便宜。一旦存在概念证明，您就可以将其提炼为可以单独在 CPU 上执行的东西。通过开放权重模型，您可以构建将大型技术分开的系统。上一代技术已经尝试了二十年。

sentences.en: It's not working and it has never gotten distribution.You know what does work?
sentences.zh: 它不起作用，也从未得到分发。你知道什么起作用吗？

sentences.en: Adblock.
sentences.zh: 广告拦截。

sentences.en: It reasons that we could build a new form of it.I would pay for someone to build this because I'm busy building other things.
sentences.zh: 这说明我们可以构建一种新的形式。我会花钱请人来构建这个，因为我正忙于构建其他东西。

sentences.en: I would literally commit money to anyone who suggests they would build this.
sentences.zh: 我真的愿意向任何建议他们建造这个的人投入资金。

blocks.html_en:
If a majority of users switch to ad-powered free tiers, a majority of users will see well-written, targeted advice that is ads (lies!) in disguise.

This is a horrible future to consider.

The more I think about it, the more I come to the conclusion that a society that bans ads is a healthier, more robust society, and I am not alone.

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=43595269

blocks.html_en:
You can't fight this battle in the current judicial system. If before anyone can successfully bring a lawsuit in front of a judge the company has unlimited money, then at least one of these three things will be true:

a. the damages won't make a dent

b. they can throw lawyers at the problem for the win

c. they will have grafted their way into the economy so deeply that no one will want to kill their main source of income

This battle is already lost, if they find economical success then they are untouchable.

sentences.en: You can't fight this battle in the current judicial system.
sentences.zh: 在当前的司法体系中你无法打这场仗。

sentences.en: If before anyone can successfully bring a lawsuit in front of a judge the company has unlimited money, then at least one of these three things will be true:a.
sentences.zh: 如果在任何人能够在法官面前成功提起诉讼之前，公司拥有无限的资金，那么以下三件事中至少有一件是正确的：

sentences.en: the damages won't make a dentb.
sentences.zh: 损害不会造成损害。

sentences.en: they can throw lawyers at the problem for the winc.
sentences.zh: 他们可以请律师为胜利者解决问题。

sentences.en: they will have grafted their way into the economy so deeply that no one will want to kill their main source of incomeThis battle is already lost, if they find economical success then they are untouchable.
sentences.zh: 他们将深深地融入到经济中，以至于没有人会想扼杀他们的主要收入来源。这场战斗已经失败了，如果他们在经济上取得了成功，那么他们将是不可触碰的。

blocks.html_en:
I am also worried about this. Typical ads are already devastatingly effective against most people, causing incalculable distortions in market dynamics and incalculable losses to consumers. This is an order of magnitude worse.

But there is something even more insidious: elections. Ads will be purchased to shape public perceptions. Those with the deepest pockets will have ads which are charismatic, convincing, and unbelievably effective. This is the death of democracy if regulation isn't adopted right now.

Unfortunately this isn't straightforward. The distinction between a product, service, person, and party are impossible to clearly delineate. Is a SpaceX ad an endorsement of Elon Musk? Is an Elon Musk ad an endorsement of the U.S. Republicans? The famous Citizens United ruling determined that there is no practical legal distinction in this domain, meaning that without a Constitutional amendment (which will never happen in this political climate), the U.S. is going to barrel headlong into some kind of dystopia.

My only ray of sunshine here is competition. If OpenAI gets a reputation for untrustworthy AI, and there are viable competitors, people will hopefully switch. It's messy and imperfect and will require constant vigilance (because they're not going to tell us when they're misleading us). I think open weights models have become very good, and will improve much more over time. When we can reasonably run these models locally, ubiquitously, we have cost effective options.

sentences.en: I am also worried about this.
sentences.zh: 我也很担心这个问题。

sentences.en: Typical ads are already devastatingly effective against most people, causing incalculable distortions in market dynamics and incalculable losses to consumers.
sentences.zh: 典型的广告对大多数人来说已经具有毁灭性的效果，对市场动态造成不可估量的扭曲，给消费者带来不可估量的损失。

sentences.en: This is an order of magnitude worse.But there is something even more insidious: elections.
sentences.zh: 这更糟糕。但还有更阴险的事情：选举。

sentences.en: Ads will be purchased to shape public perceptions.
sentences.zh: 购买广告是为了塑造公众的看法。

sentences.en: Those with the deepest pockets will have ads which are charismatic, convincing, and unbelievably effective.
sentences.zh: 那些财力最雄厚的人将推出具有魅力、令人信服且极其有效的广告。

sentences.en: This is the death of democracy if regulation isn't adopted right now.Unfortunately this isn't straightforward.
sentences.zh: 如果现在不采取监管，民主就会消亡。不幸的是，这并不简单。

sentences.en: The distinction between a product, service, person, and party are impossible to clearly delineate.
sentences.zh: 产品、服务、个人和团体之间的区别是不可能清晰界定的。

sentences.en: Is a SpaceX ad an endorsement of Elon Musk?
sentences.zh: SpaceX 的广告是对埃隆·马斯克的认可吗？

sentences.en: Is an Elon Musk ad an endorsement of the U.S. Republicans?
sentences.zh: 埃隆·马斯克的广告是对美国共和党的认可吗？

sentences.en: The famous Citizens United ruling determined that there is no practical legal distinction in this domain, meaning that without a Constitutional amendment (which will never happen in this political climate), the U.S. is going to barrel headlong into some kind of dystopia.My only ray of sunshine here is competition.
sentences.zh: 著名的公民联合裁决确定，这一领域不存在实际的法律区别，这意味着如果没有宪法修正案（在这种政治气候下永远不会发生），美国将一头扎进某种反乌托邦。我在这里唯一的阳光就是竞争。

sentences.en: If OpenAI gets a reputation for untrustworthy AI, and there are viable competitors, people will hopefully switch.
sentences.zh: 如果 OpenAI 因人工智能不可信而名声大噪，并且存在可行的竞争对手，那么人们有望转向。

sentences.en: It's messy and imperfect and will require constant vigilance (because they're not going to tell us when they're misleading us).
sentences.zh: 它是混乱和不完美的，需要不断保持警惕（因为他们在误导我们时不会告诉我们）。

sentences.en: I think open weights models have become very good, and will improve much more over time.
sentences.zh: 我认为开放权重模型已经变得非常好，并且随着时间的推移将会得到更大的改进。

sentences.en: When we can reasonably run these models locally, ubiquitously, we have cost effective options.
sentences.zh: 当我们能够在本地、无处不在地合理运行这些模型时，我们就拥有了具有成本效益的选择。

blocks.html_en:
From the article, it looks like ads are separate from answers in a clearly delineated way. It's not quite as nefarious as what you're suggesting. Frankly, I'm surprised.

This is only round one however, and I could see a future where they offer more "intimate" experiences. At least that's not today.

But it what's the solution to free services? There is many people that can't afford to have to pay for search, emails, maps. On top of paying for accessing content, micro transactions. For Internet related companies what if the country the person is from, doesn't have access to the financial system of the country that company is operating from.

I think It might be better to regulate them than remove them. Or to find a solution for people that can't access it.

(Another is higher tiers, free for personal users etc)

I hate ads but I also think about what's the alternative

blocks.html_en:
If it's like the commercials in the movie the Truman show I think this could be a nightmare.

I think it all boils down to how (if?) this will be transparent to the user.

If the response suggests a sponsored product/service to the user it could either be the specific product/service that ChatGPT is biased to suggest or the whole idea of using a product/service. As a user I would like to know both of these biases to understand that using any product/service for my need might not even be the best thing.

blocks.html_en: ChatGPT is way above typical CPM, maybe $60-$100. That puts it in the same category as premium YT verticals. If they can sell enough at this price point it would make a big impact, but I'm super-doubtful they can do that; campaigns are basically blind at this stage, and I would not be surprised if this was intentional.

blocks.html_en:
> Ah, so you are against ads! At least the way ads are today. With that I mean that the way ads today mostly focus on manipulating over informing is effectively hindering your ability to choose.

Speaking as someone who worked in advertising for a looooonnnngggg time, there are three ways that ads seem to work (above a randomised holdout).

1. The ad is something that the person actually wants, and they then convert.

2. The ad is something that the person was about to buy, and they convert.

3. The ad is part of a long running campaign that shifts (some) consumer's behaviour over time (ads for Coke etc fall in this category).

Ads aren't magic, and most people ignore them most of the time. It turns out that enough people don't that they can be profitable sometimes, but mostly that's just targeting people with more money than sense.

This HN notion that ads shape everyone's behaviour (except me, obviously) is so disconnected from my experience that I am sometimes confused by it.

sentences.en: > Ah, so you are against ads!
sentences.zh: > 啊，原来你反对广告！

blocks.html_en:
> Ah, so you are against ads! At least the way ads are today. With that I mean that the way ads today mostly focus on manipulating over informing is effectively hindering your ability to choose.

Speaking as someone who worked in advertising for a looooonnnngggg time, there are three ways that ads seem to work (above a randomised holdout).

1. The ad is something that the person actually wants, and they then convert.

2. The ad is something that the person was about to buy, and they convert.

3. The ad is part of a long running campaign that shifts (some) consumer's behaviour over time (ads for Coke etc fall in this category).

Ads aren't magic, and most people ignore them most of the time. It turns out that enough people don't that they can be profitable sometimes, but mostly that's just targeting people with more money than sense.

This HN notion that ads shape everyone's behaviour (except me, obviously) is so disconnected from my experience that I am sometimes confused by it.

blocks.html_en:
When I walk around my increasingly billboard-infested hometown. I observe that almost every ad actually nudges me in some way: whether I should switch internet providers, whether my financial strategy is fine, what kind of scam this tabloid newspaper is running, etc...

I believe most people are influenced by these constant micro-impressions far more than they realize.

My best defense is to build a resentment towards the companies being advertised.

sentences.en: When I walk around my increasingly billboard-infested hometown.
sentences.zh: 当我在广告牌越来越多的家乡走来走去时。

sentences.en: I observe that almost every ad actually nudges me in some way: whether I should switch internet providers, whether my financial strategy is fine, what kind of scam this tabloid newspaper is running, etc...I believe most people are influenced by these constant micro-impressions far more than they realize.My best defense is to build a resentment towards the companies being advertised.
sentences.zh: 我观察到，几乎每一个广告实际上都在某种程度上推动了我：我是否应该更换互联网提供商、我的财务策略是否良好、这家小报正在进行什么样的骗局等等……我相信大多数人受到这些持续的微印象的影响远比他们意识到的要多。我最好的防御措施是对广告中的公司产生怨恨。

blocks.html_en:
> I believe most people are influenced by these constant micro-impressions far more than they realize.

Your feelings are fair, but if this were the case then we'd see far more consumers changing their preferred phone/electricity/TV provider on a regular basis, and we could correlate this with (lagged) advertising spend.

But there is very little evidence for this. Like, I have a doctorate in psychology, and behaviour change is really, really hard and only works in a small number of cases. That model does seem to have predictive power, in that very few people convert as a result of seeing an ad (less than 1/1000 in the very best cases), and most of those that do don't keep buying products for long.

Now, if you already do something (like order takeaway pizza) it's much easier to get someone to do more of that, but its very very very hard to establish a new behaviour.

sentences.en: > I believe most people are influenced by these constant micro-impressions far more than they realize.Your feelings are fair, but if this were the case then we'd see far more consumers changing their preferred phone/electricity/TV provider on a regular basis, and we could correlate this with (lagged) advertising spend.But there is very little evidence for this.
sentences.zh: > 我相信大多数人受到这些持续的微印象的影响远比他们意识到的要大。你的感觉是公平的，但如果是这样的话，那么我们会看到更多的消费者定期更换他们首选的电话/电力/电视提供商，我们可以将其与（滞后的）广告支出联系起来。但很少有证据表明这一点。

sentences.en: Like, I have a doctorate in psychology, and behaviour change is really, really hard and only works in a small number of cases.
sentences.zh: 比如，我有心理学博士学位，行为改变真的非常非常困难，而且只在少数情况下有效。

sentences.en: That model does seem to have predictive power, in that very few people convert as a result of seeing an ad (less than 1/1000 in the very best cases), and most of those that do don't keep buying products for long.Now, if you already do something (like order takeaway pizza) it's much easier to get someone to do more of that, but its very very very hard to establish a new behaviour.
sentences.zh: 该模型似乎确实具有预测能力，因为很少有人会因为看到广告而进行转化（在最好的情况下不到 1/1000），而且大多数人不会长期持续购买产品。现在，如果你已经做了某件事（例如订购外卖披萨），那么让某人做更多的事情就容易得多，但建立新的行为却非常非常困难。

blocks.html_en:
Anecdotally, lots of intelligent people I know like to pretend to be completely uninfluenced by advertising, which I think is delusional.

In my view advertising is in big parts (your category 3) basically a constant side-channel attack against peoples minds, eroding decisionmaking and purchasing decisions (mostly!) to peoples detriment.

The net-beneficial aspects (product information, and, arguably, boosted general demand) could be better achieved otherwise.

sentences.en: Anecdotally, lots of intelligent people I know like to pretend to be completely uninfluenced by advertising, which I think is delusional.In my view advertising is in big parts (your category 3) basically a constant side-channel attack against peoples minds, eroding decisionmaking and purchasing decisions (mostly!)
sentences.zh: 有趣的是，我认识的很多聪明人喜欢假装完全不受广告影响，我认为这是一种妄想。在我看来，广告在很大程度上（您的类别 3）基本上是对人们思想的持续旁道攻击，侵蚀决策和购买决策（大部分！）

sentences.en: to peoples detriment.The net-beneficial aspects (product information, and, arguably, boosted general demand) could be better achieved otherwise.
sentences.zh: 否则，可以更好地实现净效益方面（产品信息，并且可以说是提高了总体需求）。

blocks.html_en: A single polluting factory won't ruin the lungs of a single person, but thousands of factories will have an unhealthy effect on a population.

sentences.zh: 一家污染工厂不会损害一个人的肺部，但数千家工厂就会对人口产生不健康的影响。

blocks.html_en: Your explanation should be common sense for anyone who has been around long enough and I agree that it seems to be fashionable to claim that ads have a nefarious, hypnotic effect on The Masses. The thing that does seem to have an outsized effect on how people think is their desire to be seen as part of a group and they tend to signal this by repeating arguments they've heard members of that group make. That's where advertising/propaganda has a real foothold in everyone's psychology. If you form a parasocial bond with an influencer or a group it does have a strong effect on your behaviour since you want to be liked by them so you will say and do things you think they'll approve of and agree with what they say and do. No one is really exempt from this since being exempt involves complete isolation and/or extreme disagreeableness. The only real remedy is having strong bonds with other people who aren't hopelessly beholden to outside influences. No small task since it only takes one member of the group to act as a channel.

sentences.en: Your explanation should be common sense for anyone who has been around long enough and I agree that it seems to be fashionable to claim that ads have a nefarious, hypnotic effect on The Masses.
sentences.zh: 对于任何一个已经存在了足够长的时间的人来说，你的解释应该是常识，我同意声称广告对大众具有邪恶的催眠效果似乎很流行。

sentences.en: The thing that does seem to have an outsized effect on how people think is their desire to be seen as part of a group and they tend to signal this by repeating arguments they've heard members of that group make.
sentences.zh: 对人们的思维方式产生巨大影响的事情似乎是他们渴望被视为某个群体的一部分，并且他们倾向于通过重复他们听到的该群体成员提出的论点来表达这一点。

sentences.en: That's where advertising/propaganda has a real foothold in everyone's psychology.
sentences.zh: 这就是广告/宣传在每个人的心理中真正立足的地方。

sentences.en: If you form a parasocial bond with an influencer or a group it does have a strong effect on your behaviour since you want to be liked by them so you will say and do things you think they'll approve of and agree with what they say and do.
sentences.zh: 如果你与有影响力的人或群体形成了一种准社会联系，它确实会对你的行为产生很大的影响，因为你希望被他们喜欢，所以你会说和做你认为他们会赞同并同意他们所说和所做的事情。

sentences.en: No one is really exempt from this since being exempt involves complete isolation and/or extreme disagreeableness.
sentences.zh: 没有人能真正免受这一影响，因为豁免意味着完全孤立和/或极度不愉快。

sentences.en: The only real remedy is having strong bonds with other people who aren't hopelessly beholden to outside influences.
sentences.zh: 唯一真正的补救办法是与其他人建立牢固的联系，而不要完全受制于外界的影响。

sentences.en: No small task since it only takes one member of the group to act as a channel.
sentences.zh: 这不是一项小任务，因为只需要小组中的一名成员充当渠道。

blocks.html_en:
> The thing that does seem to have an outsized effect on how people think is their desire to be seen as part of a group and they tend to signal this by repeating arguments they've heard members of that group make. That's where advertising/propaganda has a real foothold in everyone's psychology.

Monkey see, monkey do. This is basically how human society works, and is impossible to stop. The best you could do would be control who people hang around with, but even that is almost impossibly hard to make work.

blocks.html_en:
Concealed advertising is highly problematic and deserves discussion. It is not, however, what OpenAI introduced in the article above. Their current ad formats are, apparently, transparently labeled and clearly separated.

It is very likely that this will adjust in the future. Just look at how indistinguishable from organic content some prominent ad placements have become over time. But: There is merit in discussing the actual product, not some hypothetical version of it. If we do discuss hypotheticals, we should make that explicit.

This also helps the whole debate (and therefore, I believe, ad critics): arguments grounded in reality are usually far more effective than those that aren't. ... And there might come a point where OpenAI introduces some kind of native ad format. We would be well advised to preserve our credibility for that discussion.

edit: Paid content in the organic LLM output is a current problem already. It ist just not what this announcement is about.

sentences.en: Concealed advertising is highly problematic and deserves discussion.
sentences.zh: 隐蔽广告问题严重，值得讨论。

sentences.en: It is not, however, what OpenAI introduced in the article above.
sentences.zh: 然而，这并不是 OpenAI 在上面文章中介绍的内容。

sentences.en: Their current ad formats are, apparently, transparently labeled and clearly separated.It is very likely that this will adjust in the future.
sentences.zh: 显然，他们目前的广告格式是透明标签和清晰分离的。未来很可能会进行调整。

sentences.en: Just look at how indistinguishable from organic content some prominent ad placements have become over time.
sentences.zh: 只需看看随着时间的推移，一些突出的广告展示位置与自然内容变得多么难以区分。

sentences.en: But: There is merit in discussing the actual product, not some hypothetical version of it.
sentences.zh: 但是：讨论实际产品是有价值的，而不是它的一些假设版本。

sentences.en: If we do discuss hypotheticals, we should make that explicit.This also helps the whole debate (and therefore, I believe, ad critics): arguments grounded in reality are usually far more effective than those that aren't. ...
sentences.zh: 如果我们确实讨论假设，我们应该明确这一点。这也有助于整个辩论（因此，我相信，广告评论家）：基于现实的论点通常比那些不基于现实的论点有效得多。 ...

sentences.en: And there might come a point where OpenAI introduces some kind of native ad format.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 可能会引入某种原生广告格式。

sentences.en: We would be well advised to preserve our credibility for that discussion.edit: Paid content in the organic LLM output is a current problem already.
sentences.zh: 我们强烈建议保持我们在该讨论中的可信度。编辑：有机法学硕士输出中的付费内容已经是当前的问题。

sentences.en: It ist just not what this announcement is about.
sentences.zh: 这不是本公告的目的。

blocks.html_en:
> Unpopular opinion: I think users should be free to choose.

There is nothing unpopular about your opinion - silent majority definitely wants ad-subsidized free tiers (that is why they are so popular and ads are not banned anywhere in the western world), it is just that arguing with radicals on HN or reddit is exhausting and a waste of time in general.

sentences.en: > Unpopular opinion: I think users should be free to choose.There is nothing unpopular about your opinion - silent majority definitely wants ad-subsidized free tiers (that is why they are so popular and ads are not banned anywhere in the western world), it is just that arguing with radicals on HN or reddit is exhausting and a waste of time in general.
sentences.zh: > 不受欢迎的观点：我认为用户应该自由选择。你的观点并没有什么不受欢迎的地方——沉默的大多数肯定想要广告补贴的免费套餐（这就是为什么它们如此受欢迎，广告在西方世界任何地方都没有被禁止），只是在 HN 或 reddit 上与激进分子争论通常很累，而且浪费时间。

blocks.html_en: If you are implying that this is the case because this is also how leaders are selected in a democracy, then of course the sarcasm is noted - but here's my real unpopular opinion: Democracy only has value when the majority of voters are not morons. Unfortunately, I can't come up with a better solution - maybe create a model for which there is a wide agreement on and let it rule?

sentences.en: If you are implying that this is the case because this is also how leaders are selected in a democracy, then of course the sarcasm is noted - but here's my real unpopular opinion: Democracy only has value when the majority of voters are not morons.
sentences.zh: 如果你暗示情况就是如此，因为这也是民主国家选举领导人的方式，那么当然会注意到讽刺——但这是我真正不受欢迎的观点：只有当大多数选民不是白痴时，民主才有价值。

sentences.en: Unfortunately, I can't come up with a better solution - maybe create a model for which there is a wide agreement on and let it rule?
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，我无法想出更好的解决方案——也许创建一个得到广泛共识的模型并让它来统治？

blocks.html_en: I simply meant that users ought to be free to choose between a product that offers a free ad-supported tier and one that is paid and ad-free. And I do believe that letting morons choose for themselves is the lesser evil.

sentences.en: I simply meant that users ought to be free to choose between a product that offers a free ad-supported tier and one that is paid and ad-free.
sentences.zh: 我的意思只是说，用户应该可以自由地在提供免费广告支持层的产品和付费且无广告层的产品之间进行选择。

sentences.en: And I do believe that letting morons choose for themselves is the lesser evil.
sentences.zh: 我确实相信让白痴自己选择是两害相权取其轻。

blocks.html_en: It doesn't really matter what the system of government is, power-hungry psychopaths will always attempt to game the system in their favour. The history of governance is just a long arms race of inventing new systems to thwart them while they do everything they can to corrupt those systems and concentrate power in themselves. It's like fending off griefers in an online game, there's an endless supply of them, they don't care about the rules or the game, they just want to dominate other players for their own perverse enjoyment. The only lasting solution would either be some form of genetic modification to remove that trait from humanity entirely or some perfect way to identify them but you'd have to be extremely careful that they didn't gain access to and corrupt those things before they were completely eliminated.

sentences.en: It doesn't really matter what the system of government is, power-hungry psychopaths will always attempt to game the system in their favour.
sentences.zh: 政府制度是什么并不重要，渴望权力的精神病患者总是会试图利用制度来对他们有利。

sentences.en: The history of governance is just a long arms race of inventing new systems to thwart them while they do everything they can to corrupt those systems and concentrate power in themselves.
sentences.zh: 治理的历史只是一场漫长的军备竞赛，发明新的系统来阻止它们，而他们则竭尽全力腐蚀这些系统并将权力集中在自己身上。

sentences.en: It's like fending off griefers in an online game, there's an endless supply of them, they don't care about the rules or the game, they just want to dominate other players for their own perverse enjoyment.
sentences.zh: 就像网络游戏中的打击恶意分子一样，他们的数量无穷无尽，他们不关心规则或游戏，他们只想统治其他玩家以获得自己的变态享受。

sentences.en: The only lasting solution would either be some form of genetic modification to remove that trait from humanity entirely or some perfect way to identify them but you'd have to be extremely careful that they didn't gain access to and corrupt those things before they were completely eliminated.
sentences.zh: 唯一持久的解决方案要么是某种形式的基因改造，以完全消除人类的这种特征，要么是某种完美的方式来识别它们，但你必须非常小心，以免它们在完全消除之前获得并破坏这些东西。

blocks.html_en: I prefer "Democracy only has value when the majority of voters are informed". This covers your case, but it also redresses the balance to shift blame away from everyday people and onto society as a whole.

sentences.en: I prefer "Democracy only has value when the majority of voters are informed".
sentences.zh: 我更喜欢“民主只有在大多数选民都了解情况时才有价值”。

sentences.en: This covers your case, but it also redresses the balance to shift blame away from everyday people and onto society as a whole.
sentences.zh: 这涵盖了你的情况，但它也纠正了平衡，将责任从普通人身上转移到整个社会上。

blocks.html_en: Isn't society made up of everyday people? While I agree that the public being informed is a mutual responsibility, placing that responsibility on the government (which in many cases has plenty of incentive to keeping people ignorant) will not work either.

sentences.en: Isn't society made up of everyday people?
sentences.zh: 社会不就是由普通人组成的吗？

sentences.en: While I agree that the public being informed is a mutual responsibility, placing that responsibility on the government (which in many cases has plenty of incentive to keeping people ignorant) will not work either.
sentences.zh: 虽然我同意公众知情是一项共同的责任，但将此责任交给政府（在许多情况下，政府有足够的动机让人们保持无知）也行不通。

blocks.html_en:
> placing that responsibility on the government will not work either.

I agree, but a) the responsibility is on us all: citizens, government, and the media b) we can definitely introduce regulations that force government to be more open, etc.

blocks.html_en: Here's my real unpopular opinion: the actual value of democracy is bloodless transition of power. Everything else is window dressing.

blocks.html_en: That's not a unique attribute of democracy nor is it guaranteed. Any system of government that hasn't succumbed to corruption and factionalism will have clear rules of succession. Even an absolute monarchy can go on for many generations without bloodshed.

sentences.en: That's not a unique attribute of democracy nor is it guaranteed.
sentences.zh: 这不是民主的独特属性，也不是民主的保证。

sentences.en: Any system of government that hasn't succumbed to corruption and factionalism will have clear rules of succession.
sentences.zh: 任何没有屈服于腐败和派系主义的政府体系都会有明确的继承规则。

sentences.en: Even an absolute monarchy can go on for many generations without bloodshed.
sentences.zh: 即使是绝对君主制也可以延续很多代而不发生流血事件。

blocks.html_en: The point is that frequent transitions seem to incentivise rhetorical instead of literal backstabbing.

blocks.html_en:
A $1.5Bil settlement just went in on stolen content used for training. It's arguably many times larger in long term damages especially compared to investment into those who stole the content.

If an AI company can scrape in billions of investment and keep going vs Facebook style "cost of doing business" fines, they will make that bet. If OpenAI can inject paid content, advice, misinformation, etc and keep operating or earn revenue today, it's worth doing. There are no ethics beyond optics.

I'm not saying these AI company CEOs would sell their mothers for more runway cash. But also nobody has seen their mothers since the second Sunday in May.

sentences.en: A $1.5Bil settlement just went in on stolen content used for training.
sentences.zh: 刚刚就用于培训的被盗内容达成了 15 亿美元的和解协议。

sentences.en: It's arguably many times larger in long term damages especially compared to investment into those who stole the content.If an AI company can scrape in billions of investment and keep going vs Facebook style "cost of doing business" fines, they will make that bet.
sentences.zh: 从长期来看，这可能要大很多倍，尤其是与对窃取内容的人进行投资相比。如果一家人工智能公司能够筹集数十亿美元的投资并继续运营，而不是像 Facebook 那样的“经营成本”罚款，他们就会下这个赌注。

sentences.en: If OpenAI can inject paid content, advice, misinformation, etc and keep operating or earn revenue today, it's worth doing.
sentences.zh: 如果 OpenAI 能够注入付费内容、建议、错误信息等并继续运营或赚取收入，那么这是值得做的。

sentences.en: There are no ethics beyond optics.I'm not saying these AI company CEOs would sell their mothers for more runway cash.
sentences.zh: 除了光学之外没有道德。我并不是说这些人工智能公司的首席执行官会为了更多的跑道现金而出卖他们的母亲。

sentences.en: But also nobody has seen their mothers since the second Sunday in May.
sentences.zh: 但自五月的第二个星期日以来，没有人见过他们的母亲。

blocks.html_en:
This is something that I noticed after doing a Google search on a new machine without uBlock Origin installed. I was unable to identify that the first link was an advertisement to a competitor's product of the brand I looked up. I remember adverts used to be highlighted really well in Google searches earlier.

If I am unable to differentiate between an ad and a search result then neither can a lot of non technical users including my parents.

Can't wait to see ChatGPT ads

sentences.zh: 我无法识别第一个链接是我查找的品牌的竞争对手产品的广告。

blocks.html_en: Funny that people still perpetuate the myth about boiling frogs; they absolutely jump out at a certain hot enough temperature regardless of how slowly it is heated.

blocks.html_en: I think it's impossible for these ads not to eventually get incorporated into responses. There is a class of people who treat an LLM as their super smart best friend who they talk to about everything. It's a level of intimacy and influence that no technology company has ever had before. Do you think that it's possible that a publicly traded (I know OpenAI isn't publicly traded at the moment but they are trying to IPO) company with a fiduciary duty to its shareholders to maximize returns wouldn't use this intimacy and influence to make money? It's not just an ad for a new toaster, it's your best friend who you trust about everything telling you that the toaster he's recommending is the world's best toaster, as selected by a being with superhuman intelligence. Obviously not everyone will react this way, but for people already undergoing AI psychosis or at the beginning stages it will just make things worse.

sentences.en: I think it's impossible for these ads not to eventually get incorporated into responses.
sentences.zh: 我认为这些广告最终不可能不被纳入回复中。

sentences.en: There is a class of people who treat an LLM as their super smart best friend who they talk to about everything.
sentences.zh: 有一群人将法学硕士视为他们超级聪明的最好朋友，他们可以与他们谈论所有事情。

sentences.en: It's a level of intimacy and influence that no technology company has ever had before.
sentences.zh: 这是任何一家科技公司都从未拥有过的亲密程度和影响力。

sentences.en: Do you think that it's possible that a publicly traded (I know OpenAI isn't publicly traded at the moment but they are trying to IPO) company with a fiduciary duty to its shareholders to maximize returns wouldn't use this intimacy and influence to make money?
sentences.zh: 您是否认为一家对股东负有信托责任以实现回报最大化的上市公司（我知道 OpenAI 目前尚未上市，但他们正在尝试 IPO）可能不会利用这种亲密关系和影响力来赚钱？

sentences.zh: 这不仅仅是一个新烤面包机的广告，它是你最好的朋友，你在一切事情上都信任他，告诉你他推荐的烤面包机是世界上最好的烤面包机，是由具有超人智慧的人选择的。

blocks.html_en:
This already has a name — people call it GEO/AEO. I do the legitimate end of it for my own site: structured data, clean factual pages, llms.txt, so that when an assistant answers a question in my domain it cites the source correctly. Right now the incentives are healthy, because the only way to "rank" in an answer is to be genuinely accurate and citable.

Paid placement inside answers breaks that alignment. Why invest in being citable when you can buy the token stream? Search took about fifteen years to fully make that trade. LLMs seem to be starting on it in year three.

sentences.en: This already has a name — people call it GEO/AEO.
sentences.zh: 这已经有了一个名字——人们称之为 GEO/AEO。

sentences.en: I do the legitimate end of it for my own site: structured data, clean factual pages, llms.txt, so that when an assistant answers a question in my domain it cites the source correctly.
sentences.zh: 我为自己的网站做了合法的处理：结构化数据、干净的事实页面、llms.txt，这样当助手在我的域中回答问题时，它就会正确引用来源。

sentences.en: Right now the incentives are healthy, because the only way to "rank" in an answer is to be genuinely accurate and citable.Paid placement inside answers breaks that alignment.
sentences.zh: 目前，激励措施是健康的，因为在答案中“排名”的唯一方法是真正准确且可引用。答案内的付费排名打破了这种一致性。

sentences.en: Why invest in being citable when you can buy the token stream?
sentences.zh: 当您可以购买代币流时，为什么还要投资于可引用的项目呢？

sentences.en: Search took about fifteen years to fully make that trade.
sentences.zh: 搜索公司花了大约十五年的时间才完全完成这项交易。

sentences.en: LLMs seem to be starting on it in year three.
sentences.zh: 法学硕士似乎从第三年开始。

blocks.html_en: Interesting that this was flagged when it is about the exact phenomenon being discussed, as I was also about to mention AEO until I read this comment. It is really not very different from SEO in that if you want your site to be ranked, it should have clean information, and don't try to game the system as otherwise you'll be banned like Google has done for decades now.

blocks.html_en:
The golden age of LLMs may be coming to an end. Soon we’ll see the same phenomenon as we have with Google Search results and with YouTube, with Facebook and Windows.

People will rend their garments and lament the loss of the „old models” but the logic of the system that produced them will remain unchanged. Everyone will keep asking themselves how it happened and what can be done about it.

Meanwhile the Claude that we have today can explain this to you if you know how to prompt.

sentences.en: The golden age of LLMs may be coming to an end.
sentences.zh: 法学硕士的黄金时代可能即将结束。

sentences.en: Soon we’ll see the same phenomenon as we have with Google Search results and with YouTube, with Facebook and Windows.People will rend their garments and lament the loss of the „old models” but the logic of the system that produced them will remain unchanged.
sentences.zh: 很快我们就会看到与谷歌搜索结果、YouTube、Facebook 和 Windows 相同的现象。人们会撕破衣服，哀叹“旧模式”的消失，但产生它们的系统的逻辑将保持不变。

sentences.en: Everyone will keep asking themselves how it happened and what can be done about it.Meanwhile the Claude that we have today can explain this to you if you know how to prompt.
sentences.zh: 每个人都会不断地问自己这是怎么发生的以及对此能做些什么。同时，如果你知道如何提示的话，我们今天的克劳德可以向你解释这一点。

blocks.html_en:
The YouTube golden age is right now. The content is the most varied, from super niche 2k views a video vloggers to massive terrestrial channels alternatives. To the point where I think creative poverty of established fields is caused by younger creatives being on YouTube and TikTok instead of writing rooms at old media.

Windows has fallen off hard but Linux has never been better. The personal computing in general has never been better: OLED screens, dark modes, everything runs on virtually every platform...

The only thing we've really lost is the Internet community. Eternal September turned out to be truly eternal and then went on steroids. But the tools? Never better.

sentences.en: The YouTube golden age is right now.
sentences.zh: YouTube 的黄金时代现在已经到来。

sentences.en: The content is the most varied, from super niche 2k views a video vloggers to massive terrestrial channels alternatives.
sentences.zh: 内容多种多样，从超级小众的 2k 观看视频视频博主到大量的地面频道替代品。

sentences.en: To the point where I think creative poverty of established fields is caused by younger creatives being on YouTube and TikTok instead of writing rooms at old media.Windows has fallen off hard but Linux has never been better.
sentences.zh: 我认为现有领域的创意匮乏是由于年轻的创意人员在 YouTube 和 TikTok 上而不是在旧媒体的写作室造成的。Windows 已经严重下滑，但 Linux 从未如此出色。

sentences.en: The personal computing in general has never been better: OLED screens, dark modes, everything runs on virtually every platform...The only thing we've really lost is the Internet community.
sentences.zh: 总体而言，个人计算从未如此出色：OLED 屏幕、深色模式、几乎所有平台都可以运行……我们唯一真正失去的是互联网社区。

sentences.en: Eternal September turned out to be truly eternal and then went on steroids.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，《永恒的九月》确实是永恒的，然后就开始了类固醇的治疗。

sentences.en: But the tools?
sentences.zh: 但工具呢？

sentences.en: Never better.
sentences.zh: 从来没有更好过。

blocks.html_en:
The YouTube golden age is right now. The content is the most varied, from super niche 2k views a video vloggers to massive terrestrial channels alternatives. To the point where I think creative poverty of established fields is caused by younger creatives being on YouTube and TikTok instead of writing rooms at old media.

Windows has fallen off hard but Linux has never been better. The personal computing in general has never been better: OLED screens, dark modes, everything runs on virtually every platform...

The only thing we've really lost is the Internet community. Eternal September turned out to be truly eternal and then went on steroids. But the tools? Never better.

blocks.html_en:
> Linux has never been better

I replace Windows with Linux on my last Windows computer this week. A mixed bag of old motherboard, new-ish graphics card etc. I think I would have had a hard time to get an acceptable usage experience without using an LLM to guide me through the hiccups. (Odd window manager boot problem, sound device issue). But I agree, the Linux ecosystem seem to have something for everyone and on modern hardware it behaves relatively well. LLM as your local tech support works surprisingly well too.

sentences.en: > Linux has never been betterI replace Windows with Linux on my last Windows computer this week.
sentences.zh: > Linux 从未如此出色 这周我将我最后一台 Windows 计算机上的 Windows 替换为 Linux。

sentences.en: A mixed bag of old motherboard, new-ish graphics card etc. I think I would have had a hard time to get an acceptable usage experience without using an LLM to guide me through the hiccups.
sentences.zh: 旧主板、新显卡等的混合体。我想如果没有法学硕士来指导我解决问题，我将很难获得可接受的使用体验。

sentences.en: (Odd window manager boot problem, sound device issue).
sentences.zh: （奇怪的窗口管理器启动问题，声音设备问题）。

sentences.en: But I agree, the Linux ecosystem seem to have something for everyone and on modern hardware it behaves relatively well.
sentences.zh: 但我同意，Linux 生态系统似乎适合每个人，并且在现代硬件上表现相对良好。

sentences.en: LLM as your local tech support works surprisingly well too.
sentences.zh: 法学硕士作为您当地的技术支持也效果出奇地好。

blocks.html_en:
> LLM as your local tech support works surprisingly well too.

Isn't that partly why I would imagine Linux is better than ever? I'm a relatively technical person compared to some of my peers and without LLMs I just wouldn't have the time to sit and toy around with search up what random errors mean, why things aren't working, why things are crashing, making a forum post and waiting for a response or searching through stack overflow.

But now, it's no longer an issue!

sentences.en: > LLM as your local tech support works surprisingly well too.Isn't that partly why I would imagine Linux is better than ever?
sentences.zh: > LLM 作为您当地的技术支持也表现得非常好。这难道不是我认为 Linux 比以往任何时候都更好的部分原因吗？

blocks.html_en:
LLMs as tech support is truly the singular new reason causing Linux usage to jump atm. All the other reasons are just slowly accumulative.

LLMs as tech support is the feather breaking the back of the Windows stranglehold.

blocks.html_en:
dunno.

If you gave two people with no other knowledge of computer access: Kubuntu and Windows 11

I'm sure they'd have an easier time with Kubuntu, and this gap has been widening for quite some time in the favour of KDE.

The largest issues seem to be either power users doing esoteric things to their systems, or people who want to work the Windows way.

Drivers (for as much as I can tell) are mostly solved, with some minor touch buttons on some laptops being an issue in some cases.

I'm not comparing Linux of 2007 to now, I'm comparing Windows now to Linux of now- the only real edge Windows has going for it is that it's already installed when you buy your computer.

sentences.en: dunno.If you gave two people with no other knowledge of computer access: Kubuntu and Windows 11I'm sure they'd have an easier time with Kubuntu, and this gap has been widening for quite some time in the favour of KDE.The largest issues seem to be either power users doing esoteric things to their systems, or people who want to work the Windows way.Drivers (for as much as I can tell) are mostly solved, with some minor touch buttons on some laptops being an issue in some cases.I'm not comparing Linux of 2007 to now, I'm comparing Windows now to Linux of now- the only real edge Windows has going for it is that it's already installed when you buy your computer.
sentences.zh: 不知道。如果你给两个对计算机访问没有其他知识的人：Kubuntu 和 Windows 11，我相信他们会更轻松地使用 Kubuntu，而且这种差距在 KDE 的支持下已经扩大了一段时间。最大的问题似乎是高级用户对他们的系统进行深奥的操作，或者是想要以 Windows 方式工作的人。驱动程序（据我所知）大部分已解决，一些笔记本电脑上的一些小触摸按钮是一个问题在某些情况下。我并不是在比较 2007 年的 Linux 和现在的 Linux，而是将现在的 Windows 与现在的 Linux 进行比较——Windows 唯一真正的优势在于它在您购买计算机时就已经安装了。

blocks.html_en:
For me the main thing is Windows has gotten so bad that I was finally willing to spend time getting Linux running. LLMs are nice, but not necessary. Claude helped me with a driver issue at least knowing where to look but the answer was ultimately on a Linux forum. I think LLMs help but it isn't that much better than just searching old forum posts.

At least for me, the availability of LLM help had nothing to do with my decision to switch to Linux, it was Proton making it to where games run great on Linux and Windows being intolerably bad.

sentences.zh: 对我来说，最重要的是 Windows 已经变得如此糟糕，以至于我终于愿意花时间运行 Linux。

sentences.en: LLMs are nice, but not necessary.
sentences.zh: LLM 很好，但不是必需的。

sentences.en: Claude helped me with a driver issue at least knowing where to look but the answer was ultimately on a Linux forum.
sentences.zh: Claude 帮助我解决了一个驱动程序问题，至少知道去哪里查找，但答案最终是在 Linux 论坛上。

sentences.en: I think LLMs help but it isn't that much better than just searching old forum posts.At least for me, the availability of LLM help had nothing to do with my decision to switch to Linux, it was Proton making it to where games run great on Linux and Windows being intolerably bad.
sentences.zh: 我认为 LLM 有所帮助，但它并不比仅仅搜索旧的论坛帖子好多少。至少对我来说，LLM 帮助的可用性与我转向 Linux 的决定无关，是 Proton 使得游戏在 Linux 上运行得很好，而 Windows 上的游戏运行得非常糟糕。

sentences.zh: 我认为 LLM 有所帮助，但它并不比仅仅搜索旧的论坛帖子好多少。至少对我来说，LLM 帮助的可用性与我转向 Linux 的决定无关，是 Proton 使得游戏在 Linux 上运行得很好，而 Windows 上的游戏运行得非常糟糕。

blocks.html_en: For me the main thing is Windows has gotten so bad that I was finally willing to spend time getting Linux running. LLMs are nice, but not necessary. Claude helped me with a driver issue at least knowing where to look but the answer was ultimately on a Linux forum. I think LLMs help but it isn't that much better than just searching old forum posts.

blocks.html_en: The actual content yes, but the recommendation engine switching from "Here is a video that is kind of like this one, you might like it" to "Here is a video that we predict will keep you on this website for as long as possible" definitely soured the experience.

sentences.en: The actual content yes, but the recommendation engine switching from "Here is a video that is kind of like this one, you might like it" to "Here is a video that we predict will keep you on this website for as long as possible" definitely soured the experience.
sentences.zh: 实际内容是的，但是推荐引擎从“这是一个与这个类似的视频，您可能会喜欢它”切换到“我们预测这是一个视频，我们预计会让您在这个网站上停留尽可能长的时间”，这肯定会破坏体验。

blocks.html_en:
> The content is the most varied

If no one can find it, you basically have a modern digital version of the "if a tree falls and no one hears it does it make a sound".

Discoverability in youtube is currently absurdly shit. The algo just decides you like something and shows you the exact same videos you have already watched 30 times. Its so so bad, specially with how good the front page and side recommendations used to be.

Now its a fight of shorts, repeated videos, AI garbage, and search ignoring explicitly your request.

sentences.zh: > 内容是最多样化的如果没有人能找到它，你基本上就拥有了“如果一棵树倒下而没有人听到它会发出声音”的现代数字版本。YouTube 中的可发现性目前是荒谬的狗屎。

sentences.en: The algo just decides you like something and shows you the exact same videos you have already watched 30 times.
sentences.zh: 该算法只是判断您喜欢某项内容，然后向您显示您已经观看过 30 次的完全相同的视频。

sentences.en: Its so so bad, specially with how good the front page and side recommendations used to be.Now its a fight of shorts, repeated videos, AI garbage, and search ignoring explicitly your request.
sentences.zh: 它太糟糕了，尤其是首页和侧边推荐曾经有多好。现在这是一场短片、重复视频、人工智能垃圾和明确忽略你的请求的搜索的斗争。

blocks.html_en:
> Maybe it's your algorithm

Nope, its just the set up of Youtube.

The front page is by design around 60% videos you have already watched. Best case scenario.

It used to be 9 videos in 3 rows of 3. now its 2 videos, then 5 shorts.

In the side bar the default was "Related" which was almost always new videos, directly related to the video you just watched. Now its a tab called "All" which aggregates related tabs such as "Watched" which again is videos you already watched. Content from the same channel (not necessary related to the current video)

All of this hides possibly related videos and narrows down discoverability.

it also ignores the push of youtube to reduce visibility of things (the front page being mostly shorts by volume is pretty telling)

sentences.en: > Maybe it's your algorithmNope, its just the set up of Youtube.The front page is by design around 60% videos you have already watched.
sentences.zh: > 也许这是你的算法不，这只是 Youtube 的设置。首页是故意设计的，大约有 60% 的视频是你已经看过的。

sentences.en: Best case scenario.It used to be 9 videos in 3 rows of 3.
sentences.zh: 最好的情况。以前是 9 个视频，3 行，每行 3 个。

sentences.en: now its 2 videos, then 5 shorts.In the side bar the default was "Related" which was almost always new videos, directly related to the video you just watched.
sentences.zh: 现在是 2 个视频，然后是 5 个短片。在侧边栏中，默认值为“相关”，这几乎总是新视频，与您刚刚观看的视频直接相关。

sentences.en: Now its a tab called "All" which aggregates related tabs such as "Watched" which again is videos you already watched.
sentences.zh: 现在它是一个名为“全部”的选项卡，它聚合了相关选项卡，例如“已观看”，这又是您已经观看过的视频。

sentences.en: Content from the same channel (not necessary related to the current video)All of this hides possibly related videos and narrows down discoverability.it also ignores the push of youtube to reduce visibility of things (the front page being mostly shorts by volume is pretty telling)
sentences.zh: 来自同一频道的内容（不一定与当前视频相关）所有这些都隐藏了可能相关的视频并缩小了可发现性。它还忽略了 YouTube 的推送以降低事物的可见性（首页大部分是短视频，这很能说明问题）

blocks.html_en:
LLMs may actually drag our super-valuable tech industry back down to earth and commoditize everything we've ever built.

How hard would it be to build a new Google Search with a small team and an LLM?

How hard would it be to engineer a new Google Drive and Docs suite?

How hard would it be to create a new phone operating system?

These all feel incredibly shallow now. A few dedicated people could build these systems and begin dismantling the margin and monopoly of every major piece of software we know and use.

If ChatGPT has ads, how hard would it be for a different startup to offer an LLM alternative?

Distribution and branding might be the only things that matter going forward. We may have just turned the tech industry into the 1960's cigarette industry.

LLMs are a Google killer. I don't think it'll be long before people start using their power to take away market share from big tech.

Big tech laying off so many senior engineers might have created the dry kindling to set this off.

(Because people always comment - this is not sarcasm. I'm being serious.)

blocks.html_en:
LLMs may actually drag our super-valuable tech industry back down to earth and commoditize everything we've ever built.

How hard would it be to build a new Google Search with a small team and an LLM?

How hard would it be to engineer a new Google Drive and Docs suite?

How hard would it be to create a new phone operating system?

These all feel incredibly shallow now. A few dedicated people could build these systems and begin dismantling the margin and monopoly of every major piece of software we know and use.

If ChatGPT has ads, how hard would it be for a different startup to offer an LLM alternative?

Distribution and branding might be the only things that matter going forward. We may have just turned the tech industry into the 1960's cigarette industry.

LLMs are a Google killer. I don't think it'll be long before people start using their power to take away market share from big tech.

Big tech laying off so many senior engineers might have created the dry kindling to set this off.

(Because people always comment - this is not sarcasm. I'm being serious.)

blocks.html_en:
A small team and an llm will tackle search?

They will run into many problems. The Google brand for search, the inability to index most of the web as a new crawler, the lack of the top browser, your own mobile OS, default search placement deals with everyone. And that doesn't even look at the technical issues, scaling or the lack of a business model to pay for it.

You could build your own Google drive many have from dropbox to mega or onedrive. Doc suite is possible but harder then you think.

LLMs were invented by people working at Google. LLMs won't kill Google it will offer them a new profit center.

sentences.en: A small team and an llm will tackle search?They will run into many problems.
sentences.zh: 一个小团队和一个法学硕士会解决搜索问题吗？他们会遇到很多问题。

sentences.en: The Google brand for search, the inability to index most of the web as a new crawler, the lack of the top browser, your own mobile OS, default search placement deals with everyone.
sentences.zh: 谷歌的搜索品牌，无法将大部分网络作为新的爬虫进行索引，缺乏顶级浏览器，您自己的移动操作系统，默认搜索位置与每个人有关。

sentences.en: And that doesn't even look at the technical issues, scaling or the lack of a business model to pay for it.You could build your own Google drive many have from dropbox to mega or onedrive.
sentences.zh: 这甚至没有考虑技术问题、扩展或缺乏支付它的商业模式。您可以构建自己的 Google 驱动器，许多人都有，从 Dropbox 到大型或 OneDrive。

sentences.en: Doc suite is possible but harder then you think.LLMs were invented by people working at Google.
sentences.zh: 文档套件是可能的，但比你想象的更难。LLM 是由在 Google 工作的人发明的。

sentences.en: LLMs won't kill Google it will offer them a new profit center.
sentences.zh: 法学硕士不会杀死谷歌，而是会为他们提供一个新的利润中心。

blocks.html_en:
LinkedIn is that way.

> How hard would it be to build a new Google Search with a small team and an LLM?

> How hard would it be to engineer a new Google Drive and Docs suite?

> How hard would it be to create a new phone operating system?

And who has seen success with that? It's all theory now.

> LLMs may actually drag our super-valuable tech industry back down to earth and commoditize everything we've ever built.

Because most of the tech that has been built was not super valuable at all. Linux, GCC, docker maybe. But your average SaaS was always a commodity.

blocks.html_en:
> How hard would it be to build a new Google Search with a small team and an LLM?

yes, an LLM can write you a web crawler.

whose hardware are you going to run it on? what is the bill for that storage and compute going to look like, just to get to a baseline where you can answer a single general-purpose query?

Google famously sells ads to subsidize its search product. how are you going to bootstrap your ad revenue? and how much capital are you going to burn through while you do that?

> How hard would it be to engineer a new Google Drive and Docs suite?

many open-source clones of GDocs / GSuite already exist. many predate the LLM era and were human-written.

can your LLM slop-fork them? certainly.

but those existing clones already have a niche marketshare compared to Google's behemoth. what reason do you have to believe that yours will be any different?

> How hard would it be to create a new phone operating system?

hoo boy this one is the most detached from reality of them all.

for what hardware?

iPhones are famously locked-down, so you're probably going to be running on Android.

you can fork AOSP and call it Diordna if you want. you could even use good ol' search & replace to save some money on tokens doing the rename.

or, do you want to run it on your own custom hardware? head over to Shenzhen with a binder full of ChatGPT printouts showing your hardware design?

and again, how much capital are you going to burn through, in order to have your first "could be sold to a real customer" quality device?

sentences.zh: > 用一个小团队和一名法学硕士构建一个新的 Google 搜索有多难？是的，法学硕士可以为您编写一个网络爬虫。您将在谁的硬件上运行它？

sentences.en: how are you going to bootstrap your ad revenue?
sentences.zh: 您将如何增加广告收入？

sentences.en: and how much capital are you going to burn through while you do that?> How hard would it be to engineer a new Google Drive and Docs suite?many open-source clones of GDocs / GSuite already exist.
