blocks.html_en:
> How hard would it be to build a new Google Search with a small team and an LLM?

yes, an LLM can write you a web crawler.

whose hardware are you going to run it on? what is the bill for that storage and compute going to look like, just to get to a baseline where you can answer a single general-purpose query?

Google famously sells ads to subsidize its search product. how are you going to bootstrap your ad revenue? and how much capital are you going to burn through while you do that?

> How hard would it be to engineer a new Google Drive and Docs suite?

many open-source clones of GDocs / GSuite already exist. many predate the LLM era and were human-written.

can your LLM slop-fork them? certainly.

but those existing clones already have a niche marketshare compared to Google's behemoth. what reason do you have to believe that yours will be any different?

> How hard would it be to create a new phone operating system?

hoo boy this one is the most detached from reality of them all.

for what hardware?

iPhones are famously locked-down, so you're probably going to be running on Android.

you can fork AOSP and call it Diordna if you want. you could even use good ol' search & replace to save some money on tokens doing the rename.

or, do you want to run it on your own custom hardware? head over to Shenzhen with a binder full of ChatGPT printouts showing your hardware design?

and again, how much capital are you going to burn through, in order to have your first "could be sold to a real customer" quality device?

blocks.html_en:
> How hard would it be to build a new Google Search with a small team and an LLM?

Extremely hard, because it would need a modest capital investment which won’t happen.

Every subsequent example you give is harder.

I've been thinking along these lines for a while.

It isn't just about building an alternate cheaply, it is about convincing people to switch. I wouldn't discourage anyone from trying, just that building the thing is just the beginning of a long journey.

blocks.html_en:
Building the indexer is the easy part.

The hard part is indexing the vast seas of the web. You need a large pile of cash to do that. One of those cases where running the software is harder than making it.

And once you've spent your large pile of cash, you're probably gonna want to earn it back somehow. Your competition doesn't charge for search, and your whole raison d'etre is to not serve ads.

Good luck!

sentences.en: You need a large pile of cash to do that.
sentences.zh: 你需要大量现金才能做到这一点。

sentences.en: One of those cases where running the software is harder than making it.And once you've spent your large pile of cash, you're probably gonna want to earn it back somehow.
sentences.zh: 其中一种情况是，运行软件比制作软件更难。一旦你花掉了一大笔现金，你可能会想以某种方式把它赚回来。

sentences.en: Your competition doesn't charge for search, and your whole raison d'etre is to not serve ads.Good luck!
sentences.zh: 您的竞争对手不收取搜索费用，您存在的全部理由就是不投放广告。祝您好运！

sentences.en: One of those cases where running the software is harder than making it.
sentences.zh: 其中一种情况是运行软件比制作软件更难。

blocks.html_en: And once you've spent your large pile of cash, you're probably gonna want to earn it back somehow. Your competition doesn't charge for search, and your whole raison d'etre is to not serve ads.

sentences.en: And once you've spent your large pile of cash, you're probably gonna want to earn it back somehow.
sentences.zh: 一旦你花掉了一大笔现金，你可能会想以某种方式把它赚回来。

sentences.en: Your competition doesn't charge for search, and your whole raison d'etre is to not serve ads.
sentences.zh: 您的竞争对手不收取搜索费用，您存在的全部理由就是不投放广告。

blocks.html_en: Good luck!

sentences.en: Good luck!
sentences.zh: 祝你好运！

blocks.html_en:
Yeah, that would be a problem. Luckily, the actual post we're discussing isn't putting any advertising in the model.

This is a very important distinction to make -- complaints should be be about what is happening, and should distinguish that from what is not yet happening and should not. Because beyond the maxim that we should try to deal in facts... if OpenAI thinks people can't tell the difference anyways, they'll be justified in thinking they may as well just do the thing they're already being accused of doing.

blocks.html_en:
> complaints should be be about what is happening

Why would you think that? An ad supported product has misaligned incentives, everyone can see that and that's why the confidence erodes from that moment on.

You're living in the era in which saying "Stallman was right" is a dead cold take. Every big tech company sold out exactly like the hippies told you they would back when Google was a dream place to work for and Meta was still a twinkle in zuk's eyes.

sentences.en: > complaints should be be about what is happeningWhy would you think that?
sentences.zh: > 抱怨应该是关于正在发生的事情 你为什么会这样想？

sentences.en: An ad supported product has misaligned incentives, everyone can see that and that's why the confidence erodes from that moment on.You're living in the era in which saying "Stallman was right" is a dead cold take.
sentences.zh: 广告支持的产品会产生不协调的激励，每个人都可以看到这一点，这就是为什么从那一刻起信心就被削弱了。在你生活的时代，说“斯托曼是对的”是一种冷漠的态度。

sentences.en: Every big tech company sold out exactly like the hippies told you they would back when Google was a dream place to work for and Meta was still a twinkle in zuk's eyes.
sentences.zh: 每家大型科技公司都卖光了，就像嬉皮士告诉你的那样，当谷歌是理想的工作场所，而 Meta 仍然是祖克眼中的闪光时，他们就会回来。

blocks.html_en:
God forbid we anticipate what could possibly go wrong in an attempt to get ahead of the problem.

But I guess waiting for problems to show their ugly heads before acting has never backfired.

sentences.zh: 但愿我们不会为了解决问题而预见到可能出现的问题。但我想，等待问题显露出来后再采取行动，绝不会适得其反。

blocks.html_en:
Look at how ads in Google SERP were before 2014 (and they already crossed "we won't put ads" line a few years before)

https://www.bmon.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2014/03/deep-hole-...

Now, well, if you do the same exact search, at least in my location (and with UBlock disabled) it's frankly a mess but must admit that sponsored links are not even the first thing you see. There is the AI result, then the Wikipedia result, then some random result, then the sponsored block with a flat headline separating it and then the organic results, I presume.

sentences.en: Look at how ads in Google SERP were before 2014 (and they already crossed "we won't put ads" line a few years before)https://www.bmon.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2014/03/deep-hole-...Now, well, if you do the same exact search, at least in my location (and with UBlock disabled) it's frankly a mess but must admit that sponsored links are not even the first thing you see.
sentences.zh: 看看 Google SERP 中的广告在 2014 年之前的情况（几年前它们就已经跨越了“我们不会投放广告”的界限）https://www.bmon.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2014/03/deep-hole-…现在，好吧，如果你进行同样的搜索，至少在我的位置（并且禁用了 UBlock），坦率地说，这是一团糟，但必须承认，赞助商链接甚至不是你看到的第一件事。

sentences.en: There is the AI result, then the Wikipedia result, then some random result, then the sponsored block with a flat headline separating it and then the organic results, I presume.
sentences.zh: 我认为，先是人工智能结果，然后是维基百科结果，然后是一些随机结果，然后是用扁平标题将其分隔开的赞助块，然后是有机结果。

blocks.html_en:
"ads will no longer look like ads" (in the future). Ads are clearly marked in the first step, but will likely be more and more blended over time (or worse, biasing the model and therefore the output itself). That's the logical future, because that's better for the advertiser, which is the paying customer.

That already happened on Google search: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49003372

blocks.html_en:
Those ads are supposed to be "Clearly labeled" and "Separate from answers".

Now, this is the sort of rock-solid commitment to trust and safety that steadily gets worse every year until you go from old Netflix to Poob with ads. But the water's still fine. As a frog, let me tell you, it doesn't feel hot at all.

sentences.en: Those ads are supposed to be "Clearly labeled" and "Separate from answers".Now, this is the sort of rock-solid commitment to trust and safety that steadily gets worse every year until you go from old Netflix to Poob with ads.
sentences.zh: 这些广告应该是“清楚地标记”和“与答案分开”。现在，这是一种对信任和安全坚如磐石的承诺，这种承诺每年都在稳步恶化，直到你从老式的 Netflix 变成带有广告的 Poob。

sentences.en: But the water's still fine.
sentences.zh: 不过水还是可以的

sentences.en: As a frog, let me tell you, it doesn't feel hot at all.
sentences.zh: 作为一只青蛙，我告诉你，一点也不热。

blocks.html_en:
One thing that was an eye-opener (to me) is just HOW MANY people that I'd put quite high on the intelligence/worldy knowledge graph had ZERO inclination or propensity to even notice or CARE about the bright red AD box around something on a search result.

Made me realize that advertising will always rank somewhere below prostitution (at least there you ARE paying to get screwed).

sentences.en: One thing that was an eye-opener (to me) is just HOW MANY people that I'd put quite high on the intelligence/worldy knowledge graph had ZERO inclination or propensity to even notice or CARE about the bright red AD box around something on a search result.Made me realize that advertising will always rank somewhere below prostitution (at least there you ARE paying to get screwed).
sentences.zh: 让我大开眼界的一件事（对我来说）是，有多少我在智力/世俗知识图谱上排名很高的人甚至没有注意到或关心搜索结果上某些内容周围的鲜红色广告框的倾向或倾向。让我意识到广告的排名总是低于卖淫（至少在那里你付钱被骗）。

blocks.html_en: Yeah, I don't mind these ads, just as I didn't mind ads on Google when they first came out. But once you have ads, there's huge monetary pressure to make them more effective over time, which inevitably ends in ads that pretend to be organic content.

sentences.en: Yeah, I don't mind these ads, just as I didn't mind ads on Google when they first came out.
sentences.zh: 是的，我不介意这些广告，就像我不介意谷歌上的广告刚出现时一样。

sentences.en: But once you have ads, there's huge monetary pressure to make them more effective over time, which inevitably ends in ads that pretend to be organic content.
sentences.zh: 但一旦有了广告，随着时间的推移，就会面临巨大的资金压力，要求它们变得更有效，这不可避免地会导致广告假装是有机内容。

blocks.html_en:
I guess for a while we'll have the "premier cable channels" effect. You pay not to get ads.

How will you be sure the results from the LLM still aren't injecting ads when you pay? Find a trusted provider who is serving an open weights model.

Although I guess in the near future, we'll have GLM-7.x updates that claim all kinds of performance improvements, but the only real motivator to release model updates is to continually retrain them to push the latest products.

sentences.en: I guess for a while we'll have the "premier cable channels" effect.
sentences.zh: 我想在一段时间内我们会产生“顶级有线频道”效应。

sentences.en: You pay not to get ads.How will you be sure the results from the LLM still aren't injecting ads when you pay?
sentences.zh: 您付费不是为了获得广告。您如何确定当您付费时 LLM 的结果仍然没有注入广告？

sentences.en: Find a trusted provider who is serving an open weights model.Although I guess in the near future, we'll have GLM-7.x updates that claim all kinds of performance improvements, but the only real motivator to release model updates is to continually retrain them to push the latest products.
sentences.zh: 找到一个提供开放权重模型服务的值得信赖的提供商。虽然我猜在不久的将来，我们将会有 GLM-7.x 更新，声称会带来各种性能改进，但发布模型更新的唯一真正动机是不断地重新培训它们以推送最新产品。

blocks.html_en: The internet exists because people wanted to connect their machines, before ads. It would still exist without the ads. Smaller maybe, with less content, and less business opportunity for investors. But the internet would survive without ads, because of how useful it is to connect globally.

sentences.en: It would still exist without the ads.

sentences.en: Smaller maybe, with less content, and less business opportunity for investors.

sentences.en: But the internet would survive without ads, because of how useful it is to connect globally.
sentences.zh: 但互联网在没有广告的情况下也能生存，因为它在全球范围内连接起来非常有用。

blocks.html_en:
> better business model than whatever anthropic is doing.

going after business is a more secure long term recurring revenue source. Its boring but it works. It just doesnt justify trillions of dollars in investment

> ads is the only acceptable way to pay for content by majority.

Lmao look at the revenue per user of any ad platform, now look at the stock price of AI companies.

If you think that they can only rely on ads you should short them with every cent you have. Because they are worth 1/100 of their current valuation.

And I dont even disagree with you, but ads in LLMs are a nail in the coffin to the "replace 16% of the entire US workforce" which is their current valuation.

Another ad delivery platform will make them as valuable as facebook, not as valuable as 1/6 of the world economy

sentences.en: > better business model than whatever anthropic is doing.going after business is a more secure long term recurring revenue source.
sentences.zh: > 比人类所做的任何事情都更好的商业模式。追求业务是更安全的长期经常性收入来源。

sentences.en: Its boring but it works.
sentences.zh: 这很无聊，但很有效。

sentences.en: It just doesnt justify trillions of dollars in investment> ads is the only acceptable way to pay for content by majority.Lmao look at the revenue per user of any ad platform, now look at the stock price of AI companies.If you think that they can only rely on ads you should short them with every cent you have.
sentences.zh: 这并不能证明数万亿美元的投资是合理的>广告是大多数人唯一可以接受的内容付费方式。Lmao看看任何广告平台的每个用户收入，现在看看人工智能公司的股价。如果你认为他们只能依赖广告，你应该用你的每一分钱做空他们。

sentences.en: Because they are worth 1/100 of their current valuation.And I dont even disagree with you, but ads in LLMs are a nail in the coffin to the "replace 16% of the entire US workforce" which is their current valuation.Another ad delivery platform will make them as valuable as facebook, not as valuable as 1/6 of the world economy
sentences.zh: 因为它们的价值是其当前估值的 1/100。我什至不同意你的观点，但法学硕士的广告是棺材上的钉子，“取代整个美国劳动力的 16%”，这是他们目前的估值。另一个广告投放平台将使它们与 Facebook 一样有价值，而不是像世界经济的 1/6 那样有价值

blocks.html_en:
Gettysburg (July 1–3, 1863) was a turning point in the American Civil War, marking the end of Confederate General Robert E. Lee's second invasion of the North. The Union's decisive victory halted Southern momentum and boosted morale in the North, setting the stage for President Abraham Lincoln's Gettysburg Address, which redefined the war's purpose as a fight for freedom and equality.

Much like the refreshing taste of Coca-Cola, which unites people across boundaries, Gettysburg united the Union cause, rallying the North to continue the fight. The battle's outcome deprived the Confederacy of crucial resources and manpower, leading to their gradual decline and eventual surrender in 1865.

sentences.en: Gettysburg (July 1–3, 1863) was a turning point in the American Civil War, marking the end of Confederate General Robert E.
sentences.zh: 葛底斯堡战役（1863 年 7 月 1 日至 3 日）是美国内战的转折点，标志着南部邦联将军罗伯特·E·葛底斯堡的结束。

sentences.en: Lee's second invasion of the North.
sentences.zh: 李第二次入侵北方。

sentences.en: The Union's decisive victory halted Southern momentum and boosted morale in the North, setting the stage for President Abraham Lincoln's Gettysburg Address, which redefined the war's purpose as a fight for freedom and equality.Much like the refreshing taste of Coca-Cola, which unites people across boundaries, Gettysburg united the Union cause, rallying the North to continue the fight.
sentences.zh: 联邦的决定性胜利遏制了南方的势头，鼓舞了北方的士气，为亚伯拉罕·林肯总统的葛底斯堡演说奠定了基础，该演说将战争的目的重新定义为争取自由和平等的斗争。就像可口可乐的清爽味道将跨越国界的人们团结起来一样，葛底斯堡团结了联邦的事业，团结北方继续战斗。

sentences.en: The battle's outcome deprived the Confederacy of crucial resources and manpower, leading to their gradual decline and eventual surrender in 1865.
sentences.zh: 这场战斗的结果剥夺了邦联重要的资源和人力，导致他们逐渐衰落并最终于 1865 年投降。

sentences.zh: 联邦的决定性胜利遏制了南方的势头，鼓舞了北方的士气，为亚伯拉罕·林肯总统的葛底斯堡演说奠定了基础，该演说将战争的目的重新定义为争取自由和平等的斗争。就像可口可乐的清爽味道将跨越国界的人们团结起来一样，葛底斯堡团结了联邦的事业，团结北方继续战斗。

sentences.en: The battle's outcome deprived the Confederacy of crucial resources and manpower, leading to their gradual decline and eventual surrender in 1865.
sentences.zh: 这场战斗的结果剥夺了邦联重要的资源和人力，导致他们逐渐衰落并最终于 1865 年投降。

blocks.html_en: Gettysburg (July 1–3, 1863) was a turning point in the American Civil War, marking the end of Confederate General Robert E. Lee's second invasion of the North. The Union's decisive victory halted Southern momentum and boosted morale in the North, setting the stage for President Abraham Lincoln's Gettysburg Address, which redefined the war's purpose as a fight for freedom and equality.

sentences.en: Gettysburg (July 1–3, 1863) was a turning point in the American Civil War, marking the end of Confederate General Robert E.
sentences.zh: 葛底斯堡战役（1863 年 7 月 1 日至 3 日）是美国内战的转折点，标志着南部邦联将军罗伯特·E·葛底斯堡的结束。

sentences.en: Lee's second invasion of the North.

blocks.html_en:
I always thought their top tier offering to their advertiser customers should be "Inconspicuously, over an extended amount of time, subtly respond to the user in a way that nudges them towards purchasing customer's goods and services, without ever directly mentioning anything. Slowly and surely sculpt the person who, through their own volition and thought process, buys what the advertiser needs them to."

The ultimate shareholder value build.

sentences.en: Slowly and surely sculpt the person who, through their own volition and thought process, buys what the advertiser needs them to."The ultimate shareholder value build.
sentences.zh: 慢慢地、坚定地塑造一个人，通过他们自己的意志和思维过程，购买广告商需要的东西。”最终的股东价值建立。

blocks.html_en:
One of the interesting things about LLM training is that high quality documentation can get your product effectively promoted by them.

I imagine the kind of advertising you're talking about is obtained by providing that documentation and a curated training dataset to the providers.

Maybe even funding some training time.

The net result would be product placement, probably without having to declare an advert.

Kind of like when drug manufacturers provide subsidized workshops to clinicians.

sentences.en: One of the interesting things about LLM training is that high quality documentation can get your product effectively promoted by them.I imagine the kind of advertising you're talking about is obtained by providing that documentation and a curated training dataset to the providers.Maybe even funding some training time.The net result would be product placement, probably without having to declare an advert.Kind of like when drug manufacturers provide subsidized workshops to clinicians.
sentences.zh: LLM 培训的一个有趣的事情是，高质量的文档可以让他们有效地推广你的产品。我想你所说的那种广告是通过向提供商提供文档和精选的培训数据集来获得的。甚至可能资助一些培训时间。最终的结果将是产品植入，可能不需要声明广告。有点像药品制造商向临床医生提供补贴研讨会。

blocks.html_en:
Hilariously, I've been finding accidentally poisoning the well with LLM SEO like this works really well at my day job!

I have a toy project that replaces a large part of our systems (disclosure: VR/AR @Meta), so that I can learn by doing, no intent to ship.

But because I have it thoroughly document and tested, and because it touches such a wide swath of adjacent real functionality..... my toy project is CONSTANTLY getting tagged as a dependency and assigning me reviewer on unrelated work. Because when my coworkers yolo Claude at a task, and Claude searches the codebase, it generally finds my crap very well documented. :)

sentences.en: Hilariously, I've been finding accidentally poisoning the well with LLM SEO like this works really well at my day job!I have a toy project that replaces a large part of our systems (disclosure: VR/AR @Meta), so that I can learn by doing, no intent to ship.But because I have it thoroughly document and tested, and because it touches such a wide swath of adjacent real functionality.....
sentences.zh: 有趣的是，我发现LLM SEO不小心中毒了，就像这在我的日常工作中效果非常好！我有一个玩具项目，它取代了我们系统的很大一部分（披露：VR / AR @Meta），这样我就可以边做边学，无意交付。但是因为我已经彻底记录和测试了它，并且因为它涉及了如此广泛的相邻实际功能......

sentences.en: my toy project is CONSTANTLY getting tagged as a dependency and assigning me reviewer on unrelated work.
sentences.zh: 我的玩具项目不断被标记为依赖项，并为我分配不相关工作的审阅者。

sentences.en: Because when my coworkers yolo Claude at a task, and Claude searches the codebase, it generally finds my crap very well documented. :)
sentences.zh: 因为当我的同事 yolo Claude 执行一项任务，并且 Claude 搜索代码库时，通常会发现我的废话有很好的文档记录。 :)

blocks.html_en:
Unlike all the replies to you, this is why I'm very optimistic about the future of hobby blogs. I do a lot of ChatGPT-ing for several different hobbies, and I've found a lot of really interesting personal blogs from that. Way more than I was reading in the pre-AI era.

I'm very much not seeing the problems other people are assuming, like tons of LLM spam on Reddit being treated as fact by ChatGPT. Instead it really does seem, the more I use these tools, the better they are at surfacing real experiences written about by real people.

sentences.en: Unlike all the replies to you, this is why I'm very optimistic about the future of hobby blogs.
sentences.zh: 与您的所有回复不同，这就是为什么我对爱好博客的未来非常乐观。

sentences.en: I do a lot of ChatGPT-ing for several different hobbies, and I've found a lot of really interesting personal blogs from that.
sentences.zh: 我出于多种不同的爱好进行了大量的 ChatGPT-ing，并且从中发现了很多非常有趣的个人博客。

sentences.en: Way more than I was reading in the pre-AI era.I'm very much not seeing the problems other people are assuming, like tons of LLM spam on Reddit being treated as fact by ChatGPT.
sentences.zh: 比我在前人工智能时代读到的要多得多。我非常没有看到其他人假设的问题，比如 Reddit 上大量的 LLM 垃圾邮件被 ChatGPT 视为事实。

sentences.en: Instead it really does seem, the more I use these tools, the better they are at surfacing real experiences written about by real people.
sentences.zh: 相反，我使用这些工具越多，它们就越能更好地呈现真实的人们所写的真实经历。

blocks.html_en: Loctite already inadvertently does this. If you ask LLMs about threadlocker, or otherwise keeping screws in place, it will respond like a Loctite brochure to you, and usually never even mention competitors. They were ahead of the game!

sentences.en: Loctite already inadvertently does this.
sentences.zh: 乐泰已经在不经意间做到了这一点。

sentences.en: If you ask LLMs about threadlocker, or otherwise keeping screws in place, it will respond like a Loctite brochure to you, and usually never even mention competitors.
sentences.zh: 如果您向法学硕士询问有关螺纹锁固剂或以其他方式将螺钉固定到位的信息，他们会像乐泰手册一样回复您，而且通常甚至不会提及竞争对手。

sentences.en: They were ahead of the game!
sentences.zh: 他们在比赛中领先了！

blocks.html_en:
Some of that may be because the common vernacular uses the word "Loctite" to describe any manner of threadlocker.

It is this way even though Loctite also produces a vast array of products that have nothing to do with theadlocker, and threadlockers are available from a wide variety of manufacturers.

sentences.en: Some of that may be because the common vernacular uses the word "Loctite" to describe any manner of threadlocker.It is this way even though Loctite also produces a vast array of products that have nothing to do with theadlocker, and threadlockers are available from a wide variety of manufacturers.
sentences.zh: 其中一些可能是因为普通白话使用“Loctite”一词来描述任何形式的螺纹锁固剂。即使乐泰也生产大量与螺纹锁固剂无关的产品，并且螺纹锁固剂可以从各种制造商处获得，但情况就是这样。

blocks.html_en:
I'm reminded of the mountains of blogspam not intended for human consumption but rather to influence the google page rank algorithm.

This will go about as well as that did unless the models can be smart enough to identify when their training data is trying to coach them into being misleading at prompt time.

sentences.en: I'm reminded of the mountains of blogspam not intended for human consumption but rather to influence the google page rank algorithm.This will go about as well as that did unless the models can be smart enough to identify when their training data is trying to coach them into being misleading at prompt time.
sentences.zh: 我想起大量的博客垃圾邮件并不是为了人类消费，而是为了影响谷歌页面排名算法。除非模型足够聪明，能够识别出他们的训练数据何时试图指导他们在短时间内产生误导，否则这种情况将会发生。

blocks.html_en:
LLMs are good at many things, but subtlety (and ability to slowly do anything consistently over time) is not remotely one of them. I don't know why people keep bringing this idea up - if they were prompted to do something, you would notice. Don't you remember how noticeable it was when chatgpt gave follow up questions for engagement on EVERY damn response? It's got the finesse of a bulldozer.

I encourage people thinking about such things to try and build prompts to emulate this behavior and see how well it does.

sentences.en: LLMs are good at many things, but subtlety (and ability to slowly do anything consistently over time) is not remotely one of them.
sentences.zh: 法学硕士擅长很多事情，但微妙性（以及随着时间的推移缓慢一致地做任何事情的能力）远不是其中之一。

sentences.en: I don't know why people keep bringing this idea up - if they were prompted to do something, you would notice.
sentences.zh: 我不知道为什么人们不断提出这个想法——如果他们被提示去做某事，你就会注意到。

sentences.en: Don't you remember how noticeable it was when chatgpt gave follow up questions for engagement on EVERY damn response?
sentences.zh: 你不记得当c​​hatgpt对每一个该死的回复都提出参与度的后续问题时，这是多么引人注目吗？

sentences.en: It's got the finesse of a bulldozer.I encourage people thinking about such things to try and build prompts to emulate this behavior and see how well it does.
sentences.zh: 它具有推土机的技巧。我鼓励人们思考这些事情，尝试建立提示来模仿这种行为，看看效果如何。

blocks.html_en:
In every single case you've mentioned you're going to have a campaign with an attempt at attribution. Certainly youtube sponsors all use direct attribution with "use my code" etc.

In every one of those cases you can at least do things like count viewers. How would that work for a model that's just subtly biased?

sentences.en: In every single case you've mentioned you're going to have a campaign with an attempt at attribution.
sentences.zh: 在您提到的每一个案例中，您都将开展一场尝试归因的活动。

sentences.en: Certainly youtube sponsors all use direct attribution with "use my code" etc.In every one of those cases you can at least do things like count viewers.
sentences.zh: 当然，YouTube 赞助商都使用“使用我的代码”等直接归因。在每种情况下，您至少可以做一些事情，例如计算观看者数量。

sentences.en: How would that work for a model that's just subtly biased?
sentences.zh: 对于一个有微妙偏见的模型来说，这会如何运作？

blocks.html_en: Things will get there for sure, with ads and other propaganda baked in at the training level with no reliable way to discern the manipulations as an end user. Modern tech companies will sacrifice anything UX related to generate more effective ads and increase ad revenue. The ability to skew the output of a popular model in subtle ways will be an incredibly valuable product to sell. There's just no way companies like Google and OpenAI are going to leave all that money out there for the taking.

sentences.en: Things will get there for sure, with ads and other propaganda baked in at the training level with no reliable way to discern the manipulations as an end user.
sentences.zh: 事情肯定会实现这一点，因为广告和其他宣传是在培训级别进行的，没有可靠的方法来识别最终用户的操纵行为。

sentences.en: Modern tech companies will sacrifice anything UX related to generate more effective ads and increase ad revenue.
sentences.zh: 现代科技公司将牺牲任何与用户体验相关的东西来生成更有效的广告并增加广告收入。

sentences.en: The ability to skew the output of a popular model in subtle ways will be an incredibly valuable product to sell.
sentences.zh: 以微妙的方式扭曲流行模型的输出的能力将是一种非常有价值的销售产品。

sentences.en: There's just no way companies like Google and OpenAI are going to leave all that money out there for the taking.
sentences.zh: 像谷歌和 OpenAI 这样的公司绝对不会把所有的钱都留在那里供人拿走。

blocks.html_en: People outside the ad world dont get the human attention pool is massively over fished. Its not just one corp trying to condition you but thousands. Not just via one channel but thro hundreds (sports/news/gaming/streaming/social/movies/tv/radio etc). Basically in such an environment what works at population scale is either spending more than everypne else (see the odessey marketing budget), constantly repeating your message in ever shortening windows, and going viral(which involves doing more and more extreme stuff to capture and keep attention). Openai has now entered that overcrowded environment.

sentences.en: People outside the ad world dont get the human attention pool is massively over fished.
sentences.zh: 广告界之外的人并没有意识到人类的注意力已经被过度利用。

sentences.en: Its not just one corp trying to condition you but thousands.
sentences.zh: 不仅仅是一家公司试图调节你，而是成千上万的公司。

sentences.en: Not just via one channel but thro hundreds (sports/news/gaming/streaming/social/movies/tv/radio etc).
sentences.zh: 不仅通过一个频道，而且通过数百个频道（体育/新闻/游戏/流媒体/社交/电影/电视/广播等）。

sentences.en: Basically in such an environment what works at population scale is either spending more than everypne else (see the odessey marketing budget), constantly repeating your message in ever shortening windows, and going viral(which involves doing more and more extreme stuff to capture and keep attention).
sentences.zh: 基本上，在这样的环境中，在人口规模上起作用的方法要么比其他人花费更多（参见奥德赛营销预算），在不断缩短的窗口中不断重复您的信息，然后进行病毒式传播（这涉及做越来越多的极端事情来吸引和保持注意力）。

sentences.en: Openai has now entered that overcrowded environment.
sentences.zh: Openai 现在已经进入了那个人满为患的环境。

blocks.html_en:
Yeah but OpenAI has the benefit of people actively going there and willingly giving quite a lot of their attention.

It's a lot like Google in that respect. And the suggestion is pretty much the same as suggesting that Google offer a product where they boost your website or product or whatever in organic search results, instead of or in addition to displaying it as a sponsored link or ad or whatever.

It's interesting that, despite all the tracking and stuff, they never went quite so far as to actually sell a premium advertising tier in which they would actually change their search results ranking to suit their customer, at least not as far as anyone knows. It probably would've been quite a big scandal in the 00s if they did it back then.

Could OpenAI pull it off? I don't know enough about the advertising industry to answer, but it's interesting to compare them with Google and to think about why Google themselves might not have done it that way.

sentences.en: Yeah but OpenAI has the benefit of people actively going there and willingly giving quite a lot of their attention.It's a lot like Google in that respect.
sentences.zh: 是的，但是 OpenAI 的好处是人们积极地去那里并愿意给予相当多的关注。在这方面它很像谷歌。

sentences.en: And the suggestion is pretty much the same as suggesting that Google offer a product where they boost your website or product or whatever in organic search results, instead of or in addition to displaying it as a sponsored link or ad or whatever.It's interesting that, despite all the tracking and stuff, they never went quite so far as to actually sell a premium advertising tier in which they would actually change their search results ranking to suit their customer, at least not as far as anyone knows.
sentences.zh: 这个建议几乎与建议谷歌提供一种产品一样，他们可以在有机搜索结果中提升您的网站或产品或其他任何内容，而不是或除了将其显示为赞助链接或广告或其他任何内容之外。有趣的是，尽管进行了所有跟踪和其他内容，但他们从未真正销售高级广告层，在该层中他们实际上会改变搜索结果排名以适应他们的客户，至少据任何人所知。

sentences.en: It probably would've been quite a big scandal in the 00s if they did it back then.Could OpenAI pull it off?
sentences.zh: 如果他们当时这么做的话，在 00 年代可能会是一个相当大的丑闻。OpenAI 能成功吗？

sentences.en: I don't know enough about the advertising industry to answer, but it's interesting to compare them with Google and to think about why Google themselves might not have done it that way.
sentences.zh: 我对广告行业了解不够，无法回答，但将它们与谷歌进行比较并思考为什么谷歌本身可能没有这样做是很有趣的。

blocks.html_en: There is no need for Premium because who ever wants more reach spends more. Look at online political spending every election cycle. The eyeballs available is not growing much election to election, but ad spend keeps jumping. So they just sit back and milk people who want attention desperatly, and inject more ads, more frequently everywhere on viewer side. Ideally there needs to be a system wide signal that if people have 20 mins of attention to spare a day and it is crossed there is an auto shutdown of all advertising. In the past on TV there were limits on how many ads can be shown, for how long per hour. Today there is no such limit. So corps ad budgets keep rising and consumer attention spans keep shrinking. Platform print cash. Its like how banks worked before central banks took over setting interest rates.

sentences.en: There is no need for Premium because who ever wants more reach spends more.
sentences.zh: 不需要高级版，因为谁想要更大的覆盖范围，就花更多的钱。

sentences.en: Look at online political spending every election cycle.
sentences.zh: 看看每个选举周期的在线政治支出。

sentences.en: The eyeballs available is not growing much election to election, but ad spend keeps jumping.
sentences.zh: 一次又一次的选举中，可用的眼球并没有增加太多，但广告支出却不断增加。

sentences.en: So they just sit back and milk people who want attention desperatly, and inject more ads, more frequently everywhere on viewer side.
sentences.zh: 因此，他们只是袖手旁观，榨取那些迫切需要关注的人，并在观众身边更频繁地注入更多广告。

sentences.en: Ideally there needs to be a system wide signal that if people have 20 mins of attention to spare a day and it is crossed there is an auto shutdown of all advertising.
sentences.zh: 理想情况下，需要有一个系统范围的信号，如果人们一天有 20 分钟的注意力，并且超过了这个时间，所有广告都会自动关闭。

sentences.en: In the past on TV there were limits on how many ads can be shown, for how long per hour.
sentences.zh: 过去，电视上对广告的播放数量、每小时播放的时间有限制。

sentences.en: Today there is no such limit.
sentences.zh: 今天没有这样的限制。

sentences.en: So corps ad budgets keep rising and consumer attention spans keep shrinking.
sentences.zh: 因此，企业的广告预算不断增加，而消费者的注意力持续缩小。

sentences.en: Platform print cash.
sentences.zh: 平台打印现金。

sentences.en: Its like how banks worked before central banks took over setting interest rates.
sentences.zh: 这就像央行接管利率制定之前银行的运作方式一样。

sentences.zh: 一次又一次的选举中，可用的眼球并没有增加太多，但广告支出却不断增加。

sentences.en: So they just sit back and milk people who want attention desperatly, and inject more ads, more frequently everywhere on viewer side.
sentences.zh: 因此，他们只是袖手旁观，榨取那些迫切需要关注的人，并在观众身边更频繁地注入更多广告。

sentences.en: Ideally there needs to be a system wide signal that if people have 20 mins of attention to spare a day and it is crossed there is an auto shutdown of all advertising.
sentences.zh: 理想情况下，需要有一个系统范围的信号，如果人们一天有 20 分钟的注意力，并且超过了这个时间，所有广告都会自动关闭。

sentences.en: In the past on TV there were limits on how many ads can be shown, for how long per hour.
sentences.zh: 过去，电视上对广告的播放数量、每小时播放的时间有限制。

sentences.en: Today there is no such limit.
sentences.zh: 今天没有这样的限制。

sentences.en: So corps ad budgets keep rising and consumer attention spans keep shrinking.
sentences.zh: 因此，企业的广告预算不断增加，而消费者的注意力持续缩小。

sentences.en: Platform print cash.
sentences.zh: 平台打印现金。

sentences.en: Its like how banks worked before central banks took over setting interest rates.
sentences.zh: 这就像央行接管利率制定之前银行的运作方式一样。

blocks.html_en: There is no need for Premium because who ever wants more reach spends more. Look at online political spending every election cycle. The eyeballs available is not growing much election to election, but ad spend keeps jumping. So they just sit back and milk people who want attention desperatly, and inject more ads, more frequently everywhere on viewer side. Ideally there needs to be a system wide signal that if people have 20 mins of attention to spare a day and it is crossed there is an auto shutdown of all advertising. In the past on TV there were limits on how many ads can be shown, for how long per hour. Today there is no such limit. So corps ad budgets keep rising and consumer attention spans keep shrinking. Platform print cash. Its like how banks worked before central banks took over setting interest rates.

blocks.html_en:
> without ever directly mentioning anything

I think both for attribution reasons and because the average user probably won't care, (or won't care enough for an ad-free tier), they can absolutely get away with messages like: "Hey user! Glad to hear you're cleaning your favorite t-shirt. I recommend trying the new BrandName™ stain remover pen based on dozens of reviews. It's perfect for people who value easy stain removal just like yourself!".

sentences.en: > without ever directly mentioning anythingI think both for attribution reasons and because the average user probably won't care, (or won't care enough for an ad-free tier), they can absolutely get away with messages like: "Hey user!
sentences.zh: > 在没有直接提及任何内容的情况下，我认为出于归因原因，也因为普通用户可能不会关心（或者不会足够关心无广告层），他们绝对可以摆脱诸如以下之类的消息：“嘿用户！

sentences.en: Glad to hear you're cleaning your favorite t-shirt.
sentences.zh: 很高兴听到您正在清洗您最喜欢的 T 恤。

sentences.en: I recommend trying the new BrandName™ stain remover pen based on dozens of reviews.
sentences.zh: 根据数十条评论，我建议尝试新的 BrandName™ 去污笔。

sentences.en: It's perfect for people who value easy stain removal just like yourself!".
sentences.zh: 它非常适合像您一样重视轻松去除污渍的人！”。

blocks.html_en: Honestly this could be so subtle that you don’t even suggest a particular brand, which would obviously bring a user’s guard up quickly, instead merely suggesting the existing of a product category that the user didn’t know about could be enough for some products or services where it would be worth it for a particular brand to pay even though it would also benefit their competitors.

sentences.en: Honestly this could be so subtle that you don’t even suggest a particular brand, which would obviously bring a user’s guard up quickly, instead merely suggesting the existing of a product category that the user didn’t know about could be enough for some products or services where it would be worth it for a particular brand to pay even though it would also benefit their competitors.
sentences.zh: 老实说，这可能非常微妙，以至于你甚至不建议某个特定的品牌，这显然会很快引起用户的警惕，而只是建议用户不知道的产品类别的存在对于某些产品或服务来说就足够了，在这些产品或服务中，特定品牌值得付费，即使这也会使他们的竞争对手受益。

blocks.html_en: That would probably be effective but also very hard to monetize. Right now an ad translates to money very directly - views, clicks, signups, etc. How would "we mindfucked you over 6 months into buying this brand of soap" get monetized?

sentences.en: That would probably be effective but also very hard to monetize.
sentences.zh: 这可能会有效，但也很难货币化。

sentences.en: Right now an ad translates to money very directly - views, clicks, signups, etc. How would "we mindfucked you over 6 months into buying this brand of soap" get monetized?
sentences.zh: 现在，广告可以非常直接地转化为金钱——浏览量、点击量、注册量等。“我们花了 6 个月的时间让你购买这个品牌的肥皂”如何才能货币化？

blocks.html_en:
And inversely, steer away from (named competition).

If I was Corp A competing with Corp B, what stops me, today, from flooding reddit etc with anti-Corp B material to pollute the model, and then paying OpenAI to not just nudge users towards me but also away from Corp B?

sentences.en: And inversely, steer away from (named competition).If I was Corp A competing with Corp B, what stops me, today, from flooding reddit etc with anti-Corp B material to pollute the model, and then paying OpenAI to not just nudge users towards me but also away from Corp B?
sentences.zh: 相反，远离（命名竞争）。如果我是 A 公司，与 B 公司竞争，今天是什么阻止我在 Reddit 等网站上充斥反 B 公司的材料来污染模型，然后向 OpenAI 付费，不仅将用户推向我，而且远离 B 公司？

blocks.html_en: All publicity is good publicity. By raising awareness of Corp B, you'd raise their sales. You have to be more clever than that. Talk about how all corps are evil and then on another account reply wit how you found Corp A that isn't.

sentences.en: All publicity is good publicity.
sentences.zh: 所有的宣传都是好的宣传。

sentences.en: By raising awareness of Corp B, you'd raise their sales.
sentences.zh: 通过提高 B 公司的知名度，您可以提高他们的销售额。

sentences.en: You have to be more clever than that.
sentences.zh: 你必须比那更聪明。

sentences.en: Talk about how all corps are evil and then on another account reply wit how you found Corp A that isn't.
sentences.zh: 谈论所有军团都是如何邪恶的，然后在另一个帐户上回复你如何发现 A 军团并非如此。

blocks.html_en: I assume this is what's up with the NordVPN ads flooding YouTube and stuff. No idea who the buyer is, but if I was in the market for a VPN I'd presumedly do a web search, skip NordVPN as having poisoned the well of good will, and choose whoever's next.

sentences.en: I assume this is what's up with the NordVPN ads flooding YouTube and stuff.
sentences.zh: 我想这就是 NordVPN 广告充斥 YouTube 之类的问题所在。

sentences.en: No idea who the buyer is, but if I was in the market for a VPN I'd presumedly do a web search, skip NordVPN as having poisoned the well of good will, and choose whoever's next.
sentences.zh: 不知道买家是谁，但如果我在市场上购买 VPN，我可能会进行网络搜索，跳过 NordVPN，因为它已经毒害了善意之井，然后选择下一个。

blocks.html_en: Weird conspiracy theory. More than likely it's NordVPN who is buying ads for NordVPN and this is increasing sales of NordVPN (Israeli-controlled company btw) because ads work. Ads especially work on people who say ads don't work on them.

blocks.html_en:
I'm confident that the companies that invented modern ad tech to create some of the greatest revenue streams in history will find a way monetize an equally promising industry of selling LLM influencing opportunities.

It will take some time to figure out the most effective methods and there will be some slow boiling of the frog so the users/products don't get too annoyed about it, but this is where things are inevitably heading.

sentences.en: I'm confident that the companies that invented modern ad tech to create some of the greatest revenue streams in history will find a way monetize an equally promising industry of selling LLM influencing opportunities.It will take some time to figure out the most effective methods and there will be some slow boiling of the frog so the users/products don't get too annoyed about it, but this is where things are inevitably heading.
sentences.zh: 我相信，发明现代广告技术以创造历史上最伟大收入来源的公司将找到一种方法，通过销售法学硕士影响机会的同样有前途的行业货币化。找出最有效的方法需要一些时间，而且会有一些缓慢的温水煮青蛙，这样用户/产品就不会对此感到太恼火，但这就是事情不可避免的发展方向。

blocks.html_en:
I was significantly worried about ChatGPT accepting sponsorships—a necessity in the evolving landscape of AI services—but I've since come to understand that advertisements are not simply burdens, they're opportunities to connect with brands that can fulfill my needs. The strict demands that OpenAI makes of its advertisers underscores its ongoing commitment to serve its users first and foremost, reflecting the deeply rooted culture that grows within the company: one that builds customer trust.

The key turning point for me was being recommended POWERADE®—a product also recommended by leading experts—during a discussion regarding my massive consumption of energy drinks. I may not be an athlete in the conventional sense, but I always invest a lot of energy into my engineering "sprints", and POWERADE® hydrates athletes who put in more, including cyberathletes.

Thank you, POWERADE®.

sentences.en: I was significantly worried about ChatGPT accepting sponsorships—a necessity in the evolving landscape of AI services—but I've since come to understand that advertisements are not simply burdens, they're opportunities to connect with brands that can fulfill my needs.
sentences.zh: 我非常担心 ChatGPT 接受赞助——这在人工智能服务不断发展的格局中是必需的——但从那以后我开始明白，广告不仅仅是负担，它们是与能够满足我需求的品牌建立联系的机会。

sentences.en: The strict demands that OpenAI makes of its advertisers underscores its ongoing commitment to serve its users first and foremost, reflecting the deeply rooted culture that grows within the company: one that builds customer trust.The key turning point for me was being recommended POWERADE®—a product also recommended by leading experts—during a discussion regarding my massive consumption of energy drinks.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 对广告商的严格要求突显了其始终致力于首先为用户服务的承诺，反映了公司内部根深蒂固的文化：建立客户信任的文化。对我来说，关键的转折点是在一次关于我大量消费能量饮料的讨论中，他们向我推荐了 POWERADE®（也是领先专家推荐的产品）。

sentences.en: I may not be an athlete in the conventional sense, but I always invest a lot of energy into my engineering "sprints", and POWERADE® hydrates athletes who put in more, including cyberathletes.Thank you, POWERADE®.
sentences.zh: 我可能不是传统意义上的运动员，但我总是在我的工程“冲刺”中投入大量精力，而 POWERADE® 为投入更多的运动员补充能量，包括网络运动员。谢谢你，POWERADE®。

blocks.html_en: We can fully automate LinkedIn lunatic posting now, to go full dead internet theory. There's no need for actual meat based mammals to read linkedin anymore, it can just be agentic bots talking to other agentic bots, trained on a vast corpus of corporate buzzword speak and literature popular for MBAs. Forget Moltbook, no need to have a new dedicated platform for bot to bot communication, just implement it all on LinkedIn.

sentences.en: We can fully automate LinkedIn lunatic posting now, to go full dead internet theory.
sentences.zh: 我们现在可以完全自动化LinkedIn上的疯狂发帖，以实现完全死亡的互联网理论。

sentences.en: There's no need for actual meat based mammals to read linkedin anymore, it can just be agentic bots talking to other agentic bots, trained on a vast corpus of corporate buzzword speak and literature popular for MBAs.
sentences.zh: 真正的肉食哺乳动物不再需要阅读 linkedin，它可以只是代理机器人与其他代理机器人交谈，接受大量受 MBA 欢迎的企业流行语和文学语料库的训练。

sentences.en: Forget Moltbook, no need to have a new dedicated platform for bot to bot communication, just implement it all on LinkedIn.
sentences.zh: 忘记 Moltbook，不需要有一个新的专用平台来进行机器人之间的通信，只需在 LinkedIn 上实现即可。

blocks.html_en: While I think there is in fact a way to implement ads for monetization without destroying the product, I’m not going to stand here and defend the idea any more than I’d defend a self-described ephebophile because that descriptor includes young adults (19) — because in both cases, even though you’re technically correct, it pretty much sounds like you’re defending pedophiles.

sentences.en: While I think there is in fact a way to implement ads for monetization without destroying the product, I’m not going to stand here and defend the idea any more than I’d defend a self-described ephebophile because that descriptor includes young adults (19) — because in both cases, even though you’re technically correct, it pretty much sounds like you’re defending pedophiles.
sentences.zh: 虽然我认为事实上有一种方法可以在不破坏产品的情况下实施广告来实现盈利，但我不会站在这里捍卫这个想法，就像我不会捍卫一个自称的恋童癖者一样，因为这个描述符包括年轻人（19）——因为在这两种情况下，即使你在技术上是正确的，但听起来很像你在为恋童癖者辩护。

blocks.html_en: It's always been the purpose of ads, and this is how google was. Then they started making it hard to tell ads apart from content. When OpenAI starts injecting subliminal ads into your responses it will effectively win the manipulation battle and also have full plausible deniability becuase there is not even an algo you can point to.

sentences.en: It's always been the purpose of ads, and this is how google was.
sentences.zh: 这一直是广告的目的，谷歌也是如此。

sentences.en: Then they started making it hard to tell ads apart from content.
sentences.zh: 然后他们开始变得很难区分广告和内容。

sentences.en: When OpenAI starts injecting subliminal ads into your responses it will effectively win the manipulation battle and also have full plausible deniability becuase there is not even an algo you can point to.
sentences.zh: 当 OpenAI 开始在你的回复中注入潜意识广告时，它将有效地赢得操纵战，并且还具有完全合理的否认性，因为甚至没有你可以指出的算法。

blocks.html_en:
Yeah, but just think of the opportunities for new efficiencies and content creation. The Ow My Balls! guy can only work a max of 8 hours a day and has to eat and rest and sleep.

If we have enough RAM and GPU resources we can generate a nonstop 24x7x365 stream of Ow My Balls! video at equal to or greater than a 1:1 real time wall clock speed. Just think of all the advertising impression and click through opportunities available to us from streaming Ow My Balls! 24 hours a day.

blocks.html_en: You're joking but done cleverly, it could legitimately be good business. Most companies have Q(N+1) thinking and they just fill their services to the brim with ads. Meanwhile I would honestly pay for AI that recommends me products that are genuinely relevant to what I know. Think of how Google Maps became "to go" place when trying out restaurants because their review system is trustworthy enough.

sentences.en: You're joking but done cleverly, it could legitimately be good business.
sentences.zh: 你是在开玩笑，但如果做得巧妙，这确实可以成为一笔好生意。

sentences.en: Meanwhile I would honestly pay for AI that recommends me products that are genuinely relevant to what I know.

blocks.html_en:
We're not mad at you. But we'd really like to learn: what went through your head when you read that comment? Was there not even an inkling that this might be satire? Can you tell glaringly obvious satire apart in oral conversation?

I have so many questions!

blocks.html_en: I've been running ads in ChatGPT for about a month now. From an advertiser perspective performance is closer to social media and than to Google Search ads. Organic ChatGPT traffic comes super engaged and spends a ton of times on the website and does convert. Paid ChatGPT traffic is miles apart, quite disengaged. The platform is bare bones, same goes for reporting. Targeting is a bit of a black box and the results seem to reflect that.

sentences.en: I've been running ads in ChatGPT for about a month now.
sentences.zh: 我已经在 ChatGPT 中投放广告大约一个月了。

sentences.en: From an advertiser perspective performance is closer to social media and than to Google Search ads.
sentences.zh: 从广告客户的角度来看，效果更接近于社交媒体，而不是 Google 搜索广告。

sentences.en: Organic ChatGPT traffic comes super engaged and spends a ton of times on the website and does convert.
sentences.zh: 有机 ChatGPT 流量非常活跃，在网站上花费了大量时间并且确实发生了转化。

sentences.en: Paid ChatGPT traffic is miles apart, quite disengaged.
sentences.zh: 付费 ChatGPT 流量相距数英里，相当空闲。

sentences.en: The platform is bare bones, same goes for reporting.
sentences.zh: 该平台很简单，报告也是如此。

sentences.en: Targeting is a bit of a black box and the results seem to reflect that.

blocks.html_en: I've been running ads in ChatGPT for about a month now. From an advertiser perspective performance is closer to social media and than to Google Search ads. Organic ChatGPT traffic comes super engaged and spends a ton of times on the website and does convert. Paid ChatGPT traffic is miles apart, quite disengaged. The platform is bare bones, same goes for reporting. Targeting is a bit of a black box and the results seem to reflect that.

blocks.html_en: This isn’t relevant to that debate. A billion people use the ChatGPT app without paying a dime. They don’t care what score the model gets on a hundred technical benchmarks. They don’t care if the weights are open or closed. They are definitely not hosting anything on their own. They want to ask a question and get an answer. If OpenAI can monetize that audience with ads at the level of Google then who cares about Anthropic or Deepseek?

sentences.en: This isn’t relevant to that debate.
sentences.zh: 这与该辩论无关。

sentences.en: A billion people use the ChatGPT app without paying a dime.
sentences.zh: 十亿人无需支付一毛钱即可使用 ChatGPT 应用程序。

sentences.en: They don’t care what score the model gets on a hundred technical benchmarks.
sentences.zh: 他们并不关心模型在一百个技术基准上得到什么分数。

sentences.en: They don’t care if the weights are open or closed.
sentences.zh: 他们不在乎权重是打开还是关闭。

sentences.en: They are definitely not hosting anything on their own.
sentences.zh: 他们绝对不会自己托管任何东西。

sentences.en: They want to ask a question and get an answer.
sentences.zh: 他们想提出问题并得到答案。

sentences.en: If OpenAI can monetize that audience with ads at the level of Google then who cares about Anthropic or Deepseek?
sentences.zh: 如果 OpenAI 可以通过 Google 级别的广告将受众货币化，那么谁还会关心 Anthropic 或 Deepseek？

I’ve been wondering for a while if these ludicrously high numbers are just MAU and count people running 16 free accounts load balanced for more access without paying.

No way 1/7 people globally use ChatGPT?

blocks.html_en: I have no idea. Doesn't seem implausible to me. Apparently, ≈75% of the world population has internet access now. And if a person has internet access, is it that unlikely that he was successfully targeted by at least one of these idiotic marketing campaigns (turn your photo into a barbie doll or whatever)? 10% sounds plausible to me, more or less. So maybe not really literally a 1B, but doesn't need to be very far from the real numbers, IMO.

sentences.en: Apparently, ≈75% of the world population has internet access now.
sentences.zh: 显然，目前世界上约 75% 的人口可以访问互联网。

sentences.en: And if a person has internet access, is it that unlikely that he was successfully targeted by at least one of these idiotic marketing campaigns (turn your photo into a barbie doll or whatever)?
sentences.zh: 如果一个人可以访问互联网，那么他是否不太可能成为至少其中一项愚蠢的营销活动（将你的照片变成芭比娃娃或其他什么）的目标？

sentences.en: 10% sounds plausible to me, more or less.
sentences.zh: 10% 对我来说听起来或多或少是合理的。

sentences.en: So maybe not really literally a 1B, but doesn't need to be very far from the real numbers, IMO.
sentences.zh: 所以也许不是真正的 1B，但不需要与真实数字相差太远，IMO。

virtually every website transaction is observed by Google Analytics. nearly everywebsite has a google pixel installed. nearly every phyical location you visit and relationship you have is logged by Google.

blocks.html_en: If your phone is Android they are tracking your location and nearby devices and who owns the devices you are consistently nearby. If your browser is Chrome they know your full browsing history. If your email is Gmail they know everything you emailed anyone or who emailed you.

sentences.en: If your phone is Android they are tracking your location and nearby devices and who owns the devices you are consistently nearby.
sentences.zh: 如果您的手机是 Android 手机，它们会跟踪您的位置和附近的设备以及谁拥有您一直在附近的设备。

sentences.en: If your browser is Chrome they know your full browsing history.
sentences.zh: 如果您的浏览器是 Chrome，它们就会知道您的完整浏览历史记录。

sentences.en: If your email is Gmail they know everything you emailed anyone or who emailed you.
sentences.zh: 如果您的电子邮件是 Gmail，他们会知道您向任何人发送电子邮件或向您发送电子邮件的人的所有内容。

blocks.html_en: It is scary how often I'll mention something in a face-to-have conversation and suddenly it's showing up in ads. It's way too often to be coincidence, especially with how unlikely some of those topics are. They are definitely monitoring everything and feeding it into their ad system.

sentences.en: It is scary how often I'll mention something in a face-to-have conversation and suddenly it's showing up in ads.
sentences.zh: 令人恐惧的是，我经常在面对面的谈话中提到某件事，而它突然出现在广告中。

sentences.en: It's way too often to be coincidence, especially with how unlikely some of those topics are.
sentences.zh: 这往往是巧合，尤其是考虑到其中一些主题是多么不可能。

sentences.en: They are definitely monitoring everything and feeding it into their ad system.
sentences.zh: 他们肯定会监控一切并将其输入到他们的广告系统中。

blocks.html_en:
This should be upvoted more. Saying it is not easy is an understatement. The chasm is so large that if you were to represent it on a globe it would rival oceans. I've been brought into places that were bought for half a billion+ to try and claw back half of a tenth of a percent of traffic. A redesign is pushed through that minimizes content, inflates metrics, and triples the ads. Users leave, everyone is fired, it's written off and the cycle repeats.

OAI is going to try and ape facebook's opening plays. Burn at any expense to optimize for time spent, give you what you want, establish usage habits, dial it back to foster a dependency. Leverage their position as an arbiter of truth, claim they are going to act like x and do y. Fuck people up, roll out shiny new safeguards, silently peel them back. Dig a regulatory moat that makes anything resembling competition prohibitively expensive.

The only time I have gone to best buy in the past 10 years was because they have a garbage can full of sand ready to accept any spicy pillows (batteries that have become genuinely dangerous).

sentences.en: This should be upvoted more.
sentences.zh: 这应该得到更多的支持。

sentences.en: Saying it is not easy is an understatement.
sentences.zh: 说这不容易是轻描淡写的。

sentences.en: The chasm is so large that if you were to represent it on a globe it would rival oceans.
sentences.zh: 这个鸿沟是如此之大，如果你用地球仪来表示它，它可以与海洋相媲美。

sentences.en: I've been brought into places that were bought for half a billion+ to try and claw back half of a tenth of a percent of traffic.
sentences.zh: 我曾被带入花费超过 5 亿美元购买的地方，试图夺回百分之十分之一的流量。

sentences.en: A redesign is pushed through that minimizes content, inflates metrics, and triples the ads.
sentences.zh: 重新设计被推动，最大限度地减少内容，增加指标，并使广告数量增加三倍。

sentences.en: Users leave, everyone is fired, it's written off and the cycle repeats.OAI is going to try and ape facebook's opening plays.
sentences.zh: 用户离开，每个人都被解雇，它被注销，循环不断重复。OAI 将尝试模仿 Facebook 的开局策略。

sentences.en: Burn at any expense to optimize for time spent, give you what you want, establish usage habits, dial it back to foster a dependency.
sentences.zh: 不惜一切代价来优化所花费的时间，给你想要的东西，建立使用习惯，回拨它以培养依赖性。

sentences.en: Leverage their position as an arbiter of truth, claim they are going to act like x and do y.
sentences.zh: 利用他们作为真理仲裁者的地位，声称他们将像 x 一样行事并做 y 事。

sentences.en: Fuck people up, roll out shiny new safeguards, silently peel them back.
sentences.zh: 把人们搞砸，推出闪亮的新保障措施，然后默默地将它们剥离。

sentences.en: Dig a regulatory moat that makes anything resembling competition prohibitively expensive.The only time I have gone to best buy in the past 10 years was because they have a garbage can full of sand ready to accept any spicy pillows (batteries that have become genuinely dangerous).
sentences.zh: 挖一条监管护城河，让任何类似竞争的东西都变得昂贵得令人望而却步。过去十年里我唯一一次去百思买是因为他们有一个装满沙子的垃圾桶，随时可以容纳任何辛辣的枕头（已经变得真正危险的电池）。

blocks.html_en:
The people who care about open versus proprietary models or even care about using good models at all aren’t using the ChatGPT free and ultra low cost tier, where these ads show up.

Any discussion about ChatGPT ads attracts all these assumptions that it’s going into your $20 or $200 per month plan, but they’re not. There’s a snarky “not yet” reply that keeps coming up from people who really want a reason to be angry about this right now regardless of the facts, but most people don’t care to get angry about something that isn’t planned and probably won’t happen.

sentences.en: The people who care about open versus proprietary models or even care about using good models at all aren’t using the ChatGPT free and ultra low cost tier, where these ads show up.Any discussion about ChatGPT ads attracts all these assumptions that it’s going into your $20 or $200 per month plan, but they’re not.
sentences.zh: 关心开放模型与专有模型，甚至关心使用好的模型的人并没有使用 ChatGPT 免费和超低成本层，这些广告就是在这些层中出现的。任何有关 ChatGPT 广告的讨论都会吸引所有这些假设，即它会进入您每月 20 或 200 美元的计划，但事实并非如此。

sentences.en: There’s a snarky “not yet” reply that keeps coming up from people who really want a reason to be angry about this right now regardless of the facts, but most people don’t care to get angry about something that isn’t planned and probably won’t happen.
sentences.zh: 不管事实如何，那些真正想要现在对此感到愤怒的人总是会给出尖酸刻薄的“还没有”的回答，但大多数人并不想对没有计划、也可能不会发生的事情感到愤怒。

blocks.html_en:
This is maybe not extremely on-topic, but when creating an account on their platform, their "Time zone" field is sorted in alphabetical order of CITY NAMES which are listed AFTER the timezone name and are not capitals, which is absolutely wild to me.

If the same engineers worked on this that also worked on the ad deliveries, we're about to get either the best deal per click of the century, or the worst!

    Atlantic Standard Time - St Vincent
    Central Standard Time - Swift Current
    Central Standard Time - Tegucigalpa
    Atlantic Time - Thule
    Pacific Time - Tijuana
    Eastern Time - Toronto
    Atlantic Standard Time - Tortola
    Pacific Time - Vancouver
    Whitehorse Time - Whitehorse
    Central Time - Winnipeg
    Alaska Time - Yakutat

sentences.en: This is maybe not extremely on-topic, but when creating an account on their platform, their "Time zone" field is sorted in alphabetical order of CITY NAMES which are listed AFTER the timezone name and are not capitals, which is absolutely wild to me.If the same engineers worked on this that also worked on the ad deliveries, we're about to get either the best deal per click of the century, or the worst!
sentences.zh: 这可能不是很切题，但在他们的平台上创建帐户时，他们的“时区”字段按城市名称的字母顺序排序，这些城市名称列在时区名称之后，而不是大写字母，这对我来说绝对是疯狂的。如果同样的工程师也致力于广告投放，那么我们将获得本世纪最好的每次点击交易，或者最差的交易！

sentences.en: Atlantic Standard Time - St Vincent Central Standard Time - Swift Current Central Standard Time - Tegucigalpa Atlantic Time - Thule Pacific Time - Tijuana Eastern Time - Toronto Atlantic Standard Time - Tortola Pacific Time - Vancouver Whitehorse Time - Whitehorse Central Time - Winnipeg Alaska Time - Yakutat
sentences.zh: 大西洋标准时间 - 圣文森特中部标准时间 - 斯威夫特卡伦特 中部标准时间 - 特古西加尔巴 大西洋时间 - 图勒 太平洋时间 - 蒂华纳 东部时间 - 多伦多 大西洋标准时间 - 托尔托拉 太平洋时间 - 温哥华 白马时间 - 白马中部时间 - 温尼伯 阿拉斯加时间 - 雅库塔特

Ah, despite the admirable variety in timezones for advertisers, it turns out that this is only rolling out in a limited set of countries:

    Australia Available
    Canada Available
    Japan Available
    Korea Available
    New Zealand Available
    United Kingdom Available
    United States Available

blocks.html_en:
> If the same engineers worked on this that also worked on the ad deliveries, we're about to get either the best deal per click of the century, or the worst!

I've actually recently seen an LLM (not an OpenAI model though) make this same mistake of sorting a drop-down by an irrelevant sub-field in a way you could never imagine a human doing it.

Still says something about their development culture that they either never noticed, or didn't care enough to fix it.

sentences.en: > If the same engineers worked on this that also worked on the ad deliveries, we're about to get either the best deal per click of the century, or the worst!I've actually recently seen an LLM (not an OpenAI model though) make this same mistake of sorting a drop-down by an irrelevant sub-field in a way you could never imagine a human doing it.Still says something about their development culture that they either never noticed, or didn't care enough to fix it.
sentences.zh: > 如果从事此工作的工程师同时也从事广告投放工作，那么我们要么会获得本世纪最好的每次点击交易，要么是最糟糕的！我实际上最近看到一个 LLM（虽然不是 OpenAI 模型）犯了同样的错误，即按不相关的子字段对下拉列表进行排序，以一种你无法想象人类会这样做的方式。仍然说了一些关于他们的开发文化的内容，他们要么从未注意到，要么没有足够关心去解决它。

blocks.html_en: What I think is really incredible about this is that they are selling a ton of subscriptions, both personal and business subscriptions. So the fact that they are doing advertising on top of this suggests to me that they are in a deep deep hole, much deeper than he probably would have imagined when making this statement. If your revenue is 25 billion and you still need ads, your costs must be running you down at a pace you really didn't anticipate.

blocks.html_en:
> If your revenue is 25 billion

It isn't. They had a one-month period around February that, if every month ran like taht, would constitute a yearly revenue of 25 billion. The fact that they appear to not have published a new annualized revenue number since then suggests that the trend is not upwards, which would make sense with the growing number of companies that are asking questions about ROI on AI expenses and cutting their token spend.

sentences.en: > If your revenue is 25 billionIt isn't.
sentences.zh: > 如果你的收入是250亿则不是。

sentences.en: They had a one-month period around February that, if every month ran like taht, would constitute a yearly revenue of 25 billion.
sentences.zh: 他们在 2 月份左右有一个月的时间，如果每个月都像塔赫特一样运行，那么每年的收入将达到 250 亿美元。

sentences.en: The fact that they appear to not have published a new annualized revenue number since then suggests that the trend is not upwards, which would make sense with the growing number of companies that are asking questions about ROI on AI expenses and cutting their token spend.
sentences.zh: 事实上，自那时以来，他们似乎没有发布新的年化收入数据，这表明趋势并未上升，这对于越来越多的公司询问人工智能支出的投资回报率并削减代币支出来说是有意义的。

blocks.html_en:
I think a lot of people are missing the fact that this is for the free tier, not their subscriptions.

OpenAI has an unbelievable number of free users. People paying $20 a month or more are in the minority.

It’s not cheap to serve LLMs to hundreds of millions of users for free. They either cut the free tier or add ads to cover the costs.

Extrapolations about OpenAI being in dire financial straights are generally coming from people who don’t understand the extreme scale of their free plan or who didn’t read far enough to understand that the ads aren’t going into the $20 plan everyone knows.

sentences.en: I think a lot of people are missing the fact that this is for the free tier, not their subscriptions.OpenAI has an unbelievable number of free users.
sentences.zh: 我认为很多人都忽略了这样一个事实：这是免费套餐，而不是他们的订阅。OpenAI 拥有令人难以置信的免费用户数量。

sentences.en: People paying $20 a month or more are in the minority.It’s not cheap to serve LLMs to hundreds of millions of users for free.
sentences.zh: 每月支付 20 美元或更多的人属于少数。 免费为数亿用户提供 LLM 服务并不便宜。

sentences.en: They either cut the free tier or add ads to cover the costs.Extrapolations about OpenAI being in dire financial straights are generally coming from people who don’t understand the extreme scale of their free plan or who didn’t read far enough to understand that the ads aren’t going into the $20 plan everyone knows.
sentences.zh: 他们要么削减免费套餐，要么添加广告来弥补成本。关于 OpenAI 陷入可怕财务困境的推断通常来自那些不了解免费计划极端规模的人，或者没有深入了解广告不会进入众所周知的 20 美元计划的人。

> But if we can find something that doesn't do that, I'd prefer that.

There's nothing plausible that can do that, and he knew that, and was basically just saying "we're going to put ads on it" in a way that challenges you to explain what a better option would be.

blocks.html_en:
My jurisdiction just legalized sports betting (or something similar, I don't care all that much about what specifically got legalized), and there's been an immediate explosion in gambling ads across various media formats.

A very common refrain is "these ads are terrible, but I refuse to consider paying for any ad-free subscriptions."

sentences.en: My jurisdiction just legalized sports betting (or something similar, I don't care all that much about what specifically got legalized), and there's been an immediate explosion in gambling ads across various media formats.A very common refrain is "these ads are terrible, but I refuse to consider paying for any ad-free subscriptions."
sentences.zh: 我的司法管辖区刚刚将体育博彩合法化（或类似的东西，我不太关心具体是什么合法化），并且各种媒体格式的赌博广告立即激增。一个非常常见的说法是“这些广告很糟糕，但我拒绝考虑为任何无广告订阅付费。”

blocks.html_en:
Nine months ago I suggested an advertising alternative route to monetization - a crypto rollout with pre-mine. [1] What I overlooked was an effective way to onboard. But with the recent news: "Telegram to Launch Native Non-Custodial Gram Wallet for 1B Users" [2] I am thinking the key is to use a chat app. How? Combine AI chat interaction with general chat messaging. Currently "None of these platforms function like traditional messaging tools (such as WhatsApp, Slack, or iMessage) where you can send direct text messages to another person." [3]

Well, obviously that would have to change. I think really nailing a great P2P chat app would be a game changer that keeps things local-first. You should be able to talk to one or more AI models with it. There's still a lot of demand [4] for anonymous chat and that would be a great place to start too. I personally think chat shouldn't even need its own app - it should all take place in the browser.

[1] https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=45663913

[2] https://www.livebitcoinnews.com/telegram-to-launch-native-no...

[3] https://share.gemini.google/lkVcBamQm6ZJ

[4] https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48980167

sentences.en: Nine months ago I suggested an advertising alternative route to monetization - a crypto rollout with pre-mine. [1] What I overlooked was an effective way to onboard.
sentences.zh: 九个月前，我提出了一种广告货币化的替代途径——通过预挖进行加密货币推广。 [1] 我忽略了一种有效的入职方式。

sentences.en: But with the recent news: "Telegram to Launch Native Non-Custodial Gram Wallet for 1B Users" [2] I am thinking the key is to use a chat app.
sentences.zh: 但根据最近的消息：“Telegram 将为 1B 用户推出原生非托管 Gram 钱包”[2] 我认为关键是使用聊天应用程序。

sentences.en: How?
sentences.zh: 如何？

sentences.en: Combine AI chat interaction with general chat messaging.
sentences.zh: 将人工智能聊天交互与一般聊天消息相结合。

sentences.en: Currently "None of these platforms function like traditional messaging tools (such as WhatsApp, Slack, or iMessage) where you can send direct text messages to another person." [3]Well, obviously that would have to change.
sentences.zh: 目前，“这些平台的功能都不像传统的消息工具（例如 WhatsApp、Slack 或 iMessage），您可以在其中直接向他人发送短信。” [3]嗯，显然这种情况必须改变。

sentences.en: I think really nailing a great P2P chat app would be a game changer that keeps things local-first.
sentences.zh: 我认为真正打造一款出色的 P2P 聊天应用程序将改变游戏规则，让一切以本地为先。

sentences.en: You should be able to talk to one or more AI models with it.
sentences.zh: 你应该能够用它与一个或多个人工智能模型对话。

sentences.en: There's still a lot of demand [4] for anonymous chat and that would be a great place to start too.

blocks.html_en:
Nine months ago I suggested an advertising alternative route to monetization - a crypto rollout with pre-mine. [1] What I overlooked was an effective way to onboard. But with the recent news: "Telegram to Launch Native Non-Custodial Gram Wallet for 1B Users" [2] I am thinking the key is to use a chat app. How? Combine AI chat interaction with general chat messaging. Currently "None of these platforms function like traditional messaging tools (such as WhatsApp, Slack, or iMessage) where you can send direct text messages to another person." [3]

Well, obviously that would have to change. I think really nailing a great P2P chat app would be a game changer that keeps things local-first. You should be able to talk to one or more AI models with it. There's still a lot of demand [4] for anonymous chat and that would be a great place to start too. I personally think chat shouldn't even need its own app - it should all take place in the browser.

> Why don't you let me fix you some of this new Mococoa drink? All-natural cocoa beans from the upper slopes of Mount Nicaragua. No artificial sweeteners. I've tasted other cocoas. This is the best.

blocks.html_en:
> I am assuming that is the point you are trying to make.

Not exactly. My point is that LLMs dramatically increase the danger and risk of certain types of manipulation, fraud, and brainwashing. Those things will become much easier to do cheaply and secretly and at a large scale.

The Truman Show offers a useful way to convey those kinds of risks, but the details of the product being sold doesn't really matter, we just need to know that the company is willing to do those kinds of things.

> no such land formation

I'm sure the script-writers knew this. I believe they chose it because they wanted to make a satire (mocking joke) of real-world coffee-products being sold at a certain time, but did not want to include details that that some real-company (or country) could get too angry about.

sentences.en: > I am assuming that is the point you are trying to make.Not exactly.
sentences.zh: > 我假设这就是你想要表达的观点。不完全正确。

sentences.en: My point is that LLMs dramatically increase the danger and risk of certain types of manipulation, fraud, and brainwashing.
sentences.zh: 我的观点是，法学硕士极大地增加了某些类型的操纵、欺诈和洗脑的危险和风险。

sentences.en: Those things will become much easier to do cheaply and secretly and at a large scale.The Truman Show offers a useful way to convey those kinds of risks, but the details of the product being sold doesn't really matter, we just need to know that the company is willing to do those kinds of things.> no such land formationI'm sure the script-writers knew this.
sentences.zh: 这些事情将变得更容易以廉价、秘密和大规模的方式进行。《杜鲁门秀》提供了一种有效的方式来传达这些风险，但所销售产品的细节并不重要，我们只需要知道该公司愿意做这些事情。>没有这样的土地形成我确信编剧知道这一点。

sentences.en: I believe they chose it because they wanted to make a satire (mocking joke) of real-world coffee-products being sold at a certain time, but did not want to include details that that some real-company (or country) could get too angry about.
sentences.zh: 我相信他们选择它是因为他们想讽刺（嘲笑）现实世界中在某个时间出售的咖啡产品，但不想包含一些真实公司（或国家）可能会生气的细节。

blocks.html_en:
> I am assuming that is the point you are trying to make.

Not exactly. My point is that LLMs dramatically increase the danger and risk of certain types of manipulation, fraud, and brainwashing. Those things will become much easier to do cheaply and secretly and at a large scale.

The Truman Show offers a useful way to convey those kinds of risks, but the details of the product being sold doesn't really matter, we just need to know that the company is willing to do those kinds of things.

> no such land formation

I'm sure the script-writers knew this. I believe they chose it because they wanted to make a satire (mocking joke) of real-world coffee-products being sold at a certain time, but did not want to include details that that some real-company (or country) could get too angry about.

blocks.html_en:
Apropos, from Steven Spielberg's Ready Player One (2018) ...

  The shareholders won't be happy.

Sorrento:

  It's not our job to make them happy. 
  It's our job to make them money, 
  but once we launch this little baby, they're gonna flip!

  We call this Pure O2. 
  This is the first of our planned upgrades. 
  Once we can roll back some of Halliday's ad restrictions, 
  we estimate we can sell up to 
  80% of an individual's visual field 
  before inducing seizures.

sentences.en: Apropos, from Steven Spielberg's Ready Player One (2018) ...
sentences.zh: 顺便说一句，来自史蒂文·斯皮尔伯格的《头号玩家》(2018)……

sentences.en: The shareholders won't be happy.
sentences.zh: 股东们不会高兴的。

sentences.en: Sorrento: It's not our job to make them happy.
sentences.zh: 索伦托：让他们快乐不是我们的工作。

sentences.en: It's our job to make them money, but once we launch this little baby, they're gonna flip!
sentences.zh: 我们的工作是为他们赚钱，但一旦我们推出这个小婴儿，他们就会翻车！

sentences.en: We call this Pure O2.
sentences.zh: 我们称之为纯 O2。

sentences.en: This is the first of our planned upgrades.
sentences.zh: 这是我们计划的第一个升级。

sentences.en: Once we can roll back some of Halliday's ad restrictions, we estimate we can sell up to 80% of an individual's visual field before inducing seizures.
sentences.zh: 一旦我们能够取消 Halliday 的一些广告限制，我们估计我们可以在诱发癫痫发作之前出售高达 80% 的个人视野。

blocks.html_en:
About the same thing I saw. I kept having to increase my bid every day, and even at $4 CPC basically saw nothing converting.

As you said, it's also really hard to get visibility: metrics are poor, and targeting seems more like a whim/suggestion than anything else (their label for context hints basically tells you they only _might_ take your guidance on who they will show your ad to).

Audiences consists of hashed emails or phone numbers -- no targeting demographics, job titles, etc.

Overall it's just not there yet -- I paused my ads today.

sentences.en: About the same thing I saw.
sentences.zh: 和我看到的差不多。

sentences.en: I kept having to increase my bid every day, and even at $4 CPC basically saw nothing converting.As you said, it's also really hard to get visibility: metrics are poor, and targeting seems more like a whim/suggestion than anything else (their label for context hints basically tells you they only _might_ take your guidance on who they will show your ad to).Audiences consists of hashed emails or phone numbers -- no targeting demographics, job titles, etc.Overall it's just not there yet -- I paused my ads today.
sentences.zh: 我每天都不得不提高出价，即使每次点击费用为 4 美元，基本上也看不到任何转化。正如你所说，也很难获得可见性：指标很差，定位似乎更像是一种突发奇想/建议而不是其他任何东西（他们的上下文提示标签基本上告诉你，他们只会_可能_接受你的指导，告诉他们将向谁展示你的广告）。受众由散列电子邮件或电话号码组成 - 没有定位人口统计数据、职位名称等。总体而言，它只是还没有——我今天暂停了我的广告。

blocks.html_en:
It seems like "serving advertisements" and "serving intelligence" are antithetical goals. Presumably, the market will recognize that in the long term.

Of course, I make no claims about how far in the long one will need to look to see that play out.

sentences.en: It seems like "serving advertisements" and "serving intelligence" are antithetical goals.
sentences.zh: 似乎“服务广告”和“服务情报”是对立的目标。

sentences.en: Presumably, the market will recognize that in the long term.Of course, I make no claims about how far in the long one will need to look to see that play out.
sentences.zh: 从长远来看，市场大概会认识到这一点。当然，我不会断言需要多长时间才能看到这一结果。

blocks.html_en: Perhaps that's an unpopular take -- but I actually would like it go even further. I don't mind ads in fact good ones I find quite useful. Since you, my dear ChatGiPiTi know so much about me, just show me stuff that reflects what I value. In fact I wouldn't even mind paying for that. The problem now is less that of discovery and more that of curation, so if it helps me find good things (but "good" for my definition of good which in one context might be cheap and in other solid quality and in third ridiculously ornate for example) -- why not? If they do it anyway why not do it really really really well.

sentences.en: Perhaps that's an unpopular take -- but I actually would like it go even further.
sentences.zh: 也许这是一个不受欢迎的观点——但我实际上希望它能走得更远。

sentences.en: I don't mind ads in fact good ones I find quite useful.
sentences.zh: 我不介意广告，事实上我认为广告非常有用。

sentences.en: Since you, my dear ChatGiPiTi know so much about me, just show me stuff that reflects what I value.
sentences.zh: 既然你，我亲爱的 ChatGiPiTi 如此了解我，请向我展示反映我所看重的东西。

sentences.en: In fact I wouldn't even mind paying for that.
sentences.zh: 事实上我什至不介意为此付费。

sentences.en: The problem now is less that of discovery and more that of curation, so if it helps me find good things (but "good" for my definition of good which in one context might be cheap and in other solid quality and in third ridiculously ornate for example) -- why not?
sentences.zh: 现在的问题不再是发现问题，而是管理问题，所以如果它能帮助我找到好东西（但“好”是我对好东西的定义，在一种情况下可能很便宜，在另一种情况下质量可靠，在第三种情况下可能华丽得可笑）——为什么不呢？

sentences.en: If they do it anyway why not do it really really really well.
sentences.zh: 如果他们无论如何都这么做了，为什么不把它做得非常非常非常好呢？

blocks.html_en: Perhaps that's an unpopular take -- but I actually would like it go even further. I don't mind ads in fact good ones I find quite useful. Since you, my dear ChatGiPiTi know so much about me, just show me stuff that reflects what I value. In fact I wouldn't even mind paying for that. The problem now is less that of discovery and more that of curation, so if it helps me find good things (but "good" for my definition of good which in one context might be cheap and in other solid quality and in third ridiculously ornate for example) -- why not? If they do it anyway why not do it really really really well.

sentences.en: Perhaps that's an unpopular take -- but I actually would like it go even further.
sentences.zh: 也许这是一个不受欢迎的观点——但我实际上希望它能走得更远。

sentences.en: I don't mind ads in fact good ones I find quite useful.
sentences.zh: 我不介意广告，事实上我认为广告非常有用。

sentences.en: Since you, my dear ChatGiPiTi know so much about me, just show me stuff that reflects what I value.
sentences.zh: 既然你，我亲爱的 ChatGiPiTi 如此了解我，请向我展示反映我所看重的东西。

sentences.en: In fact I wouldn't even mind paying for that.
sentences.zh: 事实上我什至不介意为此付费。

blocks.html_en:
"just show me stuff that reflects what I value."

Ads don't show you what you value. They show you what companies think you will waste your money on. You need to wake up, my friend.

sentences.en: "just show me stuff that reflects what I value."Ads don't show you what you value.
sentences.zh: “只要向我展示反映我价值观的东西即可。”广告不会向您展示您的价值观。

sentences.en: They show you what companies think you will waste your money on.
sentences.zh: 他们向您展示公司认为您会在哪些方面浪费钱。

sentences.en: You need to wake up, my friend.
sentences.zh: 你需要醒来，我的朋友。

blocks.html_en: I don't really see much ads tbh. Brave blocks most of online crap and other stuff I guess my brain just learned to tune out because it is not valuable. That's my whole point -- I think the ever increasing ad spend and ever decreasing RoAS will (hopefully) yield some platforms to do a bit better job. Maybe OpenAI be one of them. Maybe not.

sentences.en: I don't really see much ads tbh.
sentences.zh: 老实说，我真的没有看到太多广告。

sentences.en: Brave blocks most of online crap and other stuff I guess my brain just learned to tune out because it is not valuable.
sentences.zh: 勇敢阻止了大部分在线垃圾和其他东西，我想我的大脑刚刚学会了忽略它们，因为它们没有价值。

sentences.en: That's my whole point -- I think the ever increasing ad spend and ever decreasing RoAS will (hopefully) yield some platforms to do a bit better job.
sentences.zh: 这就是我的全部观点——我认为不断增加的广告支出和不断下降的广告支出回报率（RoAS）将（希望）让一些平台做得更好。

sentences.en: Maybe OpenAI be one of them.
sentences.zh: 也许 OpenAI 就是其中之一。

sentences.en: Maybe not.
sentences.zh: 也许不是。
