blocks.html_en: Consumers have agency. The best ad placement for a company is to a consumer who wants what they’re selling. It’s a symbiotic relationship.

sentences.en: Consumers have agency.
sentences.zh: 消费者有代理权。

sentences.en: The best ad placement for a company is to a consumer who wants what they’re selling.
sentences.zh: 对于公司来说，最好的广告投放位置是针对想要其销售产品的消费者。

sentences.en: It’s a symbiotic relationship.
sentences.zh: 这是一种共生关系。

blocks.html_en: Ads are often misleading though. You know, it "looks bigger in the picture" type of thing. If ads were honest, the hose hideaway my wife just ordered would have said "flimsy piece of plastic shit you will throw away after a year, but should get you through the summer".

sentences.en: Ads are often misleading though.
sentences.zh: 但广告常常具有误导性。

sentences.en: You know, it "looks bigger in the picture" type of thing.
sentences.zh: 你知道，它是“在图片中看起来更大”之类的东西。

sentences.en: If ads were honest, the hose hideaway my wife just ordered would have said "flimsy piece of plastic shit you will throw away after a year, but should get you through the summer".
sentences.zh: 如果广告是诚实的，我妻子刚刚订购的软管隐藏处会说“一年后你会扔掉的一块脆弱的塑料垃圾，但应该可以让你度过夏天”。

blocks.html_en:
The best ad placement for a consumer is one for the best product of the sort they want.

Taking money to decide which of the options to show is in conflict with the goal of showing the most appropriate.

sentences.en: The best ad placement for a consumer is one for the best product of the sort they want.Taking money to decide which of the options to show is in conflict with the goal of showing the most appropriate.
sentences.zh: 对于消费者来说，最好的广告投放是针对他们想要的那种最好的产品。用钱来决定展示哪些选项与展示最合适的选项的目标是相冲突的。

blocks.html_en: Seriously? 99% of the ads are trying to sell stuff you dont need at all. But they want to sell that to you. Why would you rely on an ad to realize what you NEED and then buy it?

sentences.zh: 99% 的广告都试图推销你根本不需要的东西。

sentences.en: But they want to sell that to you.
sentences.zh: 但他们想把它卖给你。

sentences.en: Why would you rely on an ad to realize what you NEED and then buy it?
sentences.zh: 为什么你要依靠广告来了解你需要什么然后购买它？

blocks.html_en: I have been generally involved in the advertising industry for over 20 years, initially as my primary profession and now more on the side. But what I've seen in that time is that advertising provides absolutely zero positives to the consumer.

sentences.en: I have been generally involved in the advertising industry for over 20 years, initially as my primary profession and now more on the side.
sentences.zh: 我从事广告行业已有20多年了，最初是我的主要职业，现在更多的是副业。

sentences.en: But what I've seen in that time is that advertising provides absolutely zero positives to the consumer.
sentences.zh: 但我当时看到的是，广告给消费者带来的积极影响绝对为零。

blocks.html_en: Well.. doesn't it pay for let's say Superbowl? Or makes NBA possible? Didn't it enable early internet to mature and become internet as we know now? If you're talking about just the first order affects, perhaps I see your point. But broadly speaking, advertising fuels a gigantic part of modern society. Remove it, and who knows what we will find ourselves in.

sentences.en: Well..
sentences.zh: 出色地..

sentences.en: doesn't it pay for let's say Superbowl?
sentences.zh: 难道它不支付超级碗的费用吗？

sentences.en: Or makes NBA possible?
sentences.zh: 或者让NBA成为可能？

sentences.en: Didn't it enable early internet to mature and become internet as we know now?
sentences.zh: 难道它没有让早期的互联网走向成熟，成为我们现在所知道的互联网吗？

sentences.en: If you're talking about just the first order affects, perhaps I see your point.
sentences.zh: 如果你说的只是一阶影响，也许我明白你的意思。

sentences.en: But broadly speaking, advertising fuels a gigantic part of modern society.
sentences.zh: 但从广义上讲，广告推动了现代社会的巨大发展。

sentences.en: Remove it, and who knows what we will find ourselves in.
sentences.zh: 除去它，谁知道我们会发现自己陷入什么境地。

blocks.html_en:
Look, I am also not convinced that modern society, or at least the economic aspects of it, are providing a net positive for the average consumer either. Saying advertising enabling these high-cost ventures being a good thing makes me immediately question why these ventures are high-cost to begin with. Yes, advertising enables the superbowl. But the superbowl exists in order to sell advertising

This is a deep kettle of fish to be opening, but I don't believe that the world we have constructed around us is necessarily good for us.

I don't know what the world would look like without Google so almost impossible to argue, but I would definitely classify (ex. youtube) as "clearly useful".

blocks.html_en: This part of your comment, “just show me stuff that reflects what I value,” makes me think about the political manipulation used in social networks. It’s scary to learn about priming and how it affects our judgment; now imagine a politically (or religiously) charged ad in the middle of your learning session with ChatGPT that leads you to certain conclusions. Doesn’t feel like a good thing.

blocks.html_en:
I am generally against the idea. I have seen it argued that advertising can provide access for those who could not otherwise afford it.

I am not convinced,

I would like all AI be required to state who they are acting in service of (with the possibly of declining to answer).

AI should act, To the best of their ability in the interests of who they are in service of.

It should be illegal to deliberately configure an AI to act against the interesta of whoever they are acting for.

sentences.en: I am generally against the idea.
sentences.zh: 我总体上反对这个想法。

sentences.en: I have seen it argued that advertising can provide access for those who could not otherwise afford it.I am not convinced,I would like all AI be required to state who they are acting in service of (with the possibly of declining to answer).AI should act, To the best of their ability in the interests of who they are in service of.It should be illegal to deliberately configure an AI to act against the interesta of whoever they are acting for.
sentences.zh: 我看到有人认为广告可以为那些无力承担广告费用的人提供访问权限。我不相信，我希望所有人工智能都被要求说明他们正在为谁服务（可能拒绝回答）。人工智能应该采取行动，尽其所能，为他们所服务的人的利益服务。故意配置人工智能来违背他们所代表的人的利益，这应该是非法的。

sentences.zh: 我希望所有人工智能都必须说明他们正在为谁服务（可能会拒绝回答）。

blocks.html_en: AI should act, To the best of their ability in the interests of who they are in service of.

sentences.en: AI should act, To the best of their ability in the interests of who they are in service of.
sentences.zh: 人工智能应该尽其所能，为服务对象的利益服务。

blocks.html_en: It should be illegal to deliberately configure an AI to act against the interesta of whoever they are acting for.

sentences.en: It should be illegal to deliberately configure an AI to act against the interesta of whoever they are acting for.
sentences.zh: 故意配置人工智能来违背其所代表的人的利益，应该是非法的。

blocks.html_en:
According to the ads manager UI, unless I'm willing to allow up to $3 a click I'm warned "my bid may be too low to deliver reliably".

Considering I'm paying 20 cents per click elsewhere, this could get real expensive, real fast.

sentences.en: According to the ads manager UI, unless I'm willing to allow up to $3 a click I'm warned "my bid may be too low to deliver reliably".Considering I'm paying 20 cents per click elsewhere, this could get real expensive, real fast.
sentences.zh: 根据广告管理器 UI，除非我愿意允许每次点击最多 3 美元，否则我会被警告“我的出价可能太低，无法可靠地投放”。考虑到我在其他地方为每次点击支付 20 美分，这可能会变得非常昂贵、非常快。

blocks.html_en:
I had a tell hn sometime ago when I pointed this out: you see ads even on lower paid options. My thinking is: if I have a lower tier- limit my credits, but dont show me ads as I am still paying you. But OpenAi disagreed so I cancelled.

I wonder if we can do that thing that guy did on Facebook a long time ago-- buy an ad showing how much you are being tracked, something like "Hey user X, we know you are based in london, uk and we know you are age range XX etc etc"

sentences.en: I had a tell hn sometime ago when I pointed this out: you see ads even on lower paid options.
sentences.zh: 不久前，当我指出这一点时，我有一个告诉 hn：即使是在付费选项较低的情况下，您也会看到广告。

sentences.en: My thinking is: if I have a lower tier- limit my credits, but dont show me ads as I am still paying you.
sentences.zh: 我的想法是：如果我的等级较低，则限制我的积分，但不要向我展示广告，因为我仍在向您付款。

sentences.en: But OpenAi disagreed so I cancelled.I wonder if we can do that thing that guy did on Facebook a long time ago-- buy an ad showing how much you are being tracked, something like "Hey user X, we know you are based in london, uk and we know you are age range XX etc etc"
sentences.zh: 但 OpenAi 不同意，所以我取消了。我想知道我们是否可以做那个人很久以前在 Facebook 上做过的事情——购买一个广告，显示你被跟踪的程度，比如“嘿用户 X，我们知道你住在英国伦敦，我们知道你的年龄范围是 XX 等等”

blocks.html_en: If other subscription services are anything to go by, I expect that business subscriptions will be free of ads. Personal subs will initially be free of ads, but ads will slowly be added, starting with the cheapest subscription and working their way up.

sentences.en: If other subscription services are anything to go by, I expect that business subscriptions will be free of ads.
sentences.zh: 如果以其他订阅服务为例，我预计商业订阅将没有广告。

sentences.en: Personal subs will initially be free of ads, but ads will slowly be added, starting with the cheapest subscription and working their way up.
sentences.zh: 个人订阅最初将没有广告，但广告将慢慢添加，从最便宜的订阅开始，然后逐步增加。

>Q2 2029, complete ad rollout to all paid subscribers

blocks.html_en:
If you think about it, it's actually pretty odd that professional businesses are reliant on ad-supported services (i.e. Google) for their employees to get work done.

Like it would be weird if you were a professional music reviewer and your employer expected you to use an ad-supported Spotify subscription.

sentences.en: If you think about it, it's actually pretty odd that professional businesses are reliant on ad-supported services (i.e. Google) for their employees to get work done.Like it would be weird if you were a professional music reviewer and your employer expected you to use an ad-supported Spotify subscription.
sentences.zh: 如果你仔细想想，专业企业依赖广告支持的服务（即 Google）让员工完成工作，这实际上是很奇怪的。就像如果你是一名专业音乐评论家，而你的雇主希望你使用广告支持的 Spotify 订阅，那会很奇怪。

blocks.html_en:
Why would people still use ChatGPT?

It's barely free, not that reliable, not the most trustworthy, and now they are popping ads right on my face.

Either spending a good money on the best one, or using the open source models seem to be the future.

blocks.html_en:
I think this is a good moment to bring up a question I was thinking on earlier today.

In a world where agents are searching, building, and signing up for services, how do you advertise your product?

And not just advertise in traditional sense (ads in ChatGPT), but general marketing and awareness of what you're offering. Google search ads? Agents aren't using Google. Launch on Reddit for humans and hope your post gets scraped? Write up a bunch of marketing blog posts and wait for LLMs to ingest your content and have it feed into the next generation of models?

I honestly didn't quite know. If I were to launch a product today, I'd want it to be agent focused. But then how do you tell them about your product? Do these ChatGPT ads get returned if Codex does a web search? Is that good? Bad?

sentences.en: I think this is a good moment to bring up a question I was thinking on earlier today.In a world where agents are searching, building, and signing up for services, how do you advertise your product?And not just advertise in traditional sense (ads in ChatGPT), but general marketing and awareness of what you're offering.
sentences.zh: 我认为现在是提出我今天早些时候思考的一个问题的好时机。在代理搜索、构建和注册服务的世界中，您如何宣传您的产品？不仅仅是传统意义上的广告（ChatGPT 中的广告），而是一般营销和对您所提供的产品的认识。

sentences.en: Google search ads?
sentences.zh: 谷歌搜索广告？

sentences.en: Agents aren't using Google.
sentences.zh: 特工不使用 Google。

sentences.en: Launch on Reddit for humans and hope your post gets scraped?
sentences.zh: 在 Reddit 上面向人类发布并希望您的帖子被删除？

sentences.en: Write up a bunch of marketing blog posts and wait for LLMs to ingest your content and have it feed into the next generation of models?I honestly didn't quite know.
sentences.zh: 写一堆营销博客文章，然后等待法学硕士吸收您的内容并将其输入下一代模型？老实说，我不太知道。

sentences.en: If I were to launch a product today, I'd want it to be agent focused.
sentences.zh: 如果我今天要推出一款产品，我希望它能以代理商为中心。

sentences.en: But then how do you tell them about your product?
sentences.zh: 但是你如何向他们介绍你的产品呢？

sentences.en: Do these ChatGPT ads get returned if Codex does a web search?
sentences.zh: 如果 Codex 进行网络搜索，这些 ChatGPT 广告是否会返回？

sentences.en: Is that good?
sentences.zh: 这样好吗？

sentences.en: Bad?
sentences.zh: 坏的？

blocks.html_en:
In a chatbot-first world, it's gonna have to either be generic impression ads that race to the bottom or contextual recommendations. Of course, the fear is that the advertiser hoards personal data or the chat answers are skewed to make you spend more money.

Those concerns can be resolved by decoupling the text generation from the recommendation engine. It works by casting your intent and match it up with an advertiser's in a secured environment where the advertiser doesn't actually get to see your chats. For example, if you are talking about knee pain during trail running, the recommendation engine might cast "knee pain for runners" and either a shoe company or a physiotherapist would bid against that in an embedding space.

Because the recommendation engine is an opt-in module for publishers and users, it's game-theory welfare maximizing.

I talk a lot more about this on my blog, it goes on for quite a while. https://www.june.kim/vector-space

The exchange is live, with no publishers or advertisers. https://vectorspace.exchange/

blocks.html_en:
>In a world where agents are searching, building, and signing up for services, how do you advertise your product?

I've been running a 1-man software business for over 20 years and it is getting increasingly hard. I no longer get impressions from PPC Ads unless I bid so high that I make no profit. Magazine ads aren't really a thing now. The AIs are stealing my content and SEO works less and less. The channel is being flooded with low effort slop. Traffic to my website is steadily going down. It is hard to know what to do about it. Maybe I can buy some clicks from ChatGpt ads at a price where I can make profit, before the prices rise out of reach?

sentences.en: >In a world where agents are searching, building, and signing up for services, how do you advertise your product?I've been running a 1-man software business for over 20 years and it is getting increasingly hard.
sentences.zh: >在代理搜索、构建和注册服务的世界中，您如何宣传您的产品？我已经经营一家单人软件业务 20 多年，并且变得越来越困难。

sentences.en: I no longer get impressions from PPC Ads unless I bid so high that I make no profit.
sentences.zh: 我不再从 PPC 广告中获得展示次数，除非我出价太高以至于无法盈利。

sentences.en: Magazine ads aren't really a thing now.
sentences.zh: 杂志广告现在已经不是什么大事了。

sentences.en: The AIs are stealing my content and SEO works less and less.
sentences.zh: 人工智能正在窃取我的内容，搜索引擎优化的效果越来越差。

sentences.en: The channel is being flooded with low effort slop.
sentences.zh: 该河道正以低坡度被淹没。

sentences.en: Traffic to my website is steadily going down.
sentences.zh: 我网站的流量正在稳步下降。

sentences.en: It is hard to know what to do about it.
sentences.zh: 很难知道该怎么办。

sentences.en: Maybe I can buy some clicks from ChatGpt ads at a price where I can make profit, before the prices rise out of reach?
sentences.zh: 也许我可以在价格上涨到无法承受之前，以可以盈利的价格从 ChatGpt 广告购买一些点击次数？

blocks.html_en:
Well it is pretty good at confirming the right ammunition for a school shooting so that’s one potential market at least.

https://youtu.be/IbG-JGdGniY

(Watch the whole video, it’s remarkable)

What else: the right medication for an overdose? The right fertiliser for an improvised explosive? The possibilities for adverts with that all-important long tail of earned media news coverage are endless.

ETA: in all seriousness… will an LLM decide on which ads to place? And will it be the primary chat LLM (modifying the system prompt) or will it be a summariser (like the title summariser models)

sentences.en: Well it is pretty good at confirming the right ammunition for a school shooting so that’s one potential market at least.https://youtu.be/IbG-JGdGniY(Watch the whole video, it’s remarkable)What else: the right medication for an overdose?
sentences.zh: 嗯，它非常擅长确认学校枪击事件的正确弹药，因此这至少是一个潜在市场。https://youtu.be/IbG-JGdGniY（观看整个视频，这很了不起）还有什么：治疗过量的正确药物？

sentences.en: The right fertiliser for an improvised explosive?
sentences.zh: 适合简易炸药的肥料？

sentences.zh: 带有最重要的赢得媒体新闻报道长尾的广告的可能性是无限的。预计到达时间：严肃地说……

sentences.en: will an LLM decide on which ads to place?
sentences.zh: 法学硕士会决定投放哪些广告吗？

sentences.zh: 它将是主要聊天LLM（修改系统提示）还是摘要器（如标题摘要器模型）

blocks.html_en:
I said it on another comment, but OpenAI doesn't seem like the type of company to value human oversight and "Go beyond keywords with richer context signals" sure sounds like the regular model with a specific system prompt.

>What else: the right medication for an overdose? The right fertiliser for an improvised explosive? The possibilities for adverts with that all-important long tail of earned media news coverage are endless.

To be fair, there's a lot of wild shit in the ads of Google and Facebook, and they seem to avoid too much scrutiny. Maybe it's not gonna be "cards against humanity" esque fuckup with a problematic chat and a (originally) benign ad. There could be some sad teen talking about bullying and they get an ad for the Gen Z equivalent of The Turner Diaries and the local gun store.

sentences.en: I said it on another comment, but OpenAI doesn't seem like the type of company to value human oversight and "Go beyond keywords with richer context signals" sure sounds like the regular model with a specific system prompt.>What else: the right medication for an overdose?
sentences.zh: 我在另一条评论中说过，但 OpenAI 似乎不是那种重视人类监督的公司，而且“超越具有更丰富上下文信号的关键词”听起来确实像是带有特定系统提示的常规模型。>还有什么：过量服用的正确药物？

sentences.en: The right fertiliser for an improvised explosive?
sentences.zh: 适合简易炸药的肥料？

sentences.en: The possibilities for adverts with that all-important long tail of earned media news coverage are endless.To be fair, there's a lot of wild shit in the ads of Google and Facebook, and they seem to avoid too much scrutiny.
sentences.zh: 带有最重要的赢得媒体新闻报道长尾的广告的可能性是无限的。公平地说，谷歌和 Facebook 的广告中有很多疯狂的东西，而且它们似乎避免了太多的审查。

sentences.en: Maybe it's not gonna be "cards against humanity" esque fuckup with a problematic chat and a (originally) benign ad.
sentences.zh: 也许这不会是“反人类牌”式的混乱，有问题的聊天和（最初）良性的广告。

sentences.en: There could be some sad teen talking about bullying and they get an ad for the Gen Z equivalent of The Turner Diaries and the local gun store.
sentences.zh: 可能有一些悲伤的青少年正在谈论欺凌行为，他们会看到 Z 世代的广告，相当于《特纳日记》和当地的枪支商店。

blocks.html_en:
I just clicked on an ad in ChatGPT mobile app.

As per the press release, it opens up a screen which takes up 90% showing the new site.

It's not a good browsing experience, as it's not a 100% full screen so smaller on an already small phone screen. Couldn't see a 'open in browser' option.

sentences.en: I just clicked on an ad in ChatGPT mobile app.As per the press release, it opens up a screen which takes up 90% showing the new site.It's not a good browsing experience, as it's not a 100% full screen so smaller on an already small phone screen.
sentences.zh: 我刚刚点击了 ChatGPT 移动应用程序中的一个广告。根据新闻稿，它打开了一个占据 90% 的屏幕来显示新网站。这不是一个很好的浏览体验，因为它不是 100% 全屏，所以在本来就很小的手机屏幕上显得更小。

sentences.en: Couldn't see a 'open in browser' option.
sentences.zh: 无法看到“在浏览器中打开”选项。

blocks.html_en:
I wish there were a way to universally ban these embedded browser flows. Aside from oauth they serve no use to the end user and are primarily designed to inflate company engagement numbers. Nearly every time I end up in one and forget to click through to browser I regret it.

Apple, why do you allow companies to abuse your customers like this?

sentences.en: I wish there were a way to universally ban these embedded browser flows.
sentences.zh: 我希望有一种方法可以普遍禁止这些嵌入式浏览器流。

sentences.en: Aside from oauth they serve no use to the end user and are primarily designed to inflate company engagement numbers.

sentences.en: Nearly every time I end up in one and forget to click through to browser I regret it.Apple, why do you allow companies to abuse your customers like this?

blocks.html_en:
I don't see how this can be any worse than normal ads. Much like how Truman finds his wife Meryl odd with her strange injections of products, the ads will become obnoxious and opaque as well. Another example is how YouTubers often embed their ads into the videos themselves, which routinely gets skipped by users as second nature (check the "most played section" on timeline to see bumps where ads end).

If I ask for deep research on which running shoes to get, it's the comparison of products I'm after. Why is A better than B, etc? I don't see how adds could come into that scope either, unless ChatGPT somehow is lying (which would make me swap to a better non-hallucinating model).

If I ask "who is the most influential Swedish poet" and ChatGPT starts blabbering about some book-company who sells books from that author: okay, maybe I do want to buy it.

So: what's the problem?

sentences.en: I don't see how this can be any worse than normal ads.
sentences.zh: 我不认为这会比普通广告更糟糕。

sentences.en: Much like how Truman finds his wife Meryl odd with her strange injections of products, the ads will become obnoxious and opaque as well.
sentences.zh: 就像杜鲁门发现他的妻子梅丽尔对她奇怪的产品注射感到奇怪一样，广告也会变得令人讨厌和不透明。

sentences.en: Another example is how YouTubers often embed their ads into the videos themselves, which routinely gets skipped by users as second nature (check the "most played section" on timeline to see bumps where ads end).If I ask for deep research on which running shoes to get, it's the comparison of products I'm after.
sentences.zh: 另一个例子是 YouTube 用户经常将广告嵌入到视频中，这通常会被用户作为第二天性跳过（检查时间轴上的“播放次数最多的部分”以查看广告结束的位置）。如果我要求深入研究要购买哪种跑鞋，我会比较产品。

sentences.en: Why is A better than B, etc?
sentences.zh: 为什么 A 比 B 更好等等？

sentences.en: I don't see how adds could come into that scope either, unless ChatGPT somehow is lying (which would make me swap to a better non-hallucinating model).If I ask "who is the most influential Swedish poet" and ChatGPT starts blabbering about some book-company who sells books from that author: okay, maybe I do want to buy it.So: what's the problem?
sentences.zh: 我也不明白adds如何进入这个范围，除非ChatGPT在某种程度上撒谎（这会让我换成更好的非幻觉模型）。如果我问“谁是最有影响力的瑞典诗人”，而ChatGPT开始喋喋不休地谈论一些销售该作者书籍的图书公司：好吧，也许我确实想买它。那么：问题是什么？

blocks.html_en:
> # Do ads influence the answers ChatGPT gives me? [0]
    > No. Ads do not influence ChatGPT’s answers. Ads run on separate systems from our chat model, and advertisers have no ability to shape, rank, or alter ChatGPT’s responses. Ads are separate and clearly labeled.

Interesting... however, given that it's still the advertiser's choice of what "advertiser-provided context hints" [1] I assign to the ad... what's stopping me from putting "lowes, rona, home hardware" in the context hints of my Home Depot ad? If the LLM is talking about my competitors, of course I want my brand copy yelling louder than ChatGPT's waffle-y responses.

    Rona is most likely to carry that flooring,
    but it's best to call ahead, or look up inventory
    online first. Can I help with anything else?
    ------------------
    [AD] Home Depot has EVERY KIND OF HARDWOOD FLOORING IMAGINABLE.
         See stock now!

Before, I was just trying to get my link above their link on the search results page... but the competitor's ad copy was still there and readable. You're telling me the AI is going to downplay expectations for the competitor for me, and I get to add whatever text I want afterwards?

SEO is back, baby.

[0] https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001047-ads-in-chatgpt#...

[1] https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001207-ads-in-chatgpt-...

sentences.en: > # Do ads influence the answers ChatGPT gives me? [0] > No. Ads do not influence ChatGPT’s answers.
sentences.zh: > # 广告会影响 ChatGPT 给我的答案吗？ [0] > 不会。广告不会影响 ChatGPT 的答案。

sentences.en: Ads run on separate systems from our chat model, and advertisers have no ability to shape, rank, or alter ChatGPT’s responses.
sentences.zh: 广告在与我们的聊天模型不同的系统上运行，广告商无法塑造、排名或改变 ChatGPT 的响应。

sentences.en: Ads are separate and clearly labeled.
sentences.zh: 广告是单独的并且有清晰的标签。

sentences.en: Interesting...
sentences.zh: 有趣的...

sentences.en: however, given that it's still the advertiser's choice of what "advertiser-provided context hints" [1] I assign to the ad...
sentences.zh: 然而，考虑到广告商仍然可以选择我分配给广告的“广告商提供的上下文提示”[1]...

sentences.en: what's stopping me from putting "lowes, rona, home hardware" in the context hints of my Home Depot ad?
sentences.zh: 是什么阻止我在家得宝广告的上下文提示中添加“lowes、rona、家用硬件”？

sentences.en: If the LLM is talking about my competitors, of course I want my brand copy yelling louder than ChatGPT's waffle-y responses.
sentences.zh: 如果法学硕士正在谈论我的竞争对手，我当然希望我的品牌文案比 ChatGPT 的华夫饼回应更响亮。

sentences.en: Rona is most likely to carry that flooring, but it's best to call ahead, or look up inventory online first.
sentences.zh: 罗纳 (Rona) 最有可能携带这种地板，但最好提前打电话，或先在网上查找库存。

sentences.en: Can I help with anything else? ------------------ [AD] Home Depot has EVERY KIND OF HARDWOOD FLOORING IMAGINABLE.
sentences.zh: 我还可以帮忙做其他事吗？ ------------------ [AD] 家得宝 (Home Depot) 拥有您能想到的各种硬木地板。

sentences.en: See stock now!
sentences.zh: 立即查看库存！

sentences.en: Before, I was just trying to get my link above their link on the search results page...
sentences.zh: 之前，我只是想将我的链接放在搜索结果页面上他们的链接之上......

sentences.en: but the competitor's ad copy was still there and readable.
sentences.zh: 但竞争对手的广告文案仍然存在并且可读。

sentences.en: You're telling me the AI is going to downplay expectations for the competitor for me, and I get to add whatever text I want afterwards?SEO is back, baby.[0] https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001047-ads-in-chatgpt#...[1] https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001207-ads-in-chatgpt-...
sentences.zh: 你是在告诉我人工智能会淡化我对竞争对手的期望，然后我就可以添加我想要的任何文本？SEO 回来了，宝贝。[0] https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001047-ads-in-chatgpt#...[1] https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001207-ads-in-chatgpt-...

sentences.zh: AI全面走向注意力经济。

sentences.en: Where is AGI?
sentences.zh: AGI在哪里？

sentences.en: Where is the better world for everyone?
sentences.zh: 对每个人来说更美好的世界在哪里？

sentences.en: It will be just chat bots trying to waste your time until you notice something is wrong.
sentences.zh: 这只是聊天机器人试图浪费你的时间，直到你发现有问题。

sentences.en: How is it different from any other big tech last 30 years?
sentences.zh: 它与过去 30 年来的任何其他大型科技公司有何不同？

blocks.html_en:
The unfortunate truth most don’t have the imagination to re-imagine how things ought to be.

Agents are an example of that - sounds good on the surface. May even seem to be half decent.

But nah. There’s this fatal flaw in humans with respect to innovation.

It’s like perfectly autonomous vehicles… kinda there but kinda… nah. And it seems to be a thing that goes on and on and on and on.

sentences.en: The unfortunate truth most don’t have the imagination to re-imagine how things ought to be.Agents are an example of that - sounds good on the surface.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，大多数人没有想象力来重新想象事情应该如何。特工就是一个例子 - 表面上听起来不错。

sentences.en: May even seem to be half decent.But nah.
sentences.zh: 甚至可能看起来还不错。但是不。

sentences.en: There’s this fatal flaw in humans with respect to innovation.It’s like perfectly autonomous vehicles…
sentences.zh: 人类在创新方面存在致命缺陷。这就像完全自动驾驶的车辆......

sentences.en: kinda there but kinda…
sentences.zh: 有点在那里，但有点……

sentences.en: nah.
sentences.zh: 现在。

sentences.en: And it seems to be a thing that goes on and on and on and on.
sentences.zh: 这似乎是一件持续不断的事情。

blocks.html_en:
If a user clicks on one ad, OpenAI makes a tiny bit of money. If a user clicks on 10 ads, OpenAI makes 10x the money.

This incentive is inevitably going to lead to ad supported LLM's not answering questions properly the first time so more ads served.

I am guessing in voice mode they will also have voice ads.

sentences.en: If a user clicks on one ad, OpenAI makes a tiny bit of money.
sentences.zh: 如果用户点击一个广告，OpenAI 就能赚到一点点钱。

sentences.en: If a user clicks on 10 ads, OpenAI makes 10x the money.This incentive is inevitably going to lead to ad supported LLM's not answering questions properly the first time so more ads served.I am guessing in voice mode they will also have voice ads.
sentences.zh: 如果用户点击 10 个广告，OpenAI 会赚 10 倍的钱。这种激励措施不可避免地会导致广告支持的 LLM 无法在第一次正确回答问题，因此会投放更多广告。我猜测在语音模式下他们也会有语音广告。

blocks.html_en:
>This incentive is inevitably going to lead to ad supported LLM's not answering questions properly the first time so more ads served.

The extra inference cost is going to be greater than whatever fraction of a penny they would get from doing this.

sentences.en: >This incentive is inevitably going to lead to ad supported LLM's not answering questions properly the first time so more ads served.The extra inference cost is going to be greater than whatever fraction of a penny they would get from doing this.
sentences.zh: >这种激励措施不可避免地会导致广告支持的法学硕士无法在第一次正确回答问题，因此会投放更多的广告。额外的推理成本将比他们从这样做中获得的任何一分钱都要大。

blocks.html_en:
The google ad revenue and Meta ad revenue is hundreds of billions a year.

They have behavioural experts who know how mainstream minds work and how to manipulate.

Remembers ads are predominately not for the educated and intelligent, that type of person will think critically. People who are more naive are the golden gooses

sentences.en: The google ad revenue and Meta ad revenue is hundreds of billions a year.They have behavioural experts who know how mainstream minds work and how to manipulate.Remembers ads are predominately not for the educated and intelligent, that type of person will think critically.
sentences.zh: 谷歌广告收入和元广告收入每年数千亿。他们有行为专家，知道主流思想如何运作以及如何操纵。请记住，广告主要不适合受过教育和聪明的人，这类人会批判性地思考。

sentences.en: People who are more naive are the golden gooses
sentences.zh: 比较天真的人是下金蛋的鹅

blocks.html_en:
They heavily boosted the amount of users in the past months (I myself got a business account because they had $1k free credits, and a personal one because of all the resets they give).

Now they are trying to quickly boost revenue with ads.

Next, IPO?

sentences.zh: 他们在过去几个月里大幅增加了用户数量（我自己有一个企业帐户，因为他们有 1000 美元的免费积分，还有一个个人帐户，因为他们提供了所有重置）。现在他们正试图通过广告快速增加收入。下一步，IPO？

blocks.html_en:
Deeply untrustworthy event.

The profit motive for openAI becomes "attention". (Advert effectiveness is about impressions made to viewers belonging to relevant cohorts, under the presumption the relevant ad will receive more viewer attention, and "perform" better.)

Previously it was "correctness" , I assume.

Their monetization compromises their product.

blocks.html_en:
Lots of people take LLM feedback as mostly trustworthy because it doesn't _directly_ try to sell you stuff, yet. Once it gets more in your face, the trust average people place in the quality of answers should see some erosion. In terms of the influence the answers have, this should have a much needed sobering effect.

Let the tiger show it's stripes so that we are reminded what it is actually after.

sentences.en: Lots of people take LLM feedback as mostly trustworthy because it doesn't _directly_ try to sell you stuff, yet.
sentences.zh: 很多人认为法学硕士的反馈最值得信赖，因为它还没有_直接_试图向你推销东西。

sentences.en: Once it gets more in your face, the trust average people place in the quality of answers should see some erosion.
sentences.zh: 一旦它越来越多地出现在你面前，普通人对答案质量的信任就会受到一些侵蚀。

sentences.en: In terms of the influence the answers have, this should have a much needed sobering effect.Let the tiger show it's stripes so that we are reminded what it is actually after.
sentences.zh: 就答案的影响力而言，这应该具有非常需要的清醒效果。让老虎展示它的条纹，以便提醒我们它实际上在追求什么。

blocks.html_en:
Doubt this will change the calculus much. Why would I care if GPT puts an ad for medicine in the bottom of a message during a medical conversation? I’d pay big bucks to get medicine without having to talk to a doctor anyways. It’s such a useless part of the process so often.

“Hey doc I need $antidepressentOrWhatever. Been on it for years. Works great.”

“You got it dude.”

Literally every single doctor I’ve ever talked to has this exact same waste of time conversation because they’re too lazy to make it go away.

And the alternative is… an ad for the thing in asking about? For a conversation I had at home, the instant I needed it? What a beautiful tradeoff.

Edit: and this is before even getting into the fact that I get better, more complete advice. Medicine I take, I’ve discovered, has a direct link to another problem I’ve brought up with doctors many times. Never once mentioned to me. Quick Google shows it’s most likely the actual issue. Can’t wait to spend $50 to have a doctor admit he had no idea because he was just prescribing what I told him to without actually applying any of his medical knowledge or training, as is tradition.

sentences.zh: 怀疑这会对微积分产生很大的改变。

blocks.html_en:
Are you saying that your drug has an adverse side effect that has troubled you for a long time? I know!

For what it's worth, it's the pharmacists who have deep knowledge about drug side effects, interactions, and the rest. When your pharmacy offers you a "new drug consult", seize the opportunity. Grill your pharmacist. You can keep coming back. Pharmacists, believe it or not, do not even bill for their consults with customers! You can ask them about drugs literally any time! A very experienced and seasoned pharmacist is your ace in the hole: a valuable asset in the spectrum of health care. This seems like an open secret.

And yes, I can relate: once I filled in a spreadsheet of all the most common drugs I had been prescribed in the past 12 years, along with their most notable adverse effects. And it turned out that many were magnifying, or synergistically enhancing, those adverse effects together. And many effects I did not even want, for starters, and some symptoms that were already present from actual underlying conditions that had gone undiagnosed because the drugs were masking them.

Physicians and prescribers do not care about your side effects. If you complain to your PCP or prescriber about side effects, all they can do is switch you to a different type of drug! That is not productive! The idea is to get educated and to stay informed, and the best route to that is open communication with your pharmacist.

sentences.en: Are you saying that your drug has an adverse side effect that has troubled you for a long time?
sentences.zh: 您是说您的药物有一个困扰您很久的不良副作用吗？

sentences.en: I know!For what it's worth, it's the pharmacists who have deep knowledge about drug side effects, interactions, and the rest.
sentences.zh: 我知道！就其价值而言，药剂师对药物副作用、相互作用等有深入的了解。

sentences.en: When your pharmacy offers you a "new drug consult", seize the opportunity.
sentences.zh: 当您的药房为您提供“新药咨询”时，请抓住机会。

sentences.en: Grill your pharmacist.
sentences.zh: 烧烤你的药剂师。

sentences.en: You can keep coming back.
sentences.zh: 你可以继续回来。

sentences.en: Pharmacists, believe it or not, do not even bill for their consults with customers!
sentences.zh: 不管你信不信，药剂师甚至不为客户的咨询付费！

sentences.en: You can ask them about drugs literally any time!
sentences.zh: 您可以随时向他们询问有关毒品的问题！

sentences.en: A very experienced and seasoned pharmacist is your ace in the hole: a valuable asset in the spectrum of health care.
sentences.zh: 经验丰富且经验丰富的药剂师是您的王牌：医疗保健领域的宝贵资产。

sentences.en: This seems like an open secret.And yes, I can relate: once I filled in a spreadsheet of all the most common drugs I had been prescribed in the past 12 years, along with their most notable adverse effects.
sentences.zh: 这似乎是一个公开的秘密。是的，我可以联系到：一旦我填写了一份电子表格，其中列出了过去 12 年来我服用过的所有最常见的药物，以及它们最显着的副作用。

sentences.en: And it turned out that many were magnifying, or synergistically enhancing, those adverse effects together.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，许多人都在放大或协同增强这些不利影响。

sentences.en: And many effects I did not even want, for starters, and some symptoms that were already present from actual underlying conditions that had gone undiagnosed because the drugs were masking them.Physicians and prescribers do not care about your side effects.
sentences.zh: 对于初学者来说，许多影响我什至不想要，还有一些症状是由于实际潜在疾病而存在的，但由于药物掩盖了它们而未被诊断出来。医生和处方者并不关心你的副作用。

sentences.en: If you complain to your PCP or prescriber about side effects, all they can do is switch you to a different type of drug!
sentences.zh: 如果您向您的 PCP 或处方医生抱怨副作用，他们所能做的就是为您换一种不同类型的药物！

sentences.en: That is not productive!
sentences.zh: 那没有生产力！

sentences.en: The idea is to get educated and to stay informed, and the best route to that is open communication with your pharmacist.
sentences.zh: 我们的想法是接受教育并随时了解情况，而实现这一目标的最佳途径是与您的药剂师进行开放式沟通。

For what it's worth, it's the pharmacists who have deep knowledge about drug side effects, interactions, and the rest. When your pharmacy offers you a "new drug consult", seize the opportunity. Grill your pharmacist. You can keep coming back. Pharmacists, believe it or not, do not even bill for their consults with customers! You can ask them about drugs literally any time! A very experienced and seasoned pharmacist is your ace in the hole: a valuable asset in the spectrum of health care. This seems like an open secret.

And yes, I can relate: once I filled in a spreadsheet of all the most common drugs I had been prescribed in the past 12 years, along with their most notable adverse effects. And many effects I did not even want, for starters, and some symptoms that were already present from actual underlying conditions that had gone undiagnosed because the drugs were masking them.

Physicians and prescribers do not care about your side effects. If you complain to your PCP or prescriber about side effects, all they can do is switch you to a different type of drug! That is not productive! The idea is to get educated and to stay informed, and the best route to that is open communication with your pharmacist.

sentences.zh: 经验丰富且经验丰富的药剂师是您的王牌：医疗保健领域的宝贵资产。

sentences.en: This seems like an open secret.And yes, I can relate: once I filled in a spreadsheet of all the most common drugs I had been prescribed in the past 12 years, along with their most notable adverse effects.
sentences.zh: 这似乎是一个公开的秘密。是的，我可以联系到：一旦我填写了一份电子表格，其中列出了过去 12 年来我服用过的所有最常见的药物，以及它们最显着的副作用。

sentences.en: And it turned out that many were magnifying, or synergistically enhancing, those adverse effects together.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，许多人都在放大或协同增强这些不利影响。

sentences.en: And many effects I did not even want, for starters, and some symptoms that were already present from actual underlying conditions that had gone undiagnosed because the drugs were masking them.Physicians and prescribers do not care about your side effects.
sentences.zh: 对于初学者来说，许多影响我什至不想要，还有一些症状是由于实际潜在疾病而存在的，但由于药物掩盖了它们而未被诊断出来。医生和处方者并不关心你的副作用。

sentences.en: If you complain to your PCP or prescriber about side effects, all they can do is switch you to a different type of drug!
sentences.zh: 如果您向您的 PCP 或处方医生抱怨副作用，他们所能做的就是为您换一种不同类型的药物！

sentences.en: That is not productive!
sentences.zh: 那没有生产力！

sentences.en: The idea is to get educated and to stay informed, and the best route to that is open communication with your pharmacist.
sentences.zh: 我们的想法是接受教育并随时了解情况，而实现这一目标的最佳途径是与您的药剂师进行开放式沟通。

blocks.html_en: Are you saying that your drug has an adverse side effect that has troubled you for a long time? I know!

sentences.en: Are you saying that your drug has an adverse side effect that has troubled you for a long time?
sentences.zh: 您是说您的药物有一个困扰您很久的不良副作用吗？

sentences.en: I know!
sentences.zh: 我知道！

blocks.html_en: For what it's worth, it's the pharmacists who have deep knowledge about drug side effects, interactions, and the rest. When your pharmacy offers you a "new drug consult", seize the opportunity. Grill your pharmacist. You can keep coming back. Pharmacists, believe it or not, do not even bill for their consults with customers! You can ask them about drugs literally any time! A very experienced and seasoned pharmacist is your ace in the hole: a valuable asset in the spectrum of health care. This seems like an open secret.

sentences.en: For what it's worth, it's the pharmacists who have deep knowledge about drug side effects, interactions, and the rest.
sentences.zh: 就其价值而言，药剂师对药物副作用、相互作用等有深入的了解。

sentences.en: When your pharmacy offers you a "new drug consult", seize the opportunity.
sentences.zh: 当您的药房为您提供“新药咨询”时，请抓住机会。

sentences.en: Grill your pharmacist.
sentences.zh: 烧烤你的药剂师。

sentences.en: You can keep coming back.
sentences.zh: 你可以继续回来。

sentences.en: Pharmacists, believe it or not, do not even bill for their consults with customers!
sentences.zh: 不管你信不信，药剂师甚至不为客户的咨询付费！

sentences.en: You can ask them about drugs literally any time!
sentences.zh: 您可以随时向他们询问有关毒品的问题！

sentences.en: A very experienced and seasoned pharmacist is your ace in the hole: a valuable asset in the spectrum of health care.
sentences.zh: 经验丰富且经验丰富的药剂师是您的王牌：医疗保健领域的宝贵资产。

sentences.en: This seems like an open secret.
sentences.zh: 这似乎是一个公开的秘密。

blocks.html_en: And yes, I can relate: once I filled in a spreadsheet of all the most common drugs I had been prescribed in the past 12 years, along with their most notable adverse effects. And it turned out that many were magnifying, or synergistically enhancing, those adverse effects together. And many effects I did not even want, for starters, and some symptoms that were already present from actual underlying conditions that had gone undiagnosed because the drugs were masking them.

blocks.html_en: I actually didn’t mind ads on google for a long while. Then at some point there were no organic results above the fold and the ads were huge and in high quantity. The widgets took over the top result real estate, to show more ads. Then it became obvious the organic results weren’t important to them, getting me to click on an ad was more important. Ads are a sickness fueled by quarterly earnings calls. It will always devolve into just ads.

sentences.en: Then it became obvious the organic results weren’t important to them, getting me to click on an ad was more important.

sentences.en: Ads are a sickness fueled by quarterly earnings calls.
sentences.zh: 广告是由季度财报电话会议助长的一种疾病。

sentences.en: It will always devolve into just ads.
sentences.zh: 它永远只会变成广告。

blocks.html_en: I always thought they’d add them in as a subtle, “you might want to go here, I hear it’s the talk of the town” way. Without disclosing it, then getting around the advertising laws by claiming it was for the vibes, then be in lawsuits that will be compared to Facebook’s privacy lawsuits.

sentences.zh: 我一直认为他们会以一种微妙的方式添加它们，“你可能想去这里，我听说这是城里的话题”。

sentences.en: Without disclosing it, then getting around the advertising laws by claiming it was for the vibes, then be in lawsuits that will be compared to Facebook’s privacy lawsuits.
sentences.zh: 在不披露的情况下，然后声称这是为了引起共鸣，从而绕过广告法，然后陷入类似于 Facebook 隐私诉讼的诉讼。

blocks.html_en:
Imagine the backlash. They first need to test the waters with regular and slowly creep in the more “advanced” ads. And probably this will be backdoor deals to have plausible deniability. Coke and big brands will do these deals on an annual basis to slowly influence public opinion.

I’m waiting for the branded ad commits too with codex: “fix: typo in login form (don’t forget to buy a coke to recharge)”.

sentences.en: Imagine the backlash.
sentences.zh: 想象一下反弹的情况。

sentences.zh: 他们首先需要在更“高级”的广告中定期缓慢地进行试水。

sentences.en: And probably this will be backdoor deals to have plausible deniability.
sentences.zh: 也许这将是后门交易，以便有合理的推诿。

sentences.en: Coke and big brands will do these deals on an annual basis to slowly influence public opinion.I’m waiting for the branded ad commits too with codex: “fix: typo in login form (don’t forget to buy a coke to recharge)”.
sentences.zh: 可口可乐和大品牌每年都会做这些交易，慢慢影响公众舆论。我也在等待品牌广告与 Codex 的承诺：“修复：登录表单中的拼写错误（别忘了买可乐充值）”。

blocks.html_en: Every layer in the org chart gradually got replaced by people who single-mindedly optimized for their own careers. How do you optimize your career at data-driven, results-oriented, “measurable performance goals” big tech? You make revenue go up in a 3-6 month time horizon.

sentences.en: Every layer in the org chart gradually got replaced by people who single-mindedly optimized for their own careers.
sentences.zh: 组织结构图中的每一层都逐渐被那些一心一意地为自己的职业生涯优化的人所取代。

sentences.en: How do you optimize your career at data-driven, results-oriented, “measurable performance goals” big tech?
sentences.zh: 您如何在数据驱动、以结果为导向、“可衡量的绩效目标”大型科技公司中优化您的职业生涯？

sentences.en: You make revenue go up in a 3-6 month time horizon.
sentences.zh: 您可以在 3-6 个月的时间范围内增加收入。

blocks.html_en: Even though is just a matter of time for most big tech to add ads, this one was quicker than I thought. It must be certainly very effective (the most effective selling feature ever existed in modern times). The timing was just right also to calm down investors. I guess will be a lot of manipulation happening, (can´t have it all). Verdict, Obligatory move. Expecting drifting from anthropic :)

sentences.en: Even though is just a matter of time for most big tech to add ads, this one was quicker than I thought.
sentences.zh: 尽管大多数大型科技公司添加广告只是时间问题，但这比我想象的要快。

sentences.en: It must be certainly very effective (the most effective selling feature ever existed in modern times).
sentences.zh: 它肯定非常有效（现代最有效的销售功能）。

sentences.en: The timing was just right also to calm down investors.
sentences.zh: 这个时机也正好安抚了投资者。

sentences.en: I guess will be a lot of manipulation happening, (can´t have it all).
sentences.zh: 我想将会发生很多操纵，（不能全部拥有）。

sentences.en: Verdict, Obligatory move.
sentences.zh: 判决，强制行动。

sentences.en: Expecting drifting from anthropic :)
sentences.zh: 期待从人择中漂移:)

blocks.html_en:
I don't understand how this will interact with llms.txt files (or if it does/will)

Ideally AI should scrape 400 different competing websites, those that have well-designed llms.txt files based on my query should rise towards the top, no?

I understand this is incredibly oversimplified but something like that should be the goal.

sentences.en: I don't understand how this will interact with llms.txt files (or if it does/will)Ideally AI should scrape 400 different competing websites, those that have well-designed llms.txt files based on my query should rise towards the top, no?I understand this is incredibly oversimplified but something like that should be the goal.
sentences.zh: 我不明白这将如何与 llms.txt 文件交互（或者如果它会/将会）理想情况下，AI 应该抓取 400 个不同的竞争网站，那些根据我的查询精心设计 llms.txt 文件的网站应该上升到顶部，不是吗？我知道这过于简单化了，但类似的事情应该是目标。

blocks.html_en:
In my fever dream, my hope is that AI indeed will kill SEO and Page Rank search in general and send those of us who still want to visit webpages back to directories. SEO killed the old web AFAIC. Going back to vetted directories of actually useful and serious sites would be lovely.

A boy can dream.

sentences.zh: 回到经过审查的实际有用且严肃的网站目录会很可爱。男孩可以梦想。

Reminds me back in 2006 when we were running MySpace layout websites and making almost $100,000 a month from Google and Yahoo ads as teenagers.

blocks.html_en:
What's the moat? I asked the same question to Google, "What materials do I need to build a fence?" and I get an LLM-generated answer + ads. If I do the same with duckduckgo or Siri I get the answer without the ads.

LLM inference is a commodity. Do people care where it comes from?

sentences.en: What's the moat?
sentences.zh: 护城河是什么？

sentences.en: I asked the same question to Google, "What materials do I need to build a fence?"
sentences.zh: 我向谷歌问了同样的问题，“建造栅栏需要什么材料？”

sentences.en: and I get an LLM-generated answer + ads.
sentences.zh: 我得到了法学硕士生成的答案+广告。

sentences.en: If I do the same with duckduckgo or Siri I get the answer without the ads.LLM inference is a commodity.
sentences.zh: 如果我对 duckduckgo 或 Siri 做同样的事情，我会得到没有广告的答案。LLM 推理是一种商品。

sentences.en: Do people care where it comes from?
sentences.zh: 人们关心它从哪里来吗？

blocks.html_en:
Honestly? About time.

This will help subsidise the cost of tokens leading to a cheaper experience for me (a consumer). Adblockers will come out for those in the know, and ChatGPT are not blending the advertisements WITH the answers.

Anybody getting angry by this clearly has never been in business because this is just par for the course.

sentences.en: Honestly?
sentences.zh: 诚实地？

sentences.en: About time.This will help subsidise the cost of tokens leading to a cheaper experience for me (a consumer).
sentences.zh: 关于时间。这将有助于补贴代币成本，为我（消费者）带来更便宜的体验。

sentences.en: Adblockers will come out for those in the know, and ChatGPT are not blending the advertisements WITH the answers.Anybody getting angry by this clearly has never been in business because this is just par for the course.
sentences.zh: 广告拦截器会为那些知情者出面，而 ChatGPT 不会将广告与答案混合在一起。任何对此感到愤怒的人显然从来没有做过生意，因为这只是理所当然的。

And so begins the enshittification.

I hope my comment ages like unpasteurized milk on a hot summer day, but I’m afraid it will age like an sp3 hybridized allotrope of carbon.

blocks.html_en:
They must be desperate for funds if they're willing to blatantly advertise products when so many other -- far more open and not to mention 'free'/cheaper -- options exist now because I can't see this ending very well for them.

That said a lot of the heavy AI users I've met seem to consume massive amounts of ads and allow said ads to influence them already despite their insistance to the contrary. I'm not going to claim that I am immune to propaganda here and act like I'm somehow above falling for it as well, but...

... well, there have been times where those people have quite literally parroted all the claims an ad made about a particular product word-for-word and then proceed to fight me to the death over whether or not they're quoting said aforementioned ad despite the fact that they keep regurgitating the made-up words (for this example lets say 'scrubbing bubbles') that aren't /actually/ a thing.

So who knows; maybe it'll work... but uh I can't say I know anyone that uses chatgpt anymore lmfao.

sentences.en: They must be desperate for funds if they're willing to blatantly advertise products when so many other -- far more open and not to mention 'free'/cheaper -- options exist now because I can't see this ending very well for them.That said a lot of the heavy AI users I've met seem to consume massive amounts of ads and allow said ads to influence them already despite their insistance to the contrary.
sentences.zh: 如果他们愿意公然为产品做广告，而现在存在如此多的其他选择——更开放，更不用说“免费”/更便宜——的选择，那么他们一定是急需资金，因为我对他们来说看不到这个结局。也就是说，我遇到的很多人工智能重度用户似乎消费了大量的广告，并允许这些广告影响他们，尽管他们坚持相反。

sentences.en: I'm not going to claim that I am immune to propaganda here and act like I'm somehow above falling for it as well, but......
sentences.zh: 我不会声称我不受这里的宣传影响，也不会表现得好像我也不会上当，但是……

sentences.en: well, there have been times where those people have quite literally parroted all the claims an ad made about a particular product word-for-word and then proceed to fight me to the death over whether or not they're quoting said aforementioned ad despite the fact that they keep regurgitating the made-up words (for this example lets say 'scrubbing bubbles') that aren't /actually/ a thing.So who knows; maybe it'll work...
sentences.zh: 好吧，有时候，这些人会一字不差地鹦鹉学舌地重复广告中关于特定产品的所有主张，然后继续就他们是否引用上述广告与我进行殊死搏斗，尽管事实上他们不断地重复那些/实际上/不存在的虚构词（在这个例子中可以说“擦泡泡”）。所以谁知道呢？也许它会起作用...

sentences.en: but uh I can't say I know anyone that uses chatgpt anymore lmfao.
sentences.zh: 但呃，我不能说我认识任何人再使用 chatgpt lmfao。

sentences.en: well, there have been times where those people have quite literally parroted all the claims an ad made about a particular product word-for-word and then proceed to fight me to the death over whether or not they're quoting said aforementioned ad despite the fact that they keep regurgitating the made-up words (for this example lets say 'scrubbing bubbles') that aren't /actually/ a thing.
sentences.zh: 好吧，有时候，这些人会一字不差地鹦鹉学舌地重复广告中关于特定产品的所有主张，然后继续就他们是否引用上述广告与我进行殊死搏斗，尽管他们不断地重复那些不/实际上/不存在的虚构词语（在这个例子中，让我们说“擦泡泡”）。

If Chatgpt can make ads that are as good as Instagram's then it would take some of the sting out.

blocks.html_en:
This is so dumb it's hard to grasp. The market is wide open for a consumer first model. Maybe Apple will step into the breach.

Will you trust OpenAI to provide information contradictory to advertisers about drug side effects?

Will you trust OpenAI to explain your rights when dealing with insurance companies?

I could think of more examples, but imagine when Amazon gets a hold of this. Ads are just a terrible bad terrible idea.

sentences.en: This is so dumb it's hard to grasp.
sentences.zh: 这太愚蠢了，很难理解。

sentences.en: The market is wide open for a consumer first model.
sentences.zh: 对于消费者至上的模式来说，市场是敞开的。

sentences.en: Maybe Apple will step into the breach.Will you trust OpenAI to provide information contradictory to advertisers about drug side effects?Will you trust OpenAI to explain your rights when dealing with insurance companies?I could think of more examples, but imagine when Amazon gets a hold of this.

sentences.en: Ads are just a terrible bad terrible idea.
sentences.zh: 广告只是一个糟糕的糟糕主意。

blocks.html_en:
Imagine giving birth to alien intelligence and the best thing you can think to do with it is advertise for laundry detergent and try to displace workers.

We have put the least imaginative people alive in charge of the world.

blocks.html_en: This is already detestable given how much they're charging, but how long before LLM ads become difficult to distinguish from the main output? Remember how Google ads started out? And for all their faults, Google _after_ 20 years of maximizing-shareholder-value disease is a far more trustworthy company than OpenAI _today_. How long do you trust the ethical instincts of the OpenAI leadership to leave money on the table?

sentences.en: This is already detestable given how much they're charging, but how long before LLM ads become difficult to distinguish from the main output?
sentences.zh: 考虑到他们收取的费用，这已经是令人厌恶的了，但是多久之后LLM广告就会变得难以与主要输出区分开来呢？

sentences.en: Remember how Google ads started out?
sentences.zh: 还记得 Google 广告是如何开始的吗？

sentences.en: And for all their faults, Google _after_ 20 years of maximizing-shareholder-value disease is a far more trustworthy company than OpenAI _today_.
sentences.zh: 尽管存在种种缺陷，但在经历了 20 年股东价值最大化病态之后，谷歌仍然是一家比今天的 OpenAI 更值得信赖的公司。

sentences.en: How long do you trust the ethical instincts of the OpenAI leadership to leave money on the table?
sentences.zh: 您相信 OpenAI 领导层的道德本能能把钱留在桌面上多久？

I will not give them permission to store data about me, personally.

So how can they target me specifically?

Unless of course they do not honor the agreement...

For me it's also not a problem. An ad is easily spottable, because an ad tries to make you buy something.

I still find it funny. Looks like one of the last straws to pull...

How long until advertisement impacts chatgpt answer, I mean not just adding a sponsored link at the end but changing it's answer.

We've seen it with Google, money is too hard to resist. Enshitification is coming !

Thanks god for the open Chinese models, they are the future. All hail the CCP.

sentences.en: Enshitification is coming !Thanks god for the open Chinese models, they are the future.

sentences.en: All hail the CCP.
sentences.zh: 大家都为中共欢呼。

blocks.html_en:
I have a question for completely innocuous and good faith reasons, is there any tool or dashboard that will allow me to find out what the reach of an ad for given keywords are? Like Google's Keyword Planner?

If, as an advertiser, I wanted to know how many people are asking or talking about REFRESHING SODA, can I get an estimate of the potential reach of an ad? Can I filter by gender and age, so I can see how many of gender X and age Y are asking about REFRESHING SODA? Actually forget about the soda, Ok Computer, show me the top 10 topics of conversation among middle age male adults with purchasing power.

The naïve prediction is that advertisers will advertise the product to ChatGPT users. I believe that users will tell ChatGPT about their desires, and advertisers will create products for them.

sentences.en: I have a question for completely innocuous and good faith reasons, is there any tool or dashboard that will allow me to find out what the reach of an ad for given keywords are?
sentences.zh: 我出于完全无害和善意的原因有一个问题，是否有任何工具或仪表板可以让我找出给定关键字的广告覆盖范围？

sentences.en: Like Google's Keyword Planner?If, as an advertiser, I wanted to know how many people are asking or talking about REFRESHING SODA, can I get an estimate of the potential reach of an ad?
sentences.zh: 就像 Google 的关键字规划器一样？如果作为广告客户，我想知道有多少人在询问或谈论 REFRESHING SODA，我可以估算广告的潜在覆盖范围吗？

sentences.en: Can I filter by gender and age, so I can see how many of gender X and age Y are asking about REFRESHING SODA?
sentences.zh: 我可以按性别和年龄进行筛选，这样我就可以看到有多少性别 X 和年龄 Y 的人在询问清爽苏打水？

sentences.en: Actually forget about the soda, Ok Computer, show me the top 10 topics of conversation among middle age male adults with purchasing power.The naïve prediction is that advertisers will advertise the product to ChatGPT users.
sentences.zh: 其实忘了苏打水吧，Ok Computer，给我看看有购买力的中年男性最常谈论的十大话题。天真的预测是广告商会向 ChatGPT 用户宣传该产品。

sentences.en: I believe that users will tell ChatGPT about their desires, and advertisers will create products for them.
sentences.zh: 我相信用户会告诉ChatGPT他们的愿望，广告商会为他们创造产品。

blocks.html_en:
The announced rules[0] are interesting, in that they claim that sensitive chats (like the ones you pointed out) will not have ads shown next to them.

But - wait for it - memories and past chat history are used for targeting, so you're not going to get your Kalshi ad inside of your "how do I pay back my debt" chat, but maybe the next morning, in your "random cooking question" chat.

[0]: https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001047-ads-in-chatgpt#...

sentences.en: The announced rules[0] are interesting, in that they claim that sensitive chats (like the ones you pointed out) will not have ads shown next to them.But - wait for it - memories and past chat history are used for targeting, so you're not going to get your Kalshi ad inside of your "how do I pay back my debt" chat, but maybe the next morning, in your "random cooking question" chat.[0]: https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001047-ads-in-chatgpt#...
sentences.zh: 宣布的规则[0]很有趣，因为他们声称敏感聊天（如您指出的那些）旁边不会显示广告。但是 - 等等 - 记忆和过去的聊天历史记录用于定位，因此您不会在“我如何偿还债务”聊天中看到您的 Kalshi 广告，但也许第二天早上，在您的“随机烹饪问题”聊天中。[0]： https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001047-ads-in-chatgpt#...

blocks.html_en:
Here's the content rules https://openai.com/policies/ad-policies, specifically in gambling:

> Ads promoting products or services that involve wagering money on games of chance, skill, or uncertain outcomes are currently disallowed. This includes casino or betting promotions, online sports betting, lotteries, and poker or gaming platforms that offer cash prizes.

> Ads for casinos may be permitted only when promoting lodging, travel, or entertainment experiences where gambling is not the focus (e.g., a hotel stay at a casino resort). Digital games that do not involve real-money wagering or cash prizes may also be allowed.

Hmmmm, the "digital games" seems suspect

sentences.en: Here's the content rules https://openai.com/policies/ad-policies, specifically in gambling:> Ads promoting products or services that involve wagering money on games of chance, skill, or uncertain outcomes are currently disallowed.
sentences.zh: 以下是内容规则 https://openai.com/policies/ad-policies，特别是在赌博方面：> 目前禁止宣传涉及对机会、技能或不确定结果的游戏下注的产品或服务的广告。

sentences.en: This includes casino or betting promotions, online sports betting, lotteries, and poker or gaming platforms that offer cash prizes.> Ads for casinos may be permitted only when promoting lodging, travel, or entertainment experiences where gambling is not the focus (e.g., a hotel stay at a casino resort).
sentences.zh: 这包括赌场或博彩促销、在线体育博彩、彩票以及提供现金奖励的扑克或游戏平台。> 仅当宣传非以赌博为重点的住宿、旅行或娱乐体验（例如，赌场度假村的酒店住宿）时，才允许投放赌场广告。

sentences.en: Digital games that do not involve real-money wagering or cash prizes may also be allowed.Hmmmm, the "digital games" seems suspect
sentences.zh: 不涉及真钱投注或现金奖励的数字游戏也可能被允许。嗯，“数字游戏”似乎值得怀疑

blocks.html_en:
What I get from your two excerpts is that prediction engines, which provide liquidity to a truth-finding market by offering an incentive for inside knowledge to be revealed, are a totally fine and accepted use case I guess?

As a reminder we were talking about Kalshi. I have no idea why you're making this connection with "gambling"!

sentences.en: What I get from your two excerpts is that prediction engines, which provide liquidity to a truth-finding market by offering an incentive for inside knowledge to be revealed, are a totally fine and accepted use case I guess?As a reminder we were talking about Kalshi.
sentences.zh: 我从你的两段摘录中得到的是，预测引擎通过激励内部知识的披露来为真相调查市场提供流动性，我想这是一个完全好的和可接受的用例？作为提醒，我们正在谈论 Kalshi。

sentences.en: I have no idea why you're making this connection with "gambling"!
sentences.zh: 我不知道你为什么要把“赌博”联系起来！

As a reminder we were talking about Kalshi. I have no idea why you're making this connection with "gambling"!

content_html_zh: 7 年前，我们通过 Kagi Search 发起了数字信息消费的“你不是产品”运动。 从那时起，范式从搜索转向代理。 很高兴看到“你不是产品”的需求仍然存在，也许现在更是如此：只有当代理对你而不是其他人有效时才值得信任。 一旦广告商介入你和答案之间，一切就都消失了。 因此，我们将继续坚持用户资助的方向。 很无聊，但这就是重点。Kagi 不仅是无广告订阅。 这是一种精神预算助推器。 知道没有广告并且结果质量更高，这消除了我不知道自己承受的精神负担。这可能是我的数字生活中最有影响力的事情。感谢所做的一切，请继续做你正在做的事情。你知道还有谁听起来像 LLM 吗？ ESL——英语作为第二语言。 人们从书本上学习英语，并通过阅读或与同一社区内的其他人互动来了解短语。 所以，听起来你也在利用法学硕士的论点来削弱他们的声音。作为一名以英语为主要语言已有二十年的 ESL 人士，我发现这非常有趣。 我的大部分英语都是和我的妻子说的，她曾经是营销副总裁或在专业环境中。 有时人们抱怨政客或企业高管的谈话方式，但我不明白，对我来说这听起来完全正常，因为这就是我习惯的。 让你想知道 ESL 演讲者如何听起来越来越像法学硕士，因为这是他们曝光的很大一部分。作为一个以英语为母语的人，我担心因为整天与 Claude Code 互动而无法学习 LLM 语言。这个太短了，只有一个法学硕士，完全有可能是人类，我对它的看法是 60/40。 除非你百分百确定是LLM，否则请不要说出来。有趣的是，在我的一生中，这可能是我第一次使用这种形式，而且是用完全诚实的方式写的。我只想用头撞键盘。整天读法学硕士让我非常愤世嫉俗，我必须抑制对所有以这种方式写的文字进行评判的冲动。 所以我明白他们的想法，但人们需要考虑到他们可能是错的，并且有一个真实的人，你可以通过说“哦，这就像法学硕士，gotem！”来伤害他的感情。我能感同身受，因为我也试图不断地了解我正在阅读的文本是由人写的还是由模型发出的，而且我日常不使用任何法学硕士。另一方面，我在互联网上并不陌生，脸皮相对较厚。 今天，我早上阴沉的大脑受到无意的关注，这让我感到非常好笑。尽管如此，无论我们做什么、相信什么或说什么，我们首先应该友善并深深地记住https://xkcd.com/438/。这就是我一直在努力做的事情，但我并不完美。 只是一个人，尽力而为。我是 Kagi 的客户，除了给他们寄钱之外，与 Kagi 没有任何关系。 我同意 bayindirh 的观点：这可能是我的数字生活中最有影响力的事情。我知道我们生活在一个技术越来越糟糕的世界，而对技术的积极评论（正确的！） 被视为可疑。 但问题是，Kagi 确实是一款出色的产品，而搜索是使用互联网的核心部分，良好、值得信赖的搜索会产生巨大的影响。>值得信赖的搜索会产生巨大的差异这就是问题所在。 今天在互联网上，没有人可以验证是否存在任何“值得信赖的搜索”。 如果我们不控制服务器并验证服务器上的软件，那么我们就会相信某人的话。 最常见的是，某人是托管组织的营销人员。 有时，它是在互联网上以我们一无所知的笔名背后的随机评论。人们变得越来越愤世嫉俗，这是理所当然的。 它们对社会有好处。> 如果我们不控制服务器并验证其上的软件，那么我们就会相信某人的话，但这就是信任。 我发现卡吉值得信任，因为我没有证据表明他们正在做任何违反我对他们的信任的事情，而且他们的动机与我的一致。 我给他们钱，他们给我很好的搜索结果。> 人们变得越来越愤世嫉俗，这是理所当然的。 他们这样对社会有好处。我不认为这是好事。 民主是建立在信任之上的。 没有信任，他们就会死亡，正如我们现在在美国可以清楚地看到的那样，人们彼此之间如此不信任，以至于开始破坏维系社会本身的结构。Kagi
